4100415.title: Push the iOS Moda mobile app with IBM MaaS360
4100415.description: Streamline the deployment of the iOS/iPadOS mobile app for your end users
4100415.body:
The App Push deployment for iOS allows to shorten the user on-boarding process by enabling auto-registration.

Requirements

  • IBM MaaS360

  • UEM Connect set up (instructions here)

On your IBM MaaS360 portal,

Go to APPS>Catalog

Select Add>iOS>iTunes App Store App

Under App Details, search for and select the Asavie Moda App

Under Configuration,

  1. Select in the App Config Source drop-down Key/Value

  2. Enter Attribute Name device_id

  3. Enter Attribute Value %csn%

  4. Click Update

Under Policies and Distribution, select Group or Specific user you want to distribute the app to.

Click Add

4100428.title: Push the Moda mobile app with VMware Workspace ONE UEM (AirWatch)
4100428.description: Streamline the deployment of the iOS/iPadOS mobile app for your end users
4100428.body:
The App Push deployment for iOS allows to shorten the user on-boarding process by enabling auto-registration.

Requirements

  • Workspace ONE UEM (AirWatch)

  • UEM Connect set up (instructions here)

On your Workspace ONE UEM (AirWatch) portal,

Go to APPS & BOOKS>Applications>Native

Under Public, select ADD APPLICATION

  1. Choose the Apple iOS Platform

  2. Search for and select the Asavie Moda App

  3. Click SAVE AND ASSIGN

Under Distribution, choose the appropriate Assignment Groups and delivery Method (we recommend Auto).

Under Application Configuration,

  1. Enable Send Configuration

  2. Select + ADD

  3. Configuration key is device_id

  4. Value Type is String

  5. Configuration Value is {DeviceUid}

  6. Click CREATE

4100479.title: Set up UEM Connect for VMware Workspace ONE UEM
4100479.description: How to setup this integration with VMware Workspace ONE UEM
4100479.body:
Enable the API Access

Under your VMware Workspace ONE UEM portal, select:

  • Settings>All Settings>System>Advanced>API>REST API.

Under the General tab, set:

  • Enable API Access toggle to ENABLED.

Under the Authentication tab, set:

  • Basic toggle to ENABLED.

Portal URL

To find your Portal URL, select:

  • Settings>All Settings>System>Advanced>API>REST API

Under the General tab,use the domain of the REST API URL.
For example, if the REST API URL is https://as1234.awmdm.com/API, copy and use only as1234.awmdm.com as your portal URL.


API Key

To find your API Key, select:

  • Settings>All Settings>System>Advanced>API>REST API.

Under the General tab, ➕ ADD a new Service with an Admin Account Type, name it, and then just copy the APIKey within it.

4100499.title: Set up UEM Connect for IBM MaaS360
4100499.description: How to setup this integration with IBM MaaS360
4100499.body:
To connect to with UEM Connect, you need the following credentials:

  1. MaaS360 Instance

  2. App ID

  3. Billing ID

  4. App Access Key

  5. Username

  6. Password.

If you do not know your credentials, you can contact IBM MaaS360 support by email here.

4107062.title: iPhone/iPad APN Profile Deployment
4107062.description: Get your Asavie Moda devices connected via APN
4107062.body:
The APN Profile deployment is a straightforward process. Features like device security, Wi-Fi protection, and app controls are not available with the APN Profile deployment.

How to get the APN Profile installed

  • Tap on the below link to download the profile to your computer

  • Send the profile via email to the device

The APN config file can also be downloaded from the QR code below by opening the device's camera app.

United Kingdom

  • Telefonica O2 UK (4G/5G) profile: Download here (mini guide for end-users here)

  • Virgin Media O2-1 UK (4G/5G) profile: Download here (mini guide for end-users here)

  • Vodafone UK profile: Download here (mini guide for end-users here)

  • EE UK (4G) profile: Download here (mini guide for end-users here)

Ireland

  • Three IE profile: Download here (mini guide for end-users here)

  • Vodafone VOA profile: Download here (mini guide for end-users here)

Once the configuration file has downloaded on the iOS/iPadOS device to install it, select:

  • Device

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • APN under DOWNLOADED PROFILE

  • Install and follow the steps.
    If a passcode is required, this is the device unlock screen code.

Once complete, your mobile device will get Connected to the portal.

Tip: If the device does not show a status of Connected:

  • Make sure the device is not connected to Wi-Fi.

  • Select the refresh icon in the top right-hand corner

4191268.title: How to create a Group Plan
4191268.description: Setup a user in a group
4191268.body:
Creating a group for your users couldn't be any easier. A group plan allows you to control what type of website access, connection speed, and data plan a single user or a group of users can access.

How to Create a Group Plan

Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Data Control tab, and select Group Plan. When on the Group Plan, locate the (➕) sign to create a new Group Plan. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Group Name - Give your group a name.

  • Devices Available - Move a user to the group

  • Policy - Use the default policy or create a new one

  • Add Limits - Choose the type of data plan that suits you.

  • Data Allowance Reset Time - Choose the reset plan that suits you

  • Data Speed - Set speed limit to group

  • Review Settings - Review the settings before saving it.

Click here to see how to create a Group for a user.

4194391.title: How to create a policy
4194391.description: Learn how to create custom website access for your users.
4194391.body:
Policies allow you to indicate what websites users can access. You can allow or deny website access by creating a policy that best suits a group.

How to create a policy

Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Data Contol tab, and select Polices.

When on the Policies page, locate the (➕) sign to create a new Policy. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Policy Name - Choose a name for the policy.

  • Network Security - This protects the device from phishing, network threats.

  • Search Categories - Seach for a website to see what category it falls under

  • Internet Access - Customize the list of website categories by Allowing or Denying

  • Device Access to Specific Website - Input a website to either whitelist or blacklist

Save Policy

See how to create a Group Plan for a user.

4206507.title: How to create a group for a device
4206507.description: Determine the users you want to be assigned to a group by creating a dedicated group.
4206507.body:
Creating a Group for a device is a fairly simple task, so long as you know the users assigned to a group. Admins with permission will be able to create, edit, and delete a group for a user.

How to create a group for a device

Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Devices tab, and select Groups.

When on the Groups page, locate the (➕) sign to create a new Group. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Group Name - Give your group a name.

  • Devices Available - Move a user to the group

  • Alerts - Set alerts for the user(s)

  • Access Times - Set internet access for the user(s)

  • Create Group - Save new group created

The new group created will also display in the Group Plans. You can manage internet access and restrictions for the group.

4207259.title: How to set a roaming Zone for users abroad
4207259.description: Defines places where a device can connect from.
4207259.body:
A zone allows you to define what country a user can connect from. By default, your account will have two default zone. These zones are "Domestic" and "Rest of the World."

  • Domestic - Operates on your mobile network operator.

  • Rest of the World - Connects to a country/network you have not defined a zone for, or that is not the domestic zone.

Creating a Zone allows you to configure a Group Plan that better suits a group on a domestic network versus when they are abroad. If you do not set a Group Plan, users entering that zone will have no mobile data. We recommend reviewing your Group Plans to ensure nobody will end up without a mobile data connection while traveling.

How to create a zone

Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Data Control tab, and select Zones. When on the Zone tab, locate the (➕) sign to create a new Zone. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Zone Name - Give your zone a name.

  • Add Countries and Carriers to Zone - Move a country of choice to the zone.

  • Selected Countries - Choose the desired network providers for the zone by selecting the edit icon or leaving as if no changes are required.

Create Zone

Note: The Domestic Zone can not be altered.

How to edit a zone

To edit a zone, navigate to Data Control, click on Zone. On the Zone page,

  • Displays all zones previously set up, including the Domestic and Rest of World default zones.

  • Select the zone you want to edit.

How to delete a zone

Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Data Control tab, and select Zone. When on the Zone tab, locate the Zone up for deletion. Tapping on the three dots icon will enable you to delete the zone.

Note: When you delete a zone, any Groups previously using the zone to access mobile data in countries with mobile carriers previously contained in the zone will now be controlled by the Rest of World Group Plan settings.

We recommend that you now review the existing suite of Group Plans.

4210187.title: How to make bulk edits using a CSV file
4210187.description: Learn how to make changes to your portal in bulk with CSV spreadsheets
4210187.body:
Need to add or make changes at once? No need to do it one at a time. Add or edit your portal in bulk using a CSV file!

The bulk edit feature allows you to save time editing the device descriptions, emails, and device types and move user(s) to a group, all using a CSV file.

How to make bulk edits using a CSV file

Navigate to Devices, click on Devices Overview. Click on the Bulk Edit Devices symbol located at the top right.

  • Click on the Download Devices List CSV file button.

  • Open the file to edit descriptions, email, group, device type, IMEI, and iOS Supervised status within the file. Please note Device type options: Android, iPad, iPhone, Windows, Other.

  • Go back to the portal, select your CSV, and click Upload File.

4214984.title: How to create customized admin alerts
4214984.description: Allow the administrator to receive important notifications
4214984.body:
Administrators can receive cardinal notifications, such as when a user or group has almost reached its usage threshold via email.

So, let's make some edits.
Navigate to the account icon located at the top far right of the page, click on Account. In the Account page, click Customize Alerts and make edits if required.

  • Alert Email - Enter nominated email address to receive alerts

  • Alert SMS - Customize the SMS message your end-user would receive

Choose from the list of examples available.

4241320.title: iOS APN Pin Protection
4241320.description: Helping you secure your users' mobile device
4241320.body:
This prevents users from removing the iOS Profile so they cannot bypass their company controls. iOS APN Protection is enabled by default.

Note: This feature is only supported on iOS 12 and below. APN profiles installed on iOS 13 and above will not be protected unless the device is in Supervised mode.

Navigate to Security, click on iOS APN Protection.

PIN Protection - When APN Protection is enabled users are required to enter a unique PIN to remove it

  • Under PIN Protection, check Enable: if not selected.

  • Click Save iOS APN Protection.

Generate Removal PIN - If APN Protection is enabled, users are required to enter a unique PIN to remove it.

  • Enter the device number in the field and select the Retrieve PIN button

  • Select Retrieve PIN, and the device-specific removal PIN will appear.

You can also download the full account list of iOS Removal PINs by selecting Download CSV.

4248772.title: Assign administrators to your portal
4248772.description: Ensures the right person operates efficiently with the right admin access
4248772.body:
Create the right administrator access to ensure the management of your portal operates efficiently.

There are four types of administrator access that offer varying levels of access to the portal. Understanding the precise capabilities of each is essential to maintaining security and ensuring that everyone can access the areas they need to do their job.

  • Account Administrator allows for full editing permissions over the whole system.

  • System Administrator allows for full editing permissions over the whole system.

  • Device Administrator allows for limited permission over the whole system.

  • Read-only Administrator allows for limited permission over the whole system.

Note: There is no limit to the number of administrators you can have on your account, however, a license is required for each new administrator you add.

How to create a new admin

Navigate to the account icon located at the top far right of the page, click on Administrator. On the Administrator page, click on the (+) symbol located at the top right. Select the best admin type for your administrators.

Account Administrator

When an account is created, the default administrator is the account administrator.

It can not be modified or terminated, as it acts as the master for all portal access.

The Account Administrator has the following full account permissions:

  • Configure a Group Plan

  • Create, edit and delete across all modules

  • Create, edit, and delete a System Administrator, Device Administrator, and Read-only Administrator; however, they cannot edit or remove themselves.

  • Reset a password

  • There can only be one Administrator

  • Resend an admin invitation email.

System Administrator

A system administrator has the same full access as an account administrator, with the exemption that the Administrator can be deleted.

There are the following permissions available;

  • Configure a Group Plan

  • Create, edit and delete across all modules

  • Create, edit, and delete a System Administrator, Device Administrator, and Read-only Administrator; however, they cannot edit or remove themselves.

  • Reset a password

  • Resend an admin invitation email.

Device Administrator

A Device Administrator has minimal portal access. It mainly focuses on device management.

There are the following permissions available;

  • Sending device invites and group device invites

  • Enable and disable devices.

  • Read-only access to the rest of the system.

Read-only Administrator

The portal access to a Read-only Administrator is very restricted, as they cannot make edits or delete themselves or any other administrator type.

There are the following permissions available;

  • View reports and download CSV data.

  • Read-only access to all other modules in the system.

Once selected, press Save to send the invitation. The nominated Administrator has 15 days to accept the invitation, as the invitation link will expire after 15 days.

4254978.title: Wi-Fi Protect
4254978.description: Secure your wireless network connection when on public Wi-Fi
4254978.body:
Public Wi-Fi is available just about everywhere, from the local coffee shop to hotels, malls, and airports. Connecting your device on public Wi-Fi often poses a security threat to your device, making them vulnerable to criminal hackers.

The Wi-Fi Protect feature allows you to extend your network security and compliance policies to Wi-Fi.

Note: Available for iOS 13+ and Android Samsung KNOX 2.0+ devices.

Let's enable and invite your smartphone users to join.

Enable Wi-Fi Protect

Navigate to the Security tab, select the following;

  • Wi-Fi Protect Card

  • Enable Wi-Fi Protect

Your iOS and Android users will be invited to join Wi-Fi Protect when they install the app.

Invite Users

Invitation is automatic once Wi-Fi Protect is enabled, but you can always control it per device if needs to be.

Navigate to Devices Overview. In the Devices Overview, select the following

  • Tap the individual iOS or Android (Samsung KNOX) device

  • Under Wi-Fi Usage, tap Send Invitation.

Next time the app is opened, users will be invited to join Wi-Fi Protect by setting up a VPN.

Wi-Fi Policy

Navigate to Group Plans, and extend the Group Plan you want to modify the assigned Policy.

Important: You need to make sure those top level domains are allowed by the all your policies:

  • asavie.com

  • accessmylan.com

Not allowing those domains will prevent the mobile app and the Wi-Fi VPN to work properly.

Protocol and Ports required by the VPN: UDP/ 500 + 4500

4270486.title: How to set up a K12 account
4270486.description: Here is our recommendation guide on how to set up a K12 School compliant account.
4270486.body:
As schools across the United States prepare for another long-term closure due to the coronavirus (COVID-19), many schools have shifted to online classes. Our CIPA compliant solution allows you to manage the internet content your students can access and help protect them from unsafe content.

Created an unlimited by Group Plans with a restrictive policy for your users.

Let's create a policy that is school compliant.

Navigate to Data Control, click on Policy. In the Policy page, click on the (+) symbol located at the top right.

  • Policy Name - Choose a name for the policy.

  • Network Security - This protects the device from phishing, network threats.

  • Search Categories - Seach for a website to see what category it falls under.

  • Internet Access - We advise you to deny everything apart from Content Servers, Education, Science & Technology.

  • Device Access to Specific Website - Input a website to either whitelist or blacklist

Create once completed.

Let's create a Group Plan that is school compliant.

Navigate to Data Control, click on Group Plan. In the Group Plan page, click on the (+) symbol located at the top right.

  • Group Name - Give your group a name

  • Devices Available - Move a user to the group (use the bulk edit to move numbers)

  • Policy - Use the school policy created

  • Add Limits - Choose the Unlimited Plan, and do not set a group alert

  • Data Allowance Reset Time - Choose the reset plan that suits you

  • Data Speed - Set speed to 4mb/s before 5GB, and 1mb after 5GB

  • Review Settings - Review the settings before saving it

Save.

4272778.title: How to delete a policy
4272778.description: Delete custom policies that are not in use
4272778.body:
Only users with the Admin or SysAdmin role can delete a policy from the account. The "Default" cannot be deleted.

How to delete a policy

Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Data Control tab, and select Polices. When on the Policies tab, locate the Policy you wish to delete. Click on the trash symbol to delete the Policy.

Note: When deleting a policy, ensure the Policy is not assigned to a Group Plan. If the policy is attached to a group plan, the Standard Policy will be used instead.

4272785.title: How to modify a Policy
4272785.description: Make changes to an existing policy, or to finish defining a new policy
4272785.body:
Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Data Control tab, and select Polices. When on the Policies tab, locate the Policy you wish to edit. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  1. Policy Name - Choose a name for the policy.

  2. Network Security - This protects the device from phishing, network threats, and other unknown websites.

  3. Search Categories - Search for a website to see what category it falls under.

  4. Internet Access - Customize the list of website categories by Allowing or Denying.

  5. Device Access to Specific Website - Input a website to either whitelist or blacklist. This will take precedence over any blocks at Network Security or Internet Access.

Note: Once changes are made to a policy, the new changes will take effect immediately

How to add a Policy to a group

Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Data Control tab, and select Group Plan. When on the Group Plan page, locate the arrow under "More" and click "Edit." Doing so will enable you to make changes

Save

4272805.title: How to view a device
4272805.description: Manage and maintain your devices to ensure the right users are connected.
4272805.body:
Devices Overview allows you to view the connection status of your devices. You can invite a user, rename a user allowing you to manage your devices effectively.

How to find a user

Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Devices tab, and select Devices Overview.

Once in the Device Overview tab, search for a device using the General Filter icon.

To easily search for a device, enter the last four digits of the device or enter the full device phone number.

Select the check box icon to perform actions if needed.

4276749.title: How to add or remove a device/user.
4276749.description: This article explains how to add or remove a user
4276749.body:
Only users with the Admin or SysAdmin role can create or delete a user from the account.

Note : An account license is required for creating a new user. Contact your account manager if more is needed.

How to add a user


Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Devices tab, and select Devices Overview. When on the Devices Overview tab, locate the (➕) sign to create a new user/device. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  1. Device Description - Enter the name of the user

  2. Country - Choose the appropriate country for the user.

  3. Carrier - Select the network carrier

  4. Group - Choose a group to assign the user.

The new user should appear on the device overview page.

How to remove a user

Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Devices tab, and select Devices Overview.

  1. Find the device you wish to remove.

  2. Click on the trash symbol located at the far right to delete the user.

  3. Delete the user.

Note : Deleting a user from the portal free's up a license. Contact your account manager if more is needed.

4331143.title: Wi-Fi Protect Removal
4331143.description: Learn how to remove the WiFi Protect feature from your device
4331143.body:
This comprehensive guide will show you how to remove Wi-Fi Protect from your device.

Please perform all the steps in the correct order. The removal of the Wi-Fi Protect feature is only available on iOS devices operating on iOS 12.2 and below.

If Wi-Fi Protect has been disabled, open the app to remove the VPN configuration on the device automatically. It is crucial to remove the VPN profile on the device before connecting to any Wi-Fi network.

How to disable Wi-Fi Protect

Navigate to Security and tap. In the Security tab, select the following;

  • Wi-Fi Protect Card

  • Disable Wi-Fi Protect

  • Select the checkbox to agree to the terms

and Save

How to remove Wi-Fi Protect from your device

  1. From your iOS device, click Settings.

  2. Tap General and then Profile.

  3. Select the VPN profile.

  4. Tap Delete Profile.

  5. Enter your device unlock password

  6. Tap Delete.

Note:

The VPN will be removed if steps are followed correctly.
You should be able to connect to a Wi-Fi network.

4331278.title: How to set up iOS Device Security
4331278.description: Set up security to avoid your device from being compromised
4331278.body:
Setting up iOS Device Security allows you to remotely lock, locate, and wipe your company device from your portal. The feature ensures that no sensitive data is lost if a staff member loses or misplaces their mobile device. Setting up iOS Security is mandated if this feature needs to be utilized.

Enabling this feature is only possible via Windows PC or Mac.

To Enable iOS Security.

Navigate to Security and tap. In the Security tab, select the following;

  1. Tap on iOS Device Security.

  2. Select Start

  3. Tap on Next Step

  4. Enter your Apple ID

  5. Tap on Download to receive the certificate

  6. Visit Apple's Push Certificate Portal to continue here

Apple's Push Certificate Portal

  1. Click Create a Certificate

  2. Click Accept

  3. Click Choose File

  4. Browse to the recently downloaded certificate file on your computer

  5. Click Upload

  6. Click Download once the Apple Push Certificate created.

Back to setting up iOS Device Security on the portal

  1. Upload Apple Certificate

  2. Select the type of password security level you want your device to have

  3. Tap Next Step to complete

Save iOS Security


iOS Security should is set up and ready to use. iOS Security will now show a Status of Enabled. It will also display the Apple ID used and the date your Apple Certificate Expires.

Note:

Devices wiped will need to be restored from an iCloud backup or set up as new.

Apple Certificates expire after one year. We will send a reminder email to you in advance to renew the certificate and ensure your devices remain secure.

4331885.title: How to renew certs for iOS Device Security
4331885.description: How to renew the device security feature
4331885.body:
It is essential to renew your Apple Push Certificate every year. This is the best way to ensure your device remains protected. This certificate establishes a trusted connection between us and your iOS device, allows us to send management instructions to these devices using the Apple Push Notification Service.

To Renew iOS Security

Navigate to Security and tap. In the Security tab, select the following;

  1. Tap on iOS Device Security.

  2. Renew iOS Security from the option menu

  3. Click Start

  4. Tap Download the .csr file

  5. Tap Next Step

Apple's Push Certificate Portal

  1. Visit Apple's Push Certificate Portal to continue here

  2. Log in with your Apple ID

  3. Click Renew

  4. Click Upload to upload the download .csr file. Wait until the Apple Push Certificate is successfully created.

  5. Click Download for your new Apple Certificate.

  6. Save the signed certificate (.pem file) to your computer.

Back to setting up iOS Device Security on the portal

  1. Select Choose File and upload the signed certificate (.pem file) to your account.

  2. Select Renew iOS Security

Save iOS Security

Once completed, this should be renewed and ready to use. iOS Security will now show a Status of Enabled. It will also display the Apple ID used and the date your Apple Certificate Expires.

4332331.title: How to set up Android Device Security
4332331.description: Set up security on your device to avoid being compromised
4332331.body:
We believe it is crucial to get your device secure, and having your device locked to a secure level is simple to do with Samsung Security. This feature allows you to remotely lock, locate, and wipe your company device from your portal. This ensures that no sensitive data is lost if a staff member loses or misplaces their mobile device.

Improve the privacy and security of an Android device in a few simple steps.

To Enable Android Security

Navigate to Security and tap. In the Security tab, select the following;

  1. Tap on Android Device Security.

  2. Select Start

  3. Choose the appropriate level of password security Medium or High

  4. Set a Screen Lock time for the device

  5. Choose what you would like wiped - Device Only or Device & SD Card

  6. Enable Android Device Security to save

Android Device Security is ready to use. Android Security will now show a Status of Enabled.

Note: Devices that are wiped will need to be restored from a backup or set up as new.

Enable Android Device Security on your device.

If existing Android devices that have joined the service before the setup of Android Device Security, you will be given the option to enroll these devices into Device Security.

Select Enroll Devices. The next time the user opens the app, they will be asked to install Device Security.

Note:

If you choose to Enroll Devices later, then the security settings defined here will not apply to the devices. These devices will show a Security Status of Disabled until they are invited to enroll.

4332333.title: How to set up App Control
4332333.description: Get visibility on the application installed on the devices
4332333.body:
App Control gives you visibility into the Apps installed on your managed Mobile Devices. Enable App Control to define Mandatory and Permitted Apps.

Note:

Device Security (iOS & Android) must be added to the device before App Control can take effect.

To Enable App Control

Navigate to Security and tap. In the Security tab, select the following;

  1. Tap on App Control

  2. Select Start

  3. Select from the two options Mandatory App List and Permitted App List

Mandatory App List

  1. Select Create Mandatory list.

  2. Enter the App name and click Find.

  3. Select Add (repeat for each app).

  4. Click Create Mandatory App List.

  5. Click Enable App Control.

Permitted App List

  1. Select Create Permitted list.

  2. Enter the App name and click Find.

  3. Select Add (repeat for each app).

  4. Click Create Permitted App List.

  5. Click Enable App Control.

Note:

Once enabled, the App Control card is updated. This will start reflecting the list user has enabled, the number and type (iOS or Android) of apps on each list.

To Delete App Control

Navigate to Security and tap. In the Security tab, select the following;

  1. Select the three-dot icon located in the App Control card

  2. Select to disable App Control

4338675.title: Add/Remove the feature using Verizon Enterprise
4338675.description: How to set up the service on your Verizon Enterprise "mybiz" Account
4338675.body:
To avail of the service we provide, admins must enable the service to a Verizon SIM. To do so, you would need to visit your Verizon Enterprise "mybiz" Portal; once logged in, you would need to complete the following steps.

Note:

Do not forget to create the device in your account first. If you are adding the service to iOS devices, see here.

Contact your Verizon representative if your Verizon customer portal is not accessible or request adding the feature in-bulk.

How to add the service

  1. Log into Verizon Enterprise.

  2. Select Manage Account

  3. Under Services, select Manage Wireless Numbers.

  4. Expand or Search By: Wireless Number for the mobile number you wish to edit.

  5. Under Features: Chargeable, click Edit.

  6. Scroll down and select IP Provisioning.

  7. Enable the action toggle for the Private Dynamic.

  8. Next to your wireless number, enable the action toggle, and select Continue.

  9. Select the available IP pool from the dropdown and click Reserve.

  10. Ensure today's date is selected and click Submit Order.

Note:

  • When adding the Private Dynamic IP feature, Verizon will queue out the APN Over-The-Air (OTA) for 72 hours to any OTA-capable device like Android devices or Jetpacks (iOS devices are not OTA compatible. See this page for iOS deployment).

  • If a device has not been switched on within 72 hours, it may require the Private Dynamic IP feature to be removed and re-added to queue out the APN OTA for an additional 72 hours.

  • Seventy-two hours APN queue is only applicable to devices that were already active on the Verizon network; new SIM activations are not limited to this time window.

  • If your device fails to connect after adding the Private Dynamic IP feature, please contact WEHD on 800-525-0481 for further assistance.

How to disable the service

Repeat steps 1-5 above.

  1. Scroll down to IP Provisioning and toggle from green (currently enabled) to grey (removed)

  2. Select Continue.

  3. Click Submit.

Note:

Ensure Make effective today is selected.

Removed Features will be displayed. If you are configuring iOS devices, you need to finalize the APN installation on each device.

4339369.title: iPhone/iPad Deployment
4339369.description: Type of iOS/iPadOS deployment options available
4339369.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect the policies created, data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

There are three types of deployment options available for setting up your device. Choose the best one best suited for your device type.

Deployment Options

There are deployment options available.

APN Profile Installation

App Installation

APN Profile Deployment via MDM

  • iOS for Microsoft Intune

  • iOS for IBM MaaS360

  • iOS for VMware Workspace ONE UEM (formerly AirWatch)

  • iOS for MobileIron Cloud

Click on each deployment method to find out more.

4347998.title: iPhone/iPad APN Profile Deployment
4347998.description: Get your Verizon devices connected via APN
4347998.body:
The APN Profile deployment is a straightforward process. Features like device security, Wi-Fi protection, and app controls are not available with the APN Profile deployment.

How to get the APN Profile installed

  • Tap on the below link to download the profile to your computer

  • Send the profile via email to the device or via SMS from your portal.

  • You can use the Message Devices to send an SMS, but remember that iPads cannot receive an SMS.

  • Verizon: eaaa.fxasavie.gw8.vzwentp

  • Verizon 2: eaaa.asavieii.vzwentp

  • Verizon 3: eaaa.asavietw.vzwentp

  • Verizon 4: eaaa.aone.vzwentp

  • Verizon 5: eaaa.asa.vzwentp

  • Verizon 6: eaaa.asiv.vzwentp

  • Verizon 7: eaaa.atwo.vzwentp

  • Verizon 8: eaaa.asv.vzwentp

  • Verizon 9: eaaa.asix.vzwentp

  • Verizon 10: eaaa.asfiveg.vzwentp (5G MPN - Not compatible with 4G CDMA devices)

Once the configuration file has downloaded on the iOS device to install it, select:

On the Device, open:

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • APN under DOWNLOADED PROFILE

  • Install and follow the steps.
    If a passcode is required, this is the device unlock screen code.

Mobile connectivity will be lost until you add the service via Verizon Portal; here is an article on adding the feature.

Once complete, your mobile device will get connected to the portal.
The DEVICE SETUP field should now show Controlled in Devices Overview.

Tip: If the device does not show a status of Controlled:

  • Make sure the device is not connected to Wi-Fi

  • Bounce in/out of airplane mode

  • Reboot the device

  • Select the refresh icon in the top right-hand corner

4348015.title: iPhone/iPad Mobile App Deployment
4348015.description: Get your Verizon devices connected via Mobile App
4348015.body:
Download the App to utilize the full features available on the service. The App supports all operating systems on iOS 13.2 and above.

There are two ways to install the App.

  • Invite device from the portal.

  • Install the App from the App Store.

Invite Device via Portal

  • Send an invite to the device via SMS – iPhone only. This feature is only available to iPhones.

  • Send an invite to the device – iPad or iPhone. This feature is suitable for non-SMS enabled devices like iPads. iPads must be invited via email to receive the mobile app activation code.

How to invite a user to join

Navigate to Devices, click on Devices Overview.

  1. Select the checkbox of the user you wish to invite

  2. Once selected, click on the icon Choose Action and select Send SMS or Email Invitation

  3. Select Apply and Send

Note:

iPads must be invited via email to receive the mobile app activation code. If the device is an iPad, the device's email address must be filled out in the device description field to receive the invitation link.

To download the App from the App Store.

  • Search for Moda and install.

Once installed, open the App and follow these steps in the order written;

  • In the Welcome screen, press Install.

  • Under Device Verification, enter the device phone number.

  • Under Code Verification, enter the Activation Code in the App. iPhones will receive an SMS with the activation code. iPads will receive an email invitation with the activation code.

  • The following are optional: Under Location Services, press Allow Location Services, choose Always Allow Under Push Notifications, press Allow Push Notifications, and choose Allow Under Wi-Fi Protect, press Install Wi-Fi Protect, and select Allow.

Add Service via Verizon Portal

The service must be added to the device from your enterprise portal on Verizon. Click here to find out how to add the service.

Open the App installed. Once opened, click on the following.

  1. Under the menu, tap Data Security, press Download Data Security and follow the steps to download the APN profile.

  2. Once the Profile has downloaded, close the App and navigate and select the following:

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • Under Profile, select the Moda APN, Install and follow the steps.
    If you are asked for a passcode, enter the device screen unlock code.

To Enable Device Security in the App

Under Device Security, press Download Device Security and follow the steps to download the MDM profile (optional).

Once the Profile has downloaded, close the App and navigate and select the following:

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • Under Profile, select the Moda MDM, Install and follow the steps.
    If you are asked for a passcode, enter the device screen unlock code.

Once complete, your mobile device will have connected to the service.

The device should now show Controlled in Devices Overview.

4350795.title: iPhone/iPad IBM MaaS360 Deployment
4350795.description: Get your iOS Verizon devices connected via IBM MaaS360
4350795.body:
Implement the right APN setup to your chosen iOS devices by defining the appropriate MDM Policy within your IBM MaaS360 portal. A separate policy is used for iOS devices. You can choose to modify existing UEM Policies or add new ones.

Once you have your policy defined, you can apply it to individuals or groups of devices.

How to create a UEM Policy

To add a new iOS UEM Policy from within your IBM MaaS360 Portal:

  • Select Security

  • Policies

  • Select Add Policy.

Enter the following settings:

  • Name: Descriptive name for the policy. This can be APN Policy.

  • Description: Optional description

  • Type: iOS MDM

  • Start From: An existing iOS MDM policy as a template for your new policy.

Select Continue.

Expand Advanced Settings and select Cellular.

Select Edit.

Select: Configure Advanced Settings.

Enter:

  • Name of the Access Point Carrier: select the appropriate APN name here

  • Authentication Type: CHAP

  • Access Point User Name: mdm

  • Access Point Password: mdm

  • Leave all other fields blank.

Save And Publish.

To Publish the policy enter:

  • Optional description to help with any future audits

  • password.

Select Continue.

Your new iOS MDM Policy can now be applied to individual or groups of devices.

4359416.title: Jetpack Deployment
4359416.description: Get your Verizon device connected Over The Air (OTA)
4359416.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect the policies created, data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

How to connect your device

Devices are connected Over The Air (OTA) seamlessly via the Verizon Enterprise Portal. This process requires zero user interaction, making it the preferred option.

  • Admins must ensure the device has been added to the MODA Portal before proceeding to the next step.

  • To add the APN Service to your Jet-pack device, you would need to visit your Verizon Enterprise "mybiz" Portal and complete a few steps. Click here to find out how.

See this list of VZW MPN certified devices and manual steps if OTA is not supported.

Note:

  • When adding the Private Dynamic IP feature, Verizon will queue out the APN Over-The-Air (OTA) for 72 hours to any OTA-capable device like Android devices or Jetpacks (iOS devices are not OTA compatible. See this page for iOS deployment).

  • If a device has not been switched on within 72 hours, it may require the Private Dynamic IP feature to be removed and re-added to queue out the APN OTA for an additional 72 hours.

  • Seventy-two hours APN queue is only applicable to devices that were already active on the Verizon network; new SIM activations are not limited to this time window.

  • If your device fails to connect after adding the Private Dynamic IP feature, please contact WEHD on 800-525-0481 for further assistance.

4360053.title: How to search for a website category
4360053.description: Learn more about the website category search feature
4360053.body:
When searching for a website, the search Website Category feature allows you to determine what category it falls under when trying to set a user policy.

How to search for a website category

Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Data Control tab, and select Polices. When on the Polices, select the policy that needs to be edited. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Enter the website name in the Search Categories field.

  • Select Search.

The result of the search will appear to the right. You can choose Allow or Deny the resulting categories and sub-categories from within the search results.

How to test a policy

It is possible to quickly check if a policy is allowing or denying a given website. To test this, select the following:

Locate the navigation bar at the top, select the Data Control tab, and select Polices. When on the Polices, select the policy that needs to be edited. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Locate the Policy you want to test.

  • Enter the website address in the Test Website field.

  • Select Test to find out if a website is being allowed or denied by the policy.

The result will tell you if the policy is allowed or denied by the policy and what categories or specific website exceptions allow or deny access.

4360082.title: USB Modem or Dongle Deployment
4360082.description: Get your Verizon devices connected Over The Air (OTA)
4360082.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect the policies created, data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

USB Modems or Dongles come with Connection Manager Software that enables you to configure the device's settings.

How to create a new APN

Using the Connection Manager Software, create a new APN profile for the device.

  1. Enter APN: select the appropriate APN name here.

  2. The software might require credentials, set both the username and password to data.

  3. If there is an option to apply the Authentication Type, select CHAP.
    Otherwise, leave all other fields empty.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab of your account.

4360618.title: Windows Phone Deployment
4360618.description: Get your Verizon devices connected Over The Air (OTA)
4360618.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect policies created and data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

How to create a new APN

Windows Phone 10

  • Swipe down from the top of the screen and tap All settings

  • Go to Settings

  • Tap: Network & Wireless

  • Cellular SIM

  • SIM Settings

  • Scroll down and tap Add Internet APN

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field, enter: select the appropriate APN name here.

  • If prompted, enter Username: data.

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: CHAP (select normal if the type is not listed).

Tap the back button to Save

  • Scroll down, tap the Profile name created and Apply.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled.

Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. This option depends on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

Windows 8.1

On Windows Phone 8.1, you need to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • Scroll down and tap: Settings

  • Select Mobile + Sim

  • SIM Settings

  • Manual Internet APN set to on

  • Edit Internet APN Settings.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field, enter: select the appropriate APN name here.

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: CHAP (select normal if CHAP is not listed).

Press Save.

The device will now appear as Connected. If it does not appear connected, re-enter APN details.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled. Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. This will depend on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

Windows Phone 8

On Windows Phone 8, you are required to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • On Start, flick left to App list.

  • Select Settings

  • Add Internet APN

  • APN.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field, enter: select the appropriate APN name here.

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: CHAP (select normal if CHAP is not listed).

Tap Save.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled. Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. This will depend on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

Windows Phone 7 & 7.5

On Windows Phone 7 (and 7.5), you need to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • On Start, flick left to App list.

  • Select Settings

  • Add Internet APN

  • APN.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field, enter: select the appropriate APN name here.

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: CHAP (select normal if CHAP is not listed).

Tap Done.


The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

4360856.title: iPhone/iPad Microsoft Intune Deployment
4360856.description: Get your Verizon iOS/iPadOS Verizon device connected via Microsoft Intune
4360856.body:
Implement the right APN setup applied to your chosen iOS devices by configuring a Microsoft Intune Device Profile.

Note:

Microsoft Intune does not support the configuration of the APN for Samsung Android devices. Please refer to your Microsoft Intune support contact for validation here.

How to create a new APN

1. First, download the appropriate iOS Configuration Profile:

  • Verizon: eaaa.fxasavie.gw8.vzwentp

  • Verizon 2: eaaa.asavieii.vzwentp

  • Verizon 3: eaaa.asavietw.vzwentp

  • Verizon 4: eaaa.aone.vzwentp

  • Verizon 5: eaaa.asa.vzwentp

  • Verizon 6: eaaa.asiv.vzwentp

  • Verizon 7: eaaa.atwo.vzwentp

  • Verizon 8: eaaa.asv.vzwentp

  • Verizon 9: eaaa.asix.vzwentp

  • Verizon 10: eaaa.asfiveg.vzwentp (5G MPN - Not compatible with 4G CDMA devices)

2. Create a Microsoft Intune iOS Device Profile. See How to create device profile in Microsoft Intune

  • Name: descriptive name for the profile such as APN Profile

  • Description: optional description

  • Platform: iOS/iPadOS

  • Profile type: Custom.

3. Under the Custom Configuration Profile Blade:

  • Custom configuration profile name: set as required

  • Configuration profile file: Import the iOS Configuration Profile downloaded in the first step. You will see the contents of the configuration profile displayed in File Contents.

4. When complete, return to the Create profile blade and select Create.

5. To deploy the configuration profile, you must assign them to the group(s) of devices that you want to apply this setting. See How to assign user and device profiles in Microsoft Intune.

  • Choose the Microsoft Intune iOS Device Profile you created in the second step and on the APN Profile Reports blade, select:

  • MANAGE

  • Assignments.

  • Choose: Include

  • Assign to: Groups you want to apply the profile.

Save.

6. Microsoft Intune will now instruct the affected devices to check in with the Intune service. When the device checks in next, the device profile will be applied, and the device should automatically be configured to use the Asavie Moda APN. The process typically takes less than five minutes.

See Troubleshooting device profiles in Microsoft Intune for additional information.

4361217.title: iPhone/iPad VMware Workspace ONE UEM Deployment
4361217.description: Get your iOS Verizon devices connected via VMware WSP1 UEM
4361217.body:
Implement the right APN setup to your chosen iOS/iPadOS devices by defining the appropriate Device Profile within your VMware Workspace ONE UEM portal.

A separate Device Profile is used for iOS/iPadOS devices. You can choose to modify existing Device Profiles or add new ones. Once you have your profile defined, you can apply it to individuals or groups of devices.

How to create a Profile

1. Navigate to:

  • Devices

  • Profiles & Resources

  • Profiles.

2. Select:

  • ADD

  • Add Profile.

3. Select Apple iOS and Device Profile

4. Configure the profile's General settings. These settings determine how the profile is deployed and who receives it and should be set according to your organization's needs. Some recommendations include:

  • Name: descriptive name for the policy such as APN Profile

  • Description: optional description

  • Deployment: either Managed or Manual according to your general management approach Profile

  • Scope: Production or Both if you wish to test the profile in staging

  • Assignment Type: Auto

  • Allow Removal: as per management approach. Suggest With Authorization Managed By: as per administrative approach

  • Assigned Groups: Set to the groups you wish to receive this profile. In this case, we assume that the devices that should receive the APN Profile setting are grouped via a standard or smart group

  • Exclusions: as per preferred deployment approach

  • Install only on devices inside selected areas: unchecked

  • Enable Scheduling and install only during selected periods: unchecked Removal Date: unset.

5. Choose a Cellular Payload and select Configure.

6. Under the APN list (leaving the Attach APN fields blank) configure the APN as follows:

  • APN: select the appropriate APN name here

  • User Name: mdm

  • Password: mdm

  • Authentication Type: CHAP

Leave all other areas empty.

Select Save & Publish.

VMware Workspace ONE UEM will now apply this profile to the identified devices at the next available opportunity.

Source: https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-Workspace-ONE-UEM/2011/iOS_Platform/GUID-AWT-PROFILECELLULAR.html

4362621.title: iOS MobileIron Cloud Configuration
4362621.description: Get your iOS Verizon device connected to the Platform via MobileIron Cloud
4362621.body:
Implement the right APN setup to your chosen iOS devices by defining the appropriate configuration and applying this to your iOS devices within your MobileIron Cloud portal.

How to create an iOS Profile

1. From within your MobileIron Cloud portal:

  • Select:Configurations

  • + Add.

2. Select Mobile, and enter the following

  • Name: descriptive name for the profile such as APN Profile

  • Description: optional description

  • Configured APN Types: Data APNs

  • APN Name : eaaa.fxasavie.gw8.vzwentp

  • APN Authentication Type: CHAP

  • User Name: mdm

  • Password: mdm

Leave all remaining fields blank.

3. Select Next →.

4. Check Enable this configuration.

5. Choose your preferred option:

  • All Devices to deploy the configuration to all devices

  • No Devices if the setting is to be implemented later

  • Custom to select specific device groups to which this configuration should be applied and choose the groups as required.

Select Done.

MobileIron Cloud will now distribute the configuration to the selected devices.

4366236.title: How to send Push or SMS messages to Devices
4366236.description: Send messages to your at&t users via the portal
4366236.body:
You can send customized instant messages to your users, allowing you to deliver critical information to them efficiently. There are two options available for this feature:

  • You can send a message that would be received by SMS, or

  • User can receive a Push Notification if they have the app installed

How to send a message

Navigate to Devices, click on Message Devices and select New Message.

1. Send messages to the following:

  • Group or Zone - Send messages to everyone in these categories

  • All Devices - Send a message to the user by finding their number

2. Select Message Type:

  • PUSH for devices which have the app installed

  • SMS for users with devices that are SMS enabled only

3. Type a subject in the Subject field.

4. Check the Mark as Important box in the Important message field to highlight and prevent the user from deleting the message.

5. Type your message in the Create Message field.

Click Send.

Note: There is a fixed monthly SMS limit that can be sent. Push notifications, however, are unlimited.

Message History

The messaging feature has a history section, that allows you to view the previous messages you have sent over your last two billing cycles. To view this, navigate to Devices, click on Message Devices, and select Message History.

4367096.title: How to send Push messages to Devices
4367096.description: Send messages to your users via the portal
4367096.body:

How to send a message

  • Group or Zone - Send messages to everyone in these categories

  • All Devices - Send a message to the user by finding their number

  • PUSH Notification for devices which have the app installed

2. Type a subject in the Subject field.

3. Check the Mark as Important box in the Important message field to highlight and prevent the user from deleting the message.

4. Type your message in the Create Message field.

Click Send.

Message History

The messaging feature has a history section, that allows you to view the previous messages you have sent over your last two billing cycles. To view this, navigate to Devices, click on Message Devices, and select Message History.

4372481.title: USB Modem or Dongle Deployment
4372481.description: Get your VDC device connected via APN
4372481.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect the policies created, data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

USB Modems or Dongles come with Connection Manager Software that enables you to configure the device's settings.

How to create a new APN

Using the Connection Manager Software, create a new APN profile for the device.

  1. Enter APN: asavie.net.

  2. If the software requires credentials, set both the username and password to the data.

  3. If there is an option to apply the Authentication Type, select PAP.
    Otherwise, leave all other fields empty.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab of your account.

4373380.title: How to modify a Group Plan
4373380.description: Make changes to an existing group plan
4373380.body:
Modifying a group plan allows you to change any setting about a group to better suit your needs.

How to modify a group plan

Navigate to Data Control, and click on Group Plans. On the Group Plan page, identify the group you wish to apply changes to. Click on the arrow under "More."

  • Plan Details - Make edits to the group plan by clicking on the "Edit" icon. From here, you can create or change the Policy. You can change the Data Allowance type and make adjustments to the Data Speed.

  • Alerts - Choose the type of notification you wish to receive. Admins will always receive alerts by email, while the devices can either receive notifications via SMS, Push, or Emails.

  • Access Times - Set internet access times for a user(s) on an hourly basis. You can customize what time of the day or what day of the week for internet access to the device.

Once happy with the changes created, simple press Save

How to enroll your device

From the home screen, tap the Menu button.

Once complete, your mobile device should now show Controlled when viewing the device in the Devices Overview tab.

Tip: If the device does not show a status of Controlled:

Ensure the device is not connected to Wi-Fi and Mobile Data is enabled.

4376713.title: MiFi/Hotspot Device Deployment
4376713.description: Get your AML or FirstNet device connected via APN
4376713.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect the policies created, data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

Here are some of the MiFi devices we support.

  • AT&T Velocity

  • AT&T Unite (AC770S)

  • Netgear 340U Modem

How to enroll your device

AT&T Velocity

1. Connect the hotspot to the computer using Wi-Fi.

2. Log in as Administrator to AT&T Wi-Fi Manager or 192.168.1.1.

3. Login: attadmin.

  • Click: Settings

  • Network

  • Connections

  • Domestic Data Off

  • APN.

4. From the drop-down list, enter:

  • Name: AML
    APN: vpn.accessmylan.net
    Username: data
    Password: data
    Authentication: PAP

5. Click Save.

6. Change from AT&T Broadband to AML.
7. Set Default.
8. Return to Step 3. and switch Domestic Data On


Once complete, your mobile device will have connected to the service.

The device should now show Controlled in Devices Overview.

AT&T Unite (AC770S)

1. Connect to the device using a Wi‐Fi connection from a laptop or PC.

2. Open an internet browser and enter http://attunite in the address bar.

3. On the AT&T Unite Manager homepage, log in as Administrator (default administrator sign in name is attadmin).

4. Select:

  • Settings on the navigation menu on the left side

  • Mobile Broadband tab

  • APN tab.

5. Insert the following information into the Mobile profile screen:

  • Name: AML
    APN: vpn.accessmylan.net
    Username: data
    Password: data
    Authentication: PAP

and click create.

6. To select the APN to use, choose Active beside the AML entry that you just added.

Once complete, your mobile device will have connected to the service.

The device should now show Controlled in Devices Overview.

Netgear 340U Modem

1. Connect the 340U modem to your PC.

2. Install and run the AT&T All Access communications software.

3. Select the Settings icon and open the Settings screen. At this point, the 340U has no connection to the AT&T network.

4. Select the Mobile Connection menu and press the Control + Shift + M keys simultaneously to expose the hidden Mobile Profiles button.

5. Insert the following information into the Mobile profile screen:

  • Name: AML
    APN: vpn.accessmylan.net
    Username: data
    Password: data
    Authentication: PAP

and click create.

6. To select the APN to use, choose Active beside the AML entry that you just added.

Note:

The AT&T All Access communications software application should install directly from the 340U modem when you connect the modem to a Windows PC.

Once complete, your mobile device will have connected to the service.

The device should now show Controlled in Devices Overview.

FirstNet Devices

Here's a list of FirstNet devices here.

Please note that these instructions are a guide to setting up the APN on your FirstNet device (Black FirstNet SIM).

Insert the following information:

  • Name: AMLFN
    APN: 18200
    Username: data
    Password: data
    Authentication: PAP

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Devices tab of your account.

4376745.title: Windows Phone Deployment
4376745.description: Get your AML or FirstNet device connected via APN
4376745.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect policies created and data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).
We support the following Window Phones:

  • Windows 10

  • Windows 8.1

  • Windows 8

  • Windows 7 & 7.5

How to create a new APN

Windows Phone 10

  • Swipe down from the top of the screen and tap All settings.

  • Go to Settings.

  • Tap: Network & Wireless

  • Cellular SIM

  • SIM Settings.

  • Scroll down and tap Add Internet APN.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: vpn.accessmylan.net.

  • If prompted, enter: Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: PAP (select normal if the type is not listed).

Tap the back button to Save

  • Scroll down, tap the Profile name created and Apply.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled.

Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. This option depends on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

Windows 8.1

On Windows Phone 8.1, you need to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • Scroll down and tap: Settings

  • Select Mobile + Sim

  • SIM Settings

  • Manual Internet APN set to on

  • Edit Internet APN Settings.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: vpn.accessmylan.net.

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: PAP (select normal if PAP is not listed).

Press Save.

The device will now appear as Connected. If it does not appear connected, re-enter APN details.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled. Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. This will depend on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

Windows Phone 8

On Windows Phone 8, you need to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • On Start, flick left to App list.

  • Select Settings

  • Add Internet APN

  • APN.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: vpn.accessmylan.net.

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: PAP (select normal if PAP is not listed).

Tap Save.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled. Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. Depend on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

Windows Phone 7 & 7.5

On Windows Phone 7 (and 7.5), you need to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • On Start, flick left to App list.

  • Select Settings

  • Add Internet APN

  • APN.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: vpn.accessmylan.net.

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: PAP (select normal if PAP is not listed).

Tap Done.


The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

4383590.title: iPhone/iPad Mobile App Deployment
4383590.description: Get your VDC device connected via Mobile App
4383590.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect the policies created, data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

The App supports all operating systems on iOS 13.2 and above.

There are two ways to install the App.

  • Invite device from the portal.

  • Install the App from the App Store.

Invite Device via Portal

  • Send an invite to the device via SMS – iPhone only. This feature is only available to iPhones.

  • Send an invite to the device – iPad or iPhone. This feature is suitable for non-SMS enabled devices like iPads. iPads must be invited via email to receive the mobile app activation code.

How to invite a user to join

Navigate to Devices, click on Devices Overview.

  1. Select the checkbox of the user you wish to invite

  2. Once selected, click on the icon Choose Action and select Send SMS or Email Invitation

  3. Select Apply and Send

Note:

iPads must be requested to join via email to receive the mobile app activation code. If the device is an iPad, the device's email address must be filled out in the device description field to receive the invitation link.

To download the App from the App Store.

  • Search for Vodafone Data Control and install.

Once installed, open the App and follow these steps in the order written;

  • In the Welcome screen, press Install.

  • Under Device Verification, enter the device phone number.

  • Under Code Verification, enter the Activation Code in the App. iPhones will receive an SMS with the activation code. iPads will receive an email invitation with the activation code.

  • The following are optional: Under Location Services, press Allow Location Services, choose Always Allow Under Push Notifications, press Allow Push Notifications, and choose Allow Under Wi-Fi Protect, press Install Wi-Fi Protect, and select Allow.

Open the App installed. Once opened, click on the following.

1. Under the menu, tap Data Security, press Download Data Security and follow the steps to download the APN profile.

2. Once the Profile has downloaded, close the App, and navigate and select the following:

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • Under Profile, select the Moda APN, Install and follow the steps.
    If you are asked for a passcode, enter the device screen unlock code.

To Enable Device Security in the App

Under Device Security, press Download Device Security and follow the steps to download the MDM profile (optional).

Once the Profile has downloaded, close the App and navigate and select the following:

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • Under Profile, select the Moda MDM, Install and follow the steps.
    If you are asked for a passcode, enter the device screen unlock code.

Once complete, your mobile device will have connected to the service.

The device should now show Controlled in Devices Overview.

4383767.title: iPhone/iPad APN Profile Deployment
4383767.description: Get your VDC device connected via APN
4383767.body:
The APN Profile deployment is a straightforward process. Features like device security, Wi-Fi protection, and app controls are not available with the APN Profile deployment.

How to get the APN Profile installed

  • Tap on the below link to download the profile to your computer

  • Send the profile via email to the device.

Vodafone Data Control: VodafoneAPN

Once the configuration file has downloaded on the iOS device to install it, select:

  • Device

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • APN under DOWNLOADED PROFILE

  • Install and follow the steps.
    If a passcode is required, this is the device unlock screen code.

Once complete, your mobile device will get connected to the portal.
The DEVICE SETUP field should now show Controlled in Devices Overview.

Tip: If the device does not show a status of Controlled:

  • Make sure the device is not connected to Wi-Fi.

  • Select the refresh icon in the top right-hand corner

4383798.title: iPhone/iPad APN Profile Deployment
4383798.description: Get your AML or FirstNet devices connected via APN
4383798.body:
The APN Profile deployment is a straightforward process. Features like device security, Wi-Fi protection, and app controls are not available with the APN Profile deployment.

How to get the APN Profile installed

  • Tap on the below link to download the profile to your computer

  • Send the profile via email to the device or via SMS from your portal.

  • You can use the Message Devices to send an SMS, but remember that iPads cannot receive an SMS.

The APN config file can also be downloaded from the QR code below by opening the device's camera app.

  • AccessMyLAN: AMLAPN

  • AccessMyLAN For FirstNet (Black FirstNet SIM): AMLFNAPN

Once the configuration file has downloaded on the iOS device to install it, select:

  • Device

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • APN under DOWNLOADED PROFILE

  • Install and follow the steps.
    If a passcode is required, this is the device unlock screen code.

Once complete, your mobile device will get connected to the portal.
The DEVICE SETUP field should now show Controlled in Devices Overview.

Tip: If the device does not show a status of Controlled:

  • Make sure the device is not connected to Wi-Fi.

  • Select the refresh icon in the top right-hand corner

4383850.title: iPhone/iPad Mobile App Deployment
4383850.description: Get your AML or FirstNet devices connected via Mobile App
4383850.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect the policies created, data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

The AccessMyLAN app supports all operating systems on iOS 13.2 and above.

There are two ways to install the App.

  • Invite device from the portal.

  • Install the App from the App Store.

Invite Device via Portal

  • Send an invite to the device via SMS – iPhone only. This feature is only available to iPhones.

  • Send an invite to the device via Email – iPad or iPhone. This feature is suitable for non-SMS enabled devices like iPads.

How to invite a user to join

Navigate to Devices, click on Devices Overview.

  1. Select the checkbox of the user you wish to invite

  2. Once selected, click on the icon Choose Action and select Send SMS or Email Invitation

  3. Select Apply and Send

Note:

iPads must be requested to join via email to receive the mobile app activation code. If the device is an iPad, the device's email address must be filled out in the device description field to receive the invitation link.

To download the App from the Apple App Store.

Once installed, open the App and follow these steps.

1. In the Welcome screen, press Install.

2. Under Device Verification:

  • Enter the device phone number.

3. Under Code Verification, enter the Activation Code in the App.

  • iPhones will receive an SMS with the activation code.

  • iPads will receive an email invitation with the activation code.

4. The following are optional:

  • Under Location Services, press Allow Location Services and choose Always Allow

  • Under Push Notifications, press Allow Push Notifications and choose Allow

  • Under Wi-Fi Protect, press Install Wi-Fi Protect and select Allow.

5. Under the menu, tap Data Security, press Download Data Security and follow the steps to download the APN profile.

6. Once the Profile has downloaded, close the App, and navigate and select the following:

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • Under Profile, select the AML APN, Install and follow the steps.
    If you are asked for a passcode, enter the device screen unlock code.

To Enable Device Security in the App

Under Device Security, press Download Device Security and follow the steps to download the MDM profile (optional).

Once the Profile has downloaded, close the App and navigate and select the following:

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • Under Profile, select the AML MDM Install, and follow the steps.
    If you are asked for a passcode, enter the device screen unlock code.

Once complete, your mobile device will have connected to the service.

The device should now show Controlled in Devices Overview.

4399454.title: How to adjust data connection speed?
4399454.description: Practical tips for improving your data connection
4399454.body:
Regulate the data speed connection of a device by making modifications to your group plan.

When creating a plan for a Group, you can set two types of Connection Speeds:

  • The first speed can be applied before the device has used its quota for the period.

  • The second can be applied to slow its connection once it has exceeded its quota, or can be used to slow down heavy internet services such as streaming video or audio.

How to modify a group plan

Navigate to Data Control, and click on Group Plans. In the Group Plan page, identify the group you wish to apply changes. Click on the arrow under "More."

  • Plan Details - Make edits to the group plan by clicking on the "Edit" icon. From here, you can make adjustments to the Data Speed.

The following information can be used as a guide to assist you in applying Connection Speeds for Restricted Mode:

  • Email can generally be run effectively at 200Kb/s (and as low as 100Kb/s)

  • Light web browsing can be effective at around 400Kb/s

  • Streaming audio can be attained at 100Kb/s (you can obtain better quality up to 300Kb/s)

  • Streaming video requires at least 2Mb/s to run effectively without affecting the user experience.

Example restrictions: Apply rules that will allow access to email and light web browsing, but will deter the user from accessing streaming video on their mobile device; consider setting the Restricted Connection Speed to 400Kb/s.

If you want to create a scenario that devices only have access to email and no other web services, you could consider setting the Connection Speed to 200Kb/s.

Note:

You can restrict access to specific websites and Internet services both before and after a Device Quota is reached. For more, see ‘ Create a Policy ‘ and ‘ Configure a Group Plan.’

4399522.title: How to Understand Device Setup
4399522.body:
Once the deployment process has begun, you need to be aware of two main pieces of information. These are the connection status of your device(s) in the portal. It is essential to know the device connection status to get the best out of the service.

Here is the status you should know about your devices:

  • Uncontrolled: These are devices that have never connected via mobile to the service. Uncontrolled Devices include devices not constructed successfully and devices that are correctly configured but could not get a successful session for other reasons (e.g., outside of allowed hours, no quota for the zone, etc.)

  • Controlled: These devices have at least been connected once to the internet via mobile to the portal.

4418740.body:

FirstNet

Non-FirstNet SIMs are orange (see image below). They can only connect to the service with the Standard AccessMyLAN APN settings.

Non-FirstNet SIMS

See our device configuration guides for iOS, Android, Samsung, MiFi, USB modem and dongles, Windows Tablet, Windows Phone, or Blackberry for more information.

4428145.body:
