and Apply

4441641.title: What is the Rest of the World Zone?
4441641.body:
The Rest of the World Zone is described as countries and mobile carriers outside of your domestic network carrier. This zone controls the mobile data allowances and restrictions of all devices not in the domestic or other zones you have created.


Here is an example of how it works:

If you set up a new zone for Canada, the World Zone's Rest no longer covers Canada. It merely acts as a catch-all’ for Groups accessing mobile data in countries (and on mobile carriers) that you have not created zones for.

By default, every group has no Group Plan set up for the Rest of the World zone. If you do not set a Group Plan, users entering that zone will have no mobile data.

We recommend reviewing your Group Plans to make sure nobody will end up without mobile data connection while traveling.

To reduce mobile data costs for users when roaming overseas, we highly recommend that you edit the Group Plans for all Groups when operating in the Rest of the World  zone by configuring a Group Plan for each Group in the Zone.

A typical option would be to reduce the connection speed for each group in the Rest of the World zone via the Group Plan. This would slow the space and ultimately reduce overall mobile data usage while roaming.

We also advise that you consider reviewing your Policies in each Rest of World Group Plan for each group. Denying access to heavy data Internet services such as streaming video while devices are overseas will also dramatically reduce your roaming data costs for your mobile phones.

The Rest of the World Zone cannot be edited.

4442268.title: What is IMEI Lock, and the benefits of it?
4442268.body:
In English, IMEI is called International Mobile Equipment Identity. Each device has a unique number, which is typically found behind the battery.

The IMEI acts like a fingerprint to help you identify. When you enable IMEI Lock, it ensures that the SIM will only work with the device supplied to the end-user.

For example, IMEI Lock works in a similar way to a company fuel card supplied. A business fuel card is often locked to its vehicle's registration, preventing the fuel cards from being used on any other vehicle. This may prevent a loss of revenue for the business.

IMEI Lock pairs the device and SIM so that the SIM will not operate with any other device, which prevents misuse of data and revenue.

4442377.title: What are Failed Login Reports?
4442377.body:
The Report section of the portal helps you analyze the data and content usage for a user. Here are some of the following activities for a user:

  • Time: The time of the failed login in your local time.

  • Device Number: The number of the device which experienced the failed login.

  • Device Description: The device name (If the device has not been changed, the device number will appear as the device description)

  • Cause: The cause of the failed login.

  • Zone: The zone that the device was in at the time of the failed login.

  • Network: The network the device is connected to at the time of the failed login

4442465.title: How to set a reset start time for your Data Allowance?
4442465.body:
When a new group is created, the option to set a reset start time becomes available. The restart date is aligned with your network carrier's monthly billing date.

When creating the data allowance, you set a monthly data allowance for devices in that group plan.

The data allowance for each device will be re-initiated on each billing date.

When Configuring a Group Plan, you have the option of choosing when you want the reset time of your device to be. This can either be monthly or daily.

A daily reset time means your quota will be refreshed every day. This can be handy for users who share tablets or education devices.

Important:

You can only select monthly or daily data allowance reset time when you are:

  • Creating a new Group Plan or

  • Creating a roaming plan to a specific zone.

You cannot edit the data allowance reset time once it has been configured.

To change a data allowance reset time affecting your devices, you must move to a newly created Group Plan.
If you require assistance with this, contact our Support Team.

4442573.title: Can you Check Location History?
4442573.body: The location history for a device is not recorded. The location feature only stores the last know position for devices registered with the iOS or Android app, that have Allowed Location Services.

4442607.title: How to Understand Data Allowances when Moving Devices between Groups?
4442607.body:
When you move a device to a new group, the device has access to the data quota of the new group.

However, this new quota is in addition to any data the device has consumed previously within the month you move them.

For this reason, we recommend that, where possible, you move devices at the beginning of a Cycle Period. Otherwise, you could be providing a significant data allowance to an individual device and risk acquiring huge data costs due to it using Data Quotas from two Groups in the period.

An example of this is as follow:

  • Mark’s iPhone 11 is a member of the Marketing Group and has used 2GB of its 5GB Device Quota allowance this month.

  • The administrator moves the device to the Management Group, which has a 10GB Device Quota per month.

The result is that Mark’s iPhone can now consume 12GB during the month before being restricted or cut off (2GB from its time in the Marketing Group and up to 10GB allowance within the Management Group).


Notes:

If your group has a daily data allowance limit (as opposed to the default monthly limit), this event is limited to just a 24 hours time frame. This should be less of a concern.

Reports will show the data used by the device in both groups during the month in which they were moved.

4442611.title: How to Understand Data Usage Reports when Moving Devices between Groups?
4442611.body:
Reporting shows the amount of data used in the chosen date range for all groups. If a device moves to a new group during the reporting period, reports will show the combined data usage from the current and previous groups.

An example of this is as follows :

  • Mark’s iPhone 11 is a member of the Marketing Group and has spent 2GB of data so far this month.

  • The administrator moves Marks device to the Management Group, which allocates 10GB of data to each device.

  • After moving Marks iPhone, the device data usage will be 0GB for the Management Group, and the allocated data will be 10GB

  • When the Administrator runs a report for Marks phone, it will display 2GB of usage, which combines the data usage across all groups.

Note:

Filters now provide the ability to determine the data usage for each group the device was added.

Reports show the total amount of data used; however, the data allowance is the amount of data allocated to the device in its current group.


See Understanding data Allowances when Moving Devices Between Groups for more information.

4442631.title: How real-time are the Reports?
4442631.body:
The report information, such as the data usage for devices in groups and the zones sections, are updated in real-time after each mobile device session ends.


A mobile session can last up to 8 hours. You can force a device to end a mobile session by enabling Airplane mode or by turn off/on the device.


The rest of the Data Usage, like categories and website sections and the Blocked Content report, will take on average 15 minutes to refresh, although it can go up to an hour.

4442632.title: How to add new devices to my account
4442632.body: Contacting your Mobile Provider Account Manager is the easiest way to add a new device/user to your account. They will be able to assist with adding the new user to your account.

4442695.title: How long will it take for my Changes to Apply?
4442695.body:
Changes made to a Policy, such as allowing or denying categories or specific websites, are instantaneous.


Some devices can cache the DNS response for a short period. Most modern OS device policy changes apply without delays.

Quota enforcement adjustments such as Device Cut-off are also instantaneous.

4442696.title: What is iOS Supervised Mode?
4442696.body:
Supervised mode is a feature introduced by Apple that allows IT to have more power with managing company iOS devices. An administrator can apply additional restrictions like turning off AirDrop or preventing access to the App Store with supervision.

It also provides additional device configurations and features, like silently updating apps or filtering web usage.

If your iOS devices are in Supervised Mode, your devices can be activated in Devices Overview as Supervised using the Bulk Edit Devices feature.

This will allow you to benefit from the iOS APN Protection feature for devices running iOS 13 and above.

By default, your iPhones or iPads are not in supervised mode. Supervised Mode can only be set active when you set up a new device. If your iPhone or iPad has been activated already, you would need to completely erase it and set it as a new device to set up supervised mode.

More detail about iOS Supervised Mode on Apple’s website here.

4443747.title: What is a Domain Name?
4443747.body:
Domain Names are a system of assigning meaningful names to Internet web servers. Devices require IP addresses to communicate with each other.

A Domain Name Server (DNS) is a database that links meaningful names such as http://www.bbc.com to a specific IP address such as 212.58.246.92.

Each DNS contains a directory of domain names (website addresses) and translates them to Internet Protocol (IP) addresses. When you are browsing a website, you are browsing pages on an IP address. The domain name (www.website.com) is simply a reference.

  1. When users browse to a website, a request is sent to the DNS server.

  2. The DNS server looks up a database and sends back the IP address of the website.

  3. The browser then displays the web page for that IP address.

This is important to understand when managing devices.

In Policies, when you deny access to a website, you are doing so via its Domain Name (www.website.com). Users who have accessed the website before adding this restriction may still be able to access the website for a while because the IP address of the website can be cached by the device they are using.

The Policy is blocking the domain name (www.website.com), whereas the device is still using the IP address.

It is important to note that if one of the user devices has accessed the website before Restricted Mode, there is a chance that they will still be able to access it after the restriction for a limited period.

Important:

It is for this reason that setting both a restriction zone Policy and limiting the Connection Speed of devices in a group is essential.

Choosing to block ‘YouTube’ once a Group Zone quota is met can never be 100% accurate, due to how Domain Name Servers Work. Reducing Connection Speed will result in languid streaming video speed, so users are less likely to consume any significant volume of the video.

Not all web services and applications rely on DNS (Skype, for example, is not reliant on DNS) and so using the Connection Speed is always advised. The combination of both speeds and policies will help you to manage your mobile data costs successfully.

4443748.title: How to locate my device?
4443748.body:
One of the benefits of having the app installed on your device is that it has a "locate my phone" tracking feature. The feature may be a suitable solution if you cannot find the device and wish to track it down. The last known location of the device will be recorded on a map.

This feature can only be implemented with the app installed on the mobile device. Once enabled, the location of the device can be observed from the Dashboard.

You can view and make the following actions from the dashboard tab:

  • View the last know location of the device from the map. This records the time, date, and location.

  • Zoom in and out on the map to get more context on the device's surroundings.

  • Send messages to the device from the Dashboard to the device.

The refresh icon will show the updated information on the Dashboard.

Note: The location history is not stored. The location feature only records the last know position for devices registered with the iOS or Android app, with Location Services enabled set to "Always Allow".

How to troubleshoot the location device feature?

To troubleshoot your device location, ensure the mobile device has the following:

  • Confirm the device has a mobile data connection.

  • Ensure the location services are enabled on the app, set to "Always Allow".

  • Check Device Security has been enabled on the device.

  • Ensure the app is installed on the device.

Should the issue not be resolved after following the steps outlined above, please contact our support team for further assistance.

4459419.title: How to make adjustments to a Group Data Allowance?
4459419.body:
When a user consumes its assigned data allows and you wish you increase it, here is how to do:

  • Create a new Group Plan for the user.

Note:

When you move a device to a new group, the device has access to the data quota of the new group. However, this new quota is in addition to any data the device has consumed previously within the month you move them.

Click here for more acumen on what happens when you adjust the data allowance usage of a user.

4464370.title: What is Tethering?
4464370.body:
Tethering refers to a mobile device sharing its Internet connection with other devices such as tablets, laptops, and Wi-Fi-enabled devices.

The devices can be connected via Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, or by a physical cable. Tethering over Wi-Fi and Bluetooth is available on iOS (‘Personal Hotspot’), Windows Phone, and some Android devices (this will vary depending on manufacturer and software version).

IMPORTANT:

Unless your mobile device allows you to configure an APN specifically for tethering, we will NOT be able to control data used via mobile tethering.

4489002.title: How to manage your devices
4489002.description: Make bulk actions in your portal
4489002.body:
You can invite your devices to be configured from your portal dashboard. There are a couple of bulk action features on the portal, which can allow you easily make bulk actions to your devices. Administrators can respond and resolve issues after a closer inspection.

With bulk actions, you to perform the following actions on many devices simultaneously:

  • Send SMS invitation

  • Send Email invitation

  • Enable device

  • Disable device

  • Deregister app

Send SMS Invitation

Sending SMS invitations is a vital action on the portal. It allows the administrator to invite multiple devices to join the service at a go. Once the invite is sent, a summary will display a list of devices that have successfully received the SMS Invitation. This will also include a list of devices that did to review the SMS Invite. This feature is only available to SMS enabled devices.

Send Email Invitation

Sending Email invitations is another way of onboarding devices to the service. The administrator can invite multiple devices to join or send an email invitation individually to each device. This feature is ideal for non SMS enabled devices like iPads or Mi-Fis devices.

Enable Device

The bulk action feature allows you to enable many devices simultaneously. When enabled, a device is allowed to consume data and access the Internet. This may be useful if you wish to allow the Internet on all of the devices in a Group.

Disable Device

The bulk action feature allows you to disable many devices simultaneously. When disabled, the device ceases to consume data or access the Internet. This may be useful if you wish to disable the Internet on all of the devices in a Group.

Deregister app

The bulk action feature allows you to deregister the app on multiple devices simultaneously.

Note: All actions are only permissible once the device is configured to the service. It’s data usage and policy are not controlled by the administrator.

How to use the bulk action feature

To make a bulk action on your account, navigate to Devices, click on Devices Overview.

  1. Select your users.

  2. Tap on the icon "Choose Action." This will enable once a user is selected.

  3. Choose the action you wish to implement from the drop-down.

  4. Apply and Send.

  • Authentication Type: PAP

4501165.title: Configure your device
4501165.description: Follow those simple steps to set up the service on O2 UK 4G
4501165.body:
You can configure any device to connect to the service. All you need to do is change the device configured Access Point Name (APN).

We have dedicated guides for iOS/iPadOS here and Android here.

Mifis, USB Modems or Dongles come with Connection Manager Software that enables you to configure the Settings of the device. Other cellular gateways and IoT devices may need manual configuration via terminal. Please refer to the device's user guide for more information on how to configure the APN.

Using the Connection Manager Software, create a new APN profile and

  1. Enter APN : vpn.amylan

  2. If the software requires credentials, set both the username and password to data.

  3. If there is an option to apply the Authentication Type, select PAP.
    Otherwise, leave all other fields empty.

4539066.title: USB Modem or Dongle
4539066.body:
USB Modems or Dongles come with Connection Manager Software that enables you to configure the device's settings.

How to create a new APN

Using the Connection Manager Software, create a new APN profile for the device.

  1. Enter APN: appropriate value from the APN list

  2. The software might require credentials, set both the username and password to data.

  3. If there is an option to apply the Authentication Type, select CHAP.
    Otherwise, leave all other fields empty.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab of your account.

4539113.title: iOS App Installation Configuration
4539113.description: Get your Verizon iOS device connected to the Platform
4539113.body:
Download the App to utilize the full features available on the service. The App supports all operating systems on iOS 11 and above.

To download the App from the App Store.

  • Search for Moda and install.

Once installed, open the App and follow these steps in the order written;

  • In the Welcome screen, press Install.

  • Under Device Verification, enter the device phone number.

  • Under Code Verification, enter the Activation Code in the App. iPhones will receive an SMS with the activation code. iPads will receive an email invitation with the activation code.

  • The following are optional: Under Location Services, press Allow Location Services, choose Always Allow Under Push Notifications, press Allow Push Notifications, and choose Allow Under Wi-Fi Protect, press Install Wi-Fi Protect, and select Allow.

Add Service via Verizon Portal

The service must be added to the device from your enterprise portal on Verizon. Confirm with the admin the feature has been added before proceeding to the next step

Open the App installed. Once opened, click on the following.

  1. Under the menu, tap Data Security, press Download Data Security and follow the steps to download the APN profile.

  2. Once the Profile has downloaded, close the App and navigate and select the following:

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • Under Profile, select the Moda APN, Install and follow the steps.
    If you are asked for a passcode, enter the device screen unlock code.

4539143.title: Windows Phone Configuration
4539143.description: Get your Verizon Windows device connected to the Platform
4539143.body:
You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

How to create a new APN

Windows Phone 10

  • Swipe down from the top of the screen and tap All settings.

  • Go to Settings.

  • Tap: Network & Wireless

  • Cellular SIM

  • SIM Settings.

  • Scroll down and tap Add Internet APN.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: appropriate value from the APN list

  • If prompted, enter Username: data.

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: CHAP (select normal if the type is not listed).

Tap the back button to Save

  • Scroll down, tap the Profile name created and Apply.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled.

Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. This option depends on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

Windows 8.1

On Windows Phone 8.1, you need to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • Scroll down and tap: Settings

  • Select Mobile + Sim

  • SIM Settings

  • Manual Internet APN set to on

  • Edit Internet APN Settings.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: appropriate value from the APN list

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: CHAP (select normal if CHAP is not listed).

Press Save.

The device will now appear as Connected. If it does not appear connected, re-enter APN details.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled. Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. This will depend on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

Windows Phone 8

On Windows Phone 8, you are required to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • On Start, flick left to App list.

  • Select Settings

  • Add Internet APN

  • APN.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: appropriate value from the APN list

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: CHAP (select normal if CHAP is not listed).

Tap Save.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled. Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. This will depend on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

Windows Phone 7 & 7.5

On Windows Phone 7 (and 7.5), you need to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • On Start, flick left to App list.

  • Select Settings

  • Add Internet APN

  • APN.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: appropriate value from the APN list

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: CHAP (select normal if CHAP is not listed).

Tap Done.

4539157.title: iOS APN Profile Configuration
4539157.description: Get your iOS Verizon device connected to the Platform
4539157.body:
How to get the APN Profile installed

  • Tap on the below link to download the profile to your computer

  • Send the profile via email to the device or via SMS from your portal

  • You can use the Message Devices to send an SMS, but remember that iPads cannot receive an SMS.

  • Verizon 2: eaaa.asavieii.vzwentp Download profile here

  • Verizon 3: eaaa.asavietw.vzwentp Download profile here

  • Verizon 4: eaaa.aone.vzwentp Download profile here

  • Verizon 5: eaaa.asa.vzwentp Download profile here

Once the configuration file has downloaded on the iOS device to install it, select:

  • Device

  • Settings

  • General

  • APN under DOWNLOADED PROFILE

  • Install and follow the steps.
    If a passcode is required, this is the device unlock screen code.

4539283.title: Configure your Android Device
4539283.description: Follow those simple steps to set up the service on Verizon
4539283.body: The Access Point Name (APN) will be set automatically on Android devices over-the-air (OTA) as soon as the feature is added via the Verizon Enterprise (steps here).

4539355.title: Over The Air Configuration
4539355.body:
Deploy the APN configuration Over The Air (OTA) seamlessly via the Verizon Enterprise Portal. This process requires zero user interaction, making it the preferred option.

This feature is available on the following devices:

  • Jetpacks

  • Androids

  • Mi-Fi

The following is a list of the APN values for Verizon. The appropriate highlighted values for your smart device plan should be entered in the appropriate APN fields.

4539414.title: Add/Remove the feature using Verizon Enterprise.
4539414.description: This is a guide on how to set up the service on the Verizon Enterprise Portal.
4539414.body:
To avail of the service we provide, admins must enable the service to a Verizon SIM. To do so, you would need to visit your Verizon Enterprise Portal; once logged in, you would need to complete the following steps.

Note:

Do not forget to create the device in your account first. If you are adding the service to iOS devices, see here.

Contact your Verizon representative if your Verizon customer portal is not accessible or request adding the feature in-bulk.

How to add the service

  1. Log into Verizon Enterprise.

  2. Select Manage Account

  3. Under Services, select Manage Wireless Numbers.

  4. Expand or Search By: Wireless Number for the mobile number you wish to edit.

  5. Under Features: Chargeable, click Edit.

  6. Scroll down and select IP Provisioning.

  7. Enable the action toggle for the Private Dynamic.

  8. Next to your wireless number, enable the action toggle, and select Continue.

  9. Select the available IP pool from the dropdown and click Reserve.

  10. Ensure today's date is selected and click Submit Order.

Note:

  • When adding the Private Dynamic IP feature, Verizon will queue out the APN Over-The-Air (OTA) for 72 hours to any OTA-capable device like Android devices or Jetpacks (iOS devices are not OTA compatible. See this page for iOS deployment).

  • If a device has not been switched on within 72 hours, it may require the Private Dynamic IP feature to be removed and re-added to queue out the APN OTA for an additional 72 hours.

  • Seventy-two hours APN queue is only applicable to devices that were already active on the Verizon network; new SIM activations are not limited to this time window.

  • If your device fails to connect after adding the Private Dynamic IP feature, please contact WEHD on 800-525-0481 for further assistance.

How to disable the service

Repeat steps 1-5 above.

  1. Scroll down to IP Provisioning and toggle from green (currently enabled) to grey (removed)

  2. Select Continue.

  3. Click Submit.

Note:

Ensure Make effective today is selected.

Removed Features will be displayed. If you are configuring iOS devices, you need to finalize the APN installation on each device.

4553032.title: Recommendation for setting up an Education Policy
4553032.body:
The Internet has immense potential to improve the quality of education, one of the pillars of sustainable development. We believe it's important to create a compliant policy with what is recommended by the education system. Here is our recommendation:

Navigate to Data Control, click on Policies, and click on the Default Policy.

Block everything except for Education and Science & Technology. This will block the “Search” category as well. So it would be best if you built a whitelist for the following search engines: 

youtube.com
youtubei.googleapis.com
youtube.googleapis.com
googlevideo.com
youtube-nocookie.com
google.com
google.us
bing.com
duckduckgo.com

Save and Apply

4553484.title: Network Connector Cipher Suites
4553484.description: Learn the specific VPN configuration and cipher suites supported by the Network Connector TLS connection
4553484.body:
The Network Connector initiates an IP over TLS connection (TLS1.2) to specific service IP addresses on port 443 with the following spec:

Authentication: RSA 2048/SHA256 certificates

Cipher suites supported:

  • TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256_P256

  • TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256_P256

  • TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA_P256

  • TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256

  • TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256

  • TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA

  • TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384_P256

  • TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA384_P256

  • TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA_P256

  • TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384

  • TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA256

  • TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA

  • TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_3DES_EDE_CBC_SHA_P256

  • TLS_RSA_WITH_3DES_EDE_CBC_SHA.

Note:

  • The service will choose the cipher suites based on the Network Connector offers, depending on the OS version.

  • For example, if a customer is using Windows Server 2008 with a default configuration, the cipher suite would be used TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA384_P256.

For other scenarios, the suite could be different depending on OS capabilities.

4586566.title: Cookie Notice
4586566.body:
1. Introduction

We respect your privacy and are committed to protecting your personal data. This Cookie Notice will inform you as to how we use cookies when you visit our website(s) (regardless of where you visit from) or use our services, products or otherwise provide personal data to us.

2. Why we use cookies

A cookie is a small data file stored by Asavie services on your device (e.g. your phone or your computer) which collects non-Personal Identifiable Information and let you navigate the Asavie services effectively.

We use cookies to help us remember details of your visits to the management interfaces (either the Web UI or API), Help Centers or Developer Portals, all of which form the service being provided. When using our Web based interfaces, the following kinds of information are recorded, including, but not limited to technical information about your visit e.g. traffic data, location data, browser language, your IP address, the previous website from which you reached us and the type of browser you use, domain, number and time of visits. Accordingly, our Privacy Statement will apply to our treatment of the information we obtain via our cookies.

The service makes use of cookies to support analysis and understanding of how people interact with the service. We perform this analysis to help us improve the interface and effectiveness of the Asavie services. Asavie uses cookies that are persistent-based. Persistent cookies remain on your computer after you close your browser or turn off your computer. The data collection and reporting behind this analysis is currently provided by Google Analytics. Some elements of the service require you be routed to the nearest available Azure resource for the duration of the session you establish, the Azure cookies noted below are required to enable that functionality.

4586573.title: Privacy Notice
4586573.body:
1. Introduction

We respect your privacy and are committed to protecting your personal data. This Privacy Notice will inform you as to how we look after your personal data when you visit our website(s) (regardless of where you visit from) or use our services, products or otherwise provide personal data to us. This notice will also tell you about your privacy rights and how the law protects you.

2. Important information

2.1 Purpose of this Privacy Notice

Asavie, its subsidiaries and partner companies (“us”, “we” or “our”) is committed to protecting and respecting your privacy. The Privacy Notice, End User Licence Agreement and the documents referred to or linked in them set out the basis for which any data we collect from you or that you provide to us (“Data”) will be processed by us.

Please read this Privacy Notice carefully to understand our treatment and use of Data. For the purposes of the Data Protection Acts 2018, and any subsequent amendment (the “Acts”), the data processor is Asavie Technologies Limited, a private limited company and registered in Ireland (Registered Number 390724) and having its address at 100 Mount Street Lower, Grand Canal Dock, Dublin 2, D02 TY46, Ireland. Asavie is not the data controller for the purposes of the Acts. Should you require any information regarding how your data is controlled, you should consult the Data Controller Privacy Notice (typically this will be your employer) to understand how the Data Controller processes your data.

This Privacy Notice aims to give you information on how we receive and process your personal data to perform the requirements of the agreed contract(s).

Unless otherwise agreed, our website(s), products and services are not intended for children and we do not knowingly collect data relating to children. Children under the age of 13, or equivalent minimum age in the relevant jurisdiction, are not permitted to use the Site or Application except where such use is in the provision of services to educational institutions and, in those cases, use must be compliant with COPPA/GDPR requirements. If we learn that we have collected the personal information of a child under 13, or equivalent minimum age depending on jurisdiction, we will take steps to delete the information as soon as possible. Please contact us with any such information.

It is important that you read this Privacy Notice together with any other privacy notice or fair processing notice we may provide on specific occasions when we are collecting or processing personal data about you so that you are fully aware of how and why we are using your data. This Privacy Notice supplements any other such notices and is not intended to override them.

2.2 Third-party links

The website(s), products and services may include links to third-party websites, plug-ins, and applications. Clicking on those links or enabling those connections may allow third parties to collect or share data about you. We do not control these third-party website(s), products or services and are not responsible for their privacy statements. When you leave our website(s), products, or services, we encourage you to read the privacy notice of the 3rd Party website(s), products, or service.

3. The data we collect about you

3. Types of Data

Personal data, or personal information, means any information about an individual from which that person can be identified. It does not include data where the identity has been removed (anonymous data). We may collect, use, store and transfer different kinds of personal data about you to provide the service, which we have grouped together as follows:

3.1 Identity Data

When accessing our product Administrative console, the configured administrator email addresses and any associated Name or Identifier is recorded. An optional email address and textual description can be configured for your devices. If provided, then Asavie services will collect and process this information. The email address is an option to invite a device to setup the service while the device description aids understanding of the reporting and alerting provided.

3.2 Contact Data

Contact Data includes billing addresses, email addresses as noted in section 3.1 and telephone numbers.

3.3 Technical Data

Technical Data includes internet protocol (IP) address, your login data, browser type and version, time zone setting and location, browser plug-in types and versions, operating system and platform and other technology on the devices you use to contact us or access this website. For managed devices where the Application is used as the mobile device manager, then the Application may configure a password policy for the device, lock the device, initiate a remote wipe and collect the names of applications installed on the device. The application name data will only be collected if you enable the App Control feature in the Application management portal. For devices using the Application the location of the device may be captured if location services are enabled.

3.4 Profile Data

When devices use our service the Device number (MSISDN & IMEI), time and date the Device is accessing the network over mobile data or Wi-Fi, the amount of data sent and received, the location of access, websites accessed and the type of website accessed, website access attempts blocked and the type of website access attempt blocked due to policy enforcement actions are all recorded. These data points are all required to deliver the service offered and to perform the contract.

3.5 Usage Data

The Application makes use of cookies on the website(s) to support analysis and understanding of how people interact with the Application. We perform this analysis to help us improve the interface and effectiveness of the Application. Asavie uses cookies that are session-based and persistent-based. Session cookies exist only during one session. They disappear from your computer when you close your browser software or turn off your computer. Persistent cookies remain on your computer after you close your browser or turn off your computer. Please note that if you disable your Web browser’s ability to accept cookies, you will not be able to successfully use the Site. The data collection and reporting behind this analysis is currently provided by Google Analytics and Intercom. Further details on use of cookies may be found in our cookie statement here.

We use third-party analytics services to help understand your usage of our services. In particular, we provide a limited amount of your information (such as sign-up date and some personal information like your email address) to Intercom, Inc. (“Intercom”) and utilize Intercom to collect data for analytics purposes when you visit our website or use our product. As a data processor acting on our behalf, Intercom analyses your use of our website and/or product and tracks our relationship by way of cookies and similar technologies so that we can improve our service to you. For more information on Intercom's use of cookies, please visit https://www.intercom.com/terms-and-policies#cookie-policy. We may also use Intercom as a medium for communications, either through email, or through messages within our product(s). The Intercom Messenger Apps and Apps in Inbox products may also provide you with access to other third-party applications such as NativeForms. You should consult these third parties' privacy notices for further information on their use of your personal data. For more information on the privacy practices of Intercom, please visit here. Intercom’s services are governed by Intercom’s terms of use which can be found at here.

We may also collect, and use, Aggregated Data such as statistical or demographic data to improve our products and services. Aggregated Data may be derived from your personal data but is not considered personal data in law as this data does not directly or indirectly reveal your identity.

For example, we may aggregate your Usage Data to calculate the percentage of users accessing a specific website feature. However, if we combine or connect Aggregated Data with your personal data so that it can directly or indirectly identify you, we treat the combined data as personal data which will be used in accordance with this Privacy Notice.

We do not collect any Special Categories of Personal Data about you (this includes details about your race or ethnicity, religious or philosophical beliefs, sex life, sexual orientation, political opinions, trade union membership, information about your health and genetic and biometric data). Nor do we collect any information about criminal convictions and offences.

If you fail to provide personal data where we need to collect personal data by law, or under the terms of a contract we have with you and you fail to provide that data when requested, we may not be able to perform the contract we have or are trying to enter into with you.

4. How personal data is collected

We use different methods to collect data from and about you including through:

4.1 Direct interactions

You may give us your Identity, Contact and Financial Data filling in forms or by corresponding with us by post, phone, email, or otherwise. This includes personal data you provide when you interact with our website(s), products, or services.

4.2 Automated technologies or interactions

As you interact with our website, we may automatically collect Technical Data about your equipment, browsing actions and patterns. We collect this personal data by using cookies, and other similar technologies. We may also receive Technical Data about you if you visit other websites employing our cookies. Please see our cookie policy for further details.

When you use our service, we automatically collect the information outlined in section 3.

4.3 Third parties or publicly available sources

We may receive personal data about you, to be able to provide the services we offer, from various third parties as set out below:

· GEO IP information

· Autonomous System information

· Analytics providers such as Google and HubSpot

· Advertising networks such as Google

· Search information providers such as Google and Bing

5. How we use your personal data

5.1 Lawful basis

We will only use your personal data when the law allows us to. Most commonly, we will use your personal data in the following circumstances where:

· We need to perform the contract we are about to enter or have entered into with you.

· It is necessary for our legitimate interests (or those of a third party) and your interests and fundamental rights do not override those interests.

· Where we need to comply with a legal or regulatory obligation.

Generally, we do not rely on consent as a legal basis for processing your personal data other than in relation to sending third party direct marketing communications to you via email or text message. You have the right to withdraw your consent to marketing at any time by contacting us at dataprotection@asavie.com or using the available unsubscribe features in the communication.

6. Purposes for which we will use your personal data

6.1 Data Use

We may use your Data that we process to:

  • Ensure that content from the Site and/or the Application is presented in the most effective, responsive, and compatible way for you and your computer or device

  • Improve our services and products provided by us to you

  • Carry out our obligations arising from any contracts entered between you and us

  • Carry out statistical analysis for product development purposes

For customers using Asavie Self-Serve Analytics offerings, and to fulfil the contract as requested in these cases, Asavie must share the necessary data with Microsoft (Power BI) and/or Tableau. Only users authorised by the customer have access to the data presented in these 3rd Party Applications.

6.2 Change of purpose

We will only use your personal data for the purposes for which we collected it, unless we reasonably consider that we need to use it for another reason and that reason is compatible with the original purpose. If you wish to get an explanation as to how the processing for the new purpose is compatible with the original purpose, please contact us at dataprotection@asavie.com.If we need to use your personal data for an unrelated purpose, we will notify you and we will explain the legal basis which allows us to do so. Please note that we may process your personal data without your knowledge or consent, in compliance with the above rules, where this is required or permitted by law.

7. Disclosures of your personal data

We may have to share your personal data with the parties set out below for the purposes set out in the table above.

  • Internal Third Parties as set out in the Glossary.

  • External Third Parties as set out in the Glossary.

  • Third parties to whom we may choose to sell, transfer, or merge parts of our business or our assets; alternatively, we may seek to acquire other businesses or merge with them; if a change happens to our business, then the new owners or newly acquired businesses may use your personal data in the same way as set out in this Privacy Notice.

We require all third parties to respect the security of your personal data and to treat it in accordance with the law. We do not allow our third-party service providers to use your personal data for their own purposes and only permit them to process your personal data for specified purposes and in accordance with our instructions.

8. International transfers

Our products are managed via management interfaces (either the Web UI or API) that are designed to be served to the Customer Administrator from their home region, being one of EU, US or APAC, using data sets stored in that region, with the exception of provisioning (Categorisation Rules for example) and billing information which is stored in the EU or APAC.

Our products are delivered to end-users in their home region, being one of EU, US or APAC, using data sets stored in that region.

When undertaking any type of data transfers, we use contracts approved by the European Commission which give personal data the same protection it has in the European Union. We establish the adequacy of any non-EU region regarding that region’s Privacy Laws, ensuring they provide similar levels of protection to those offered by the GDPR (or similar regional Privacy Legislation) before any transfers are undertaken. Please contact your Data Controller (typically your employer) or contact us at dataprotection@asavie.com if you want further information on the mechanisms used to store or transfer your data in the provision of our services and products.

9. Data security

We have put in place appropriate security measures to prevent your personal data from being accidentally lost, used, or accessed in an unauthorised way, altered, or disclosed. In addition, we limit access to your personal data to those employees, agents, contractors and other third parties who must have such access to supply or maintain our services. All staff are trained in Data Security and Data Privacy and understand their responsibilities when processing customer data and will only process your personal data on your instructions, as needed by contract.

We have put in place procedures to deal with any suspected personal data breach and will notify you and any applicable supervisory authority or other regulator of a breach where we are legally required to do so.

10. Data retention

We will only retain your personal data for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes we collected it for, including for the purposes of satisfying any legal, accounting, or reporting requirements.

To determine the appropriate retention period for personal data, we consider the type, amount, nature, and sensitivity of the personal data, the potential risk of harm from unauthorised use or disclosure of your personal data, the purposes for which we process your personal data and whether we can achieve those purposes through other means, and the applicable legal requirements.

By law we must keep some information about our customers (including Contact, Identity, Financial and Transaction Data) after they cease being customers for tax and other legal purposes. These are outlined in our Data Retention Policies. We only ever keep such information to comply with local or regional legal requirements.

In some circumstances you can ask us to delete your data: see below for further information.

In some circumstances we may anonymise your personal data (so that it can no longer be associated with you) for research or statistical purposes in which case we may use this information indefinitely without further notice to you.

11. Your legal rights

Under certain circumstances, you have rights under data protection laws in relation to your personal data. Please read below to find out more about these rights.

To request access to your personal data or to exercise any of the rights set out above, please contact us please contact dsar@asavie.com.

For general queries regarding your rights, please contact your Data Controller (typically your employer) or contact us at dataprotection@asavie.com.


You will not have to pay a fee to access your personal data (or to exercise any of the other rights). However, we may charge a reasonable fee if your request is clearly unfounded, repetitive, or excessive. Alternatively, we may refuse to comply with your request in these circumstances.

We may need to request specific information from you to help us confirm your identity and ensure your right to access your personal data (or to exercise any of your other rights). This is a security measure to ensure that personal data is not disclosed to any person who has no right to receive it. We may also contact you to ask you for further information in relation to your request to speed up our response. We try to respond to all legitimate requests within one month. Occasionally it may take us longer than a month if your request is particularly complex or you have made several requests. In this case, we will notify you and keep you updated.

11.1 Your rights explained

Access Request: You can request access to your personal data (commonly known as a “data subject access request”). This enables you to receive a copy of the personal data we hold about you and to check that we are lawfully processing it.

Data Correction: You can request correction of the personal data that we hold about you. This enables you to have any incomplete or inaccurate data we hold about you corrected, though we may need to verify the accuracy of the new data you provide to us.

Data erasure: You can request erasure of your personal data. This enables you to ask us to delete or remove personal data where there is no good reason for us continuing to process it. You also have the right to ask us to delete or remove your personal data where you have successfully exercised your right to object to processing (see below), where we may have processed your information unlawfully or where we are required to erase your personal data to comply with local law. Note, however, that we may not always be able to comply with your request of erasure for specific legal reasons which will be notified to you, if applicable, at the time of your request.

Objecting to Processing: You can object to processing of your personal data where we are relying on a legitimate interest (or those of a third party) and there is something about your particular situation which makes you want to object to processing on this ground as you feel it impacts on your fundamental rights and freedoms. You also have the right to object where we are processing your personal data for direct marketing purposes. In some cases, we may demonstrate that we have a compelling legitimate ground to process your information which overrides your rights and freedoms.

Restriction of Processing: You can request restriction of processing of your personal data. This enables you to ask us to suspend the processing of your personal data in the following scenarios: (a) if you want us to establish the data’s accuracy; (b) where our use of the data is unlawful but you do not want us to erase it; (c) where you need us to hold the data even if we no longer require it as you need it to establish, exercise or defend legal claims; or (d) you have objected to our use of your data but we need to verify whether we have overriding legitimate grounds to use it.

Request a Data Transfer to 3rd Party: You can request the transfer of your personal data to you or to a third party. We will provide to you, or a third party you have chosen, your personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format. Note that this right only applies to automated information which you initially provided consent for us to use or where we used the information to perform a contract with you.

Withdraw Consent: You can withdraw consent at any time where we are relying on consent to process your personal data. However, this will not affect the lawfulness of any processing carried out before you withdraw your consent. If you withdraw your consent, we may not be able to provide certain offerings to you. We will advise you if this is the case at the time you withdraw your consent.

12. Glossary

Legitimate Interest means our interest in conducting and managing our business to enable us to give you the best offering and the best and most secure experience; we make sure we consider and balance any potential impact on you (both positive and negative) and your rights before we process your personal data for our legitimate interests; we do not use your personal data for activities where our interests are overridden by the impact on you (unless we have your consent or are otherwise required or permitted to by law); you can obtain further information about how we assess our legitimate interests against any potential impact on you in respect of specific activities by contacting us at dataprotection@asavie.com.

Performance of Contract means processing your data where it is necessary for the performance of a contract to which you are a party or to take steps at your request before entering such a contract.

Comply with a legal or regulatory obligation means processing your personal data where it is necessary for compliance with a legal or regulatory obligation that we are subject to.

Internal Third Parties are other companies in the Asavie Group based in the EEA that provide IT and system administration services and marketing services to the Asavie Group.

External Third Parties are

the Revenue Commissioners, regulators and other authorities acting as processors or joint controllers based in Ireland who require reporting of processing activities in certain circumstances.

Note that if you are based outside of the EEA, then some of the parties described may also be outside the EEA.

4604799.title: Web portal authentication security
4604799.description: Authentication to the web portal using 2FA, password and identity protection
4604799.body:
General users are identified in the web portal by their email address, and Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) is used to validate that the user is indeed who they claim to be. Therefore, authentication is done with both:

  • Password (something the user knows).
    The password is set by the user on registration and can be modified by them at any time.

  • Phone verification (something the user has).
    The user is required to provide a mobile phone number on registration. A unique code required for login is sent to this number via SMS (or voice call) on every subsequent login.

Password strength

Password strength is enforced to mitigate against brute-force or password spray attacks:

  • Password complexity requires a minimum length of 14 characters that must have at least 3 of the following: lowercase letter, uppercase letter, digit and symbol.

  • Passwords that are commonly used, weak, or compromised are not allowed.

Password protection and lockout

Password is required for each login request with only strong passwords being accepted. In order to protect against credential attacks, a user logging in can be locked out after several failed login attempts. Various signals are utilized like the request's IP, the password entered and other factors, to intelligently differentiate intended users from hackers and botnets. The duration of the lockout depends on the risk identified. E.g., initially, after ten failed login attempts, a 1-minute lockout is applied. This lockout period can be increased (up to five hours) if this happens many times. The lockout counter resets to zero after a successful login.

NOTEs:

  • Password expiration is not enforced. Password expiration is a mechanism for mitigation of the threat of password duplication / theft. The approach to mitigate this risk is to enforce 2FA, usage of strong and complex passwords, and detection and banning of common / compromised passwords. This also follows NIST requirements, where password expiration is not considered something that improves security.

  • Following the same approach, password reuse is allowed. The service detects and blocks known weak passwords and their variations. This applies at registration time and also on any subsequent password reset. The list of banned passwords is constantly analyzed by Azure AD Identity Protection team.

Security

The portal uses Microsoft Azure Active Directory B2C (Azure AD B2C) as the Identity Provider to manage user identities and credentials. Our servers do not save portal Credentials/Passwords.

User and session details are always stored encrypted, with all communication being protected with TLS for confidentiality and integrity.

Auditing and access logs

All user and authentication activity is logged. This includes creating, modifying, or deleting user details, validation of credentials, lockouts, and other administrative actions related to users.

NOTE: Audit logs are internal-only and available on request for incident management purposes.

4669973.title: iPhone/iPad Microsoft Intune Deployment
4669973.description: Get your AccessMyLAN or FirstNet devices connected via Microsoft Intune
4669973.body:
Implement the right APN setup applied to your chosen iOS devices by configuring a Microsoft Intune Device Profile.

Note:

Microsoft Intune does not support the configuration of the APN for Samsung Android devices. Please refer to your Microsoft Intune support contact for validation here.

How to create a new APN

1. First, download the appropriate iOS Configuration Profile:

  • AccessMyLAN APN

  • AccessMyLAN FirstNet APN

2. Create a Microsoft Intune iOS Device Profile. See How to create device profile in Microsoft Intune

  • Name: descriptive name for the profile such as APN Profile

  • Description: optional description

  • Platform: iOS/iPadOS

  • Profile type: Custom.

3. Under the Custom Configuration Profile Blade:

  • Custom configuration profile name: set as required

  • Configuration profile file: Import the iOS Configuration Profile downloaded in the first step. You will see the contents of the configuration profile displayed in File Contents.

4. When complete, return to the Create profile blade and select Create.

5. To deploy the configuration profile, you must assign them to the group(s) of devices that you want to apply this setting. See How to assign user and device profiles in Microsoft Intune.

  • Choose the Microsoft Intune iOS Device Profile you created in the second step and on the APN Profile Reports blade, select:

  • MANAGE

  • Assignments.

  • Choose: Include

  • Assign to: Groups you want to apply the profile.

Save.

6. Microsoft Intune will now instruct the affected devices to check in with the Intune service. When the device checks in next, the device profile will be applied, and the device should automatically be configured to use the AccessMyLAN APN. The process typically takes less than five minutes.

See Troubleshooting device profiles in Microsoft Intune for additional information.

4670196.description: Get your AML and FirstNet devices connected via IBM MaaS360
4670196.body:
Implement the right APN setup to your chosen iOS/iPadOS devices by defining the appropriate MDM Policy within your IBM MaaS360 portal. A separate policy is used for iOS devices. You can choose to modify existing Policies or add new ones.

Once you have your policy defined, you can apply it to individuals or groups of devices.

How to create a Policy

To add a new Policy from within your IBM MaaS360 Portal:

  • Select Security

  • Policies

  • Select Add Policy.

Enter the following settings:

  • Name: Descriptive name for the policy. This can be APN Policy.

  • Description: Optional description

  • Type: iOS MDM

  • Start From: An existing iOS MDM policy as a template for your new policy.

Select Continue

Expand Advanced Settings and select Cellular

Select Edit

Select: Configure Advanced Settings

Enter:

  • Name of the Access Point Carrier: select the appropriate APN name here

  • Authentication Type: PAP

  • Access Point User Name: mdm

  • Access Point Password: mdm

  • Leave all other fields blank.

Save And Publish.

To Publish the policy enter:

  • Optional description to help with any future audits

  • password.

Select Continue.

Your new iOS MDM Policy can now be applied to individual or groups of devices.

4670382.title: iPhone/iPad VMware Workspace ONE UEM Deployment
4670382.description: Get your AML or FirstNet devices connected via VMware WSP1 UEM
4670382.body:
Implement the right APN setup to your chosen iOS/iPadOS devices by defining the appropriate Device Profile within your VMware Workspace ONE UEM portal.

A separate Device Profile is used for iOS/iPadOS devices. You can choose to modify existing Device Profiles or add new ones. Once you have your profile defined, you can apply it to individuals or groups of devices.

How to create a Profile

1. Navigate to:

  • Devices

  • Profiles & Resources

  • Profiles.

2. Select:

  • ADD

  • Add Profile.

3. Select Apple iOS and Device Profile

4. Configure the profile's General settings. These settings determine how the profile is deployed and who receives it and should be set according to your organization's needs. Some recommendations include:

  • Name: descriptive name for the policy such as APN Profile

  • Description: optional description

  • Deployment: either Managed or Manual according to your general management approach Profile

  • Scope: Production or Both if you wish to test the profile in staging

  • Assignment Type: Auto

  • Allow Removal: as per management approach. Suggest With Authorization Managed By: as per administrative approach

  • Assigned Groups: Set to the groups you wish to receive this profile. In this case, we assume that the devices that should receive the APN Profile setting are grouped via a standard or smart group

  • Exclusions: as per preferred deployment approach

  • Install only on devices inside selected areas: unchecked

  • Enable Scheduling and install only during selected periods: unchecked Removal Date: unset.

5. Choose a Cellular Payload and select Configure.

6. Under the APN list (leaving the Attach APN fields blank) configure the APN as follows:

  • APN: select the appropriate APN name here

  • User Name: mdm

  • Password: mdm

  • Authentication Type: PAP

Leave all other areas empty.

Select Save & Publish.

VMware Workspace ONE UEM will now apply this profile to the identified devices at the next available opportunity.

Source: https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-Workspace-ONE-UEM/2011/iOS_Platform/GUID-AWT-PROFILECELLULAR.html

4670470.title: Samsung Knox VMware Workspace ONE UEM Deployment

A separate profile is used for Samsung Knox devices. You can choose to modify existing device profiles or add new ones. Once you have your profile defined, you can apply it to individuals or groups of devices.

How to create a Profile

1. Navigate to:

  • Devices

  • Profiles & Resources

  • Profiles.

2. Select:

  • ADD

  • Add Profile.

3. Select Android.

Configure the profile’s General settings. The APN profile only displays when the OEM Settings field is toggled to Enabled and Samsung is selected from the Select OEM field. These settings determine how the profile is deployed and who receives it. They should be set according to your organizations needs. Some recommendations include:

  • Name : descriptive name for the policy such as APN Profile

  • Description : optional description

  • Deployment : either Managed or Manual according to your general management approach

  • Profile Scope : Production or Both if you wish to test the profile in staging

  • Assignment Type : Auto

  • Allow Removal : unset

  • Managed By : as per administrative approach

  • Assigned Groups : set to the groups you wish to receive this profile. In this case we assume that the devices that should receive the APN Profile setting are grouped together via a standard or smart group

  • Exclusions : as per preferred deployment approach

  • Install only on devices inside selected areas : unchecked

  • Enable Scheduling and install only during selected time periods : unchecked

  • Removal Date : unset.

4. Choose the APN payload and provide the following settings:

  • Display Name : descriptive name for the profile such as APN Profile

  • Access Point Name (APN) : appropriate value from APN name here

  • Access Point Type : default

  • Mobile Country Code (MCC): appropriate value here

  • Mobile Network Code (MNC): appropriate value here

  • Access Point Username : mdm

  • Access Point Password : mdm

  • Authentication Type : PAP

  • Set as Preferred : check this box.

5. Select Save & Publish.

VMware Workspace ONE UEM will now apply this profile to the identified devices at the next available opportunity.

Source: https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-Workspace-ONE-UEM/2011/Android_Platform/GUID-AWT-PROFILEAPN.html

4670511.title: Samsung Knox Mobile App Installation
4670511.description: Get your AML or FirstNet devices connected via Mobile App
4670511.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect the policies created, data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

The AccessMyLAN app supports Android 6 and above and Knox v2.0 and above.

There are two ways to install the App.

  • Invite device from the portal.

  • Install the App from the App Store.

Invite Device via Portal

  • Send an invite to the device via SMS.

  • Send an invite to the device via Email. This feature is suitable for non-SMS enabled devices.

How to invite a user to join

Navigate to Devices, click on Devices Overview.

  1. Select the checkbox of the user you wish to invite

  2. Once selected, click on the icon Choose Action and select Send SMS or Email Invitation

  3. Select Apply and Send

To download the App from the Google Play Store

Once installed, open the AccessMyLAN app.

1. In the Welcome screen, press Install.

2.Under Device Verification:

  • Tap I’m ready

  • Activate device admin

  • Enter the device phone number.

3. Under Code Verification, enter the Activation Code in the app, which is received for:

  • SMS-enabled devices as an SMS

  • Non-SMS-enabled devices as an email invitation.

4. (optional ) Under Location Services, press Let's Continue and choose Allow.

5. Under KLMS Agent, accept the terms and conditions and press CONFIRM.

6. (optional ) Under Wi-Fi Protect, press I'm ready, choose Certificate Wi-fi, and Connection request OK.

7. Under Optimise, press, I'm ready, and make sure the app is not optimized.

Once complete, your mobile device will have connected to AccessMyLAN.

4671147.title: Why are the reports not showing data?
4671147.body:
Some users might experience a problem loading the reports page that might be empty. This sometimes can be due to certain ad blockers preventing the browser from loading the requests. You can verify if this is the cause of the problem by checking the logs for the ad blocker and confirming the requests that are being clocked.

The simplest way to solve the problem is to disable the ad blockers for the reports page and reload it (check your specific ad blocker for instructions).

Optionally, administrators can open the ad blocker configuration/dashboard and add a rule to whitelist the specific URL for the reports requests (https://api.securemobi.net/v2)

4676402.title: iPhone/iPad MobileIron Cloud Deployment
4676402.description: Get your AML or FirstNet devices connected via MobileIron Cloud
4676402.body:
Implement the right APN setup applied to your chosen iOS devices by configuring a MobileIron Cloud profile.

From within your MobileIron Cloud portal:

1. Select:

  • Configurations

  • + Add.

2. Select Mobile.

3. Enter:

  • Name: descriptive name for the profile such as APN Profile

  • Description: optional description

  • Configured APN Types : Data APNs

  • APN Name : select the appropriate APN name here

  • APN Authentication Type : PAP

  • User Name : mdm

  • Password : mdm

  • Leave all remaining fields blank.

4. Select Next →.

5. Check Enable this configuration.

6. Choose your preferred option:

  • All Devices to deploy the configuration to all devices

  • No Devices if the configuration is to be deployed later

  • Custom to select specific device groups to which this configuration should be applied

and select the groups as required.

7. Select Done.

MobileIron Cloud will now distribute the configuration to the selected devices at the next available opportunity.

4. Under KLMS Agent, accept the terms and conditions and press Next.

5. Under Location Services, press Let's Continue and choose Allow.

Once complete, your mobile device will have connected to Asavie Moda.

4676644.title: Android APN Deployment
4676644.description: Get your Verizon devices connected Over The Air (OTA)
4676644.body:
The Access Point Name (APN) will be set automatically on Android devices over-the-air (OTA) as soon as the feature is added via the Verizon Enterprise (steps here).

Note:

  • When adding the Private Dynamic IP feature, Verizon will queue out the APN Over-The-Air (OTA) for 72 hours to any OTA-capable device like Android devices or Jetpacks (iOS devices are not OTA compatible. See this page for iOS deployment).

  • If a device has not been switched on within 72 hours, it may require the Private Dynamic IP feature to be removed and re-added to queue out the APN OTA for an additional 72 hours.

  • Seventy-two hours APN queue is only applicable to devices that were already active on the Verizon network; new SIM activations are not limited to this time window.

  • If your device fails to connect after adding the Private Dynamic IP feature, please contact WEHD on 800-525-0481 for further assistance.

4676676.title: Android APN Deployment
4676676.description: Get your Asavie Moda devices via APN
4676676.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect the policies created, data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

How to connect your device

Deployment via mobile app (Samsung Knox only)

The mobile app is required for Wi-Fi Protect.

See more here.

Deployment via configuration message (Samsung, LG and others)

Available for O2 UK, EE UK and VF UK only.

The service can be deployed with the Asavie Moda support team without requiring a mobile app installation, with a few simple steps.

The device user will receive a configuration message to install on its device. This message will auto-configure the APN settings for the user automatically.

You will need to provide us with the IMEI for each device, and (optionally) the IMSI. Providing the IMSI of each device sim will allow to remove a step where the user has to enter a pin to complete the install.

Here is the end user experience if IMSI is provided:

Here is the end user experience if IMSI is not provided:

Start a conversation with support if you want to discuss this deployment option.

Deployment via manual configuration

From the home screen, tap the Menu button.

1. Navigate to:

  • Applications

  • Settings

  • Mobile Networks (tap More if necessary)

  • Access Point Names.

2. Tap the ADD button normally located at the top right of the screen.

3. Locate the following fields and enter:

  • APN: select the appropriate APN name here

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: PAP

  • MCC: select the appropriate field value here

  • MNC: select the appropriate field value here

  • APN Type: default.

4. Save the new APN configuration.

Once complete, your mobile device should now show Connected on the portal.

Tip: If the device does not show a status of Connected:

Make sure the device is not connected to Wi-Fi and Mobile Data is turned on.

Select the refresh icon in the top right-hand

4676768.title: iPhone/iPad MobileIron Cloud Deployment
4676768.description: Get your VDC devices connected via MobileIron Cloud
4676768.body:
Implement the right APN setup applied to your chosen iOS devices by configuring a MobileIron Cloud profile.

From within your MobileIron Cloud portal:

1. Select:

  • Configurations

  • + Add.

2. Select Mobile.

3. Enter:

  • Name: descriptive name for the profile such as APN Profile

  • Description: optional description

  • Configured APN Types: Data APNs

  • APN Name: asavie.net

  • APN Authentication Type: PAP

  • User Name: mdm

  • Password: mdm

  • Leave all remaining fields blank.

4. Select Next →.

5. Check Enable this configuration.

6. Choose your preferred option:

  • All Devices to deploy the configuration to all devices

  • No Devices if the configuration is to be deployed later

  • Custom to select specific device groups to which this configuration should be applied

and select the groups as required.

7. Select Done.

MobileIron Cloud will now distribute the configuration to the selected devices at the next available opportunity.

4676935.title: iPhone/iPad IBM MaaS360 Deployment
4676935.description: Get your VDC devices connected via IBM MaaS360
4676935.body:
Implement the right APN setup to your chosen iOS devices by defining the appropriate MDM Policy within your IBM MaaS360 portal. A separate policy is used for iOS devices. You can choose to modify existing UEM Policies or add new ones.

Once you have your policy defined, you can apply it to individuals or groups of devices.

How to create a UEM Policy

To add a new iOS UEM Policy from within your IBM MaaS360 Portal:

  • Select Security

  • Policies

  • Select Add Policy.

Enter the following settings:

  • Name: Descriptive name for the policy. This can be APN Policy.

  • Description: Optional description

  • Type: iOS MDM

  • Start From: An existing iOS MDM policy as a template for your new policy.

Select Continue.

Expand Advanced Settings and select Cellular.

Select Edit.

Select: Configure Advanced Settings.

Enter:

  • Name of the Access Point Carrier: asavie.net

  • Authentication Type: PAP

  • Access Point User Name: mdm

  • Access Point Password: mdm

  • Leave all other fields blank.

Save And Publish.

To Publish the policy enter:

  • Optional description to help with any future audits

  • password.

Select Continue.

Your new iOS MDM Policy can now be applied to individual or groups of devices.

4676955.title: iPhone/iPad VMware Workspace ONE UEM Deployment
4676955.description: Get your VDC devices connected via VMware WSP1 UEM
4676955.body:
Implement the right APN setup to your chosen iOS/iPadOS devices by defining the appropriate Device Profile within your VMware Workspace ONE UEM portal.

A separate Device Profile is used for iOS/iPadOS devices. You can choose to modify existing Device Profiles or add new ones. Once you have your profile defined, you can apply it to individuals or groups of devices.

How to create a Profile

1. Navigate to:

  • Devices

  • Profiles & Resources

  • Profiles.

2. Select:

  • ADD

  • Add Profile.

3. Select Apple iOS and Device Profile

4. Configure the profile's General settings. These settings determine how the profile is deployed and who receives it and should be set according to your organization's needs. Some recommendations include:

  • Name: descriptive name for the policy such as APN Profile

  • Description: optional description

  • Deployment: either Managed or Manual according to your general management approach Profile

  • Scope: Production or Both if you wish to test the profile in staging

  • Assignment Type: Auto

  • Allow Removal: as per management approach. Suggest With Authorization Managed By: as per administrative approach

  • Assigned Groups: Set to the groups you wish to receive this profile. In this case, we assume that the devices that should receive the APN Profile setting are grouped via a standard or smart group

  • Exclusions: as per preferred deployment approach

  • Install only on devices inside selected areas: unchecked

  • Enable Scheduling and install only during selected periods: unchecked Removal Date: unset.

5. Choose a Cellular Payload and select Configure.

6. Under the APN list (leaving the Attach APN fields blank) configure the APN as follows:

  • APN: asavie.net

  • User Name: mdm

  • Password: mdm

  • Authentication Type: PAP

Leave all other areas empty.

Select Save & Publish.

VMware Workspace ONE UEM will now apply this profile to the identified devices at the next available opportunity.

Source: https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-Workspace-ONE-UEM/2011/iOS_Platform/GUID-AWT-PROFILECELLULAR.html

4723031.title: Cookie Statement
4723031.body:
COOKIE STATEMENT

Asavie (“us“, “we” or “our“) uses “cookies” to help us remember details of your visits to the website as part of the service and your Account including, but not limited to technical information about your visit e.g. traffic data, location data, browser language, your IP address, the previous website from which you reached us and the type of browser you use, domain, number and time of visits. Accordingly, our Privacy Statement will apply to our treatment of the information we obtain via our cookies. A cookie is a small data file stored by the portal on your device (e.g. your phone or your computer) which collects non-Personal Identifiable Information and let you navigate the service effectively.

TYPES OF COOKIES USED ON ACCESSMYLAN SERVICE

Provider

Name

Purpose

Expiry

Google

_gid

Used to distinguish users.

24 hours

Google

_ga

Used to distinguish users

2 years

Google

_gat

Used to throttle request rate

10 minutes

Intercom

intercom-id-[app_id]

Unique anonymous identifier

9 months

Intercom

intercom-session-[app_id]

Keeping track of sessions

1 week

HOW TO MANAGE COOKIES

The service makes use of cookies to support analysis and understanding of how people interact with the portal. We perform this analysis to help us improve the interface and effectiveness of the service. Asavie uses cookies that are persistent-based. Persistent cookies remain on your computer after you close your browser or turn off your computer. The data collection and reporting behind this analysis is currently provided by Google Analytics.

Do not block localStorage on your browser as it would prevent you from logging in to the portal.

CHANGES TO THIS COOKIES STATEMENT

We can change this Cookies Statement at any time. It’s important that you review the changed Cookies Statement. If you do not wish to agree to the changed Cookies Statement, it will have no impact on your ability to use the portal or the service.

Asavie uses cookies that are session-based and persistent-based. Session cookies exist only during one session. The data collection and reporting behind this analysis is currently provided by Google Analytics and Intercom.
