4729280.title: Deployment assistance for Verizon devices: MiFi, Jetpack, Orbic
4729280.description: Steps to follow if a hotspot device does not OTA (Over the Air) connect.
4729280.body:
When a device cannot access the internet and shows uncontrolled on the Asavie portal after an OTA (Over The Air) deployment, the first power cycle the device OFF/ON.

Follow these troubleshooting steps if the device is still unable to connect:

1. Verify the device phone number matches what is listed under Devices > Devices Overview in the Asavie Portal.

2. Reset the device back to factory settings. Use a paperclip to press the RESET button for 15 seconds or until '...resetting' appears on display. Check connectivity.

Note: The RESET button may be under the battery cover, located down inside the hole marked RESET. Further help to reset specific device types: Verizon Device Support

3. Confirm the device firmware is up to date by following the manufacturer's instructions. Verizon Device Support

4. Toggle (remove and re-add) the Asavie Private-Dynamic IP feature by logging into your Verizon MyBusiness portal. Contact your Verizon representative if your Verizon customer portal is not accessible.

5. Turn the device OFF, wait 60 seconds, turn it back ON. Allow up to 10 minutes for the device to enroll before verifying the device has internet access and is in a “controlled” status in your Asavie Portal.

Should it be needed, additional assistance is available. Call WEHD (Tier 2 Verizon Support) at 800-525-0481.

Note:

  • When adding the Private Dynamic IP feature, Verizon will queue out the APN Over-The-Air (OTA) for 72 hours to any OTA-capable device like Android devices or Jetpacks (iOS devices are not OTA compatible. See this page for iOS deployment).

  • If a device has not been switched on within 72 hours, it may require the Private Dynamic IP feature to be removed and re-added to queue out the APN OTA for an additional 72 hours.

  • Seventy-two hours APN queue is only applicable to devices that were already active on the Verizon network; new SIM activations are not limited to this time window.

  • If your device fails to connect after adding the Private Dynamic IP feature, please contact WEHD on 800-525-0481 for further assistance.

4732990.title: How to make actions with Devices Overview
4732990.body:
Devices signed to the service can be viewed from the "Device Overview" tab. Admins can monitor the device status to ensure devices are enrolled and connected to the service. The option to rename a device is available.

Enable Device

The bulk action feature allows you to enable many devices simultaneously. When enabled, a device can consume data and access the Internet. This may be useful if you wish to enable the Internet on all of the devices in a Group.

Disable Device

The bulk action feature allows you to disable many devices simultaneously. When disabled, the device ceases to consume data or access the Internet. This may be useful if you wish to disable the Internet on all of the devices in a Group.

Deregister app

The bulk action feature allows you to deregister the app on multiple devices simultaneously.

Sending SMS Invitation

Send bulk SMS to users who are yet to enroll in the service. Until a device is configured to the service, its data usage and policy are not controlled by the administrator.

Note: All actions are only permissible once the device is configured to the service. The administrator does not control its data usage and policy.

How to send a bulk SMS Invitation

To make a bulk action on your account, navigate to Devices, click on Devices Overview.

  1. Select your users.

  2. Tap on the icon "Choose Action." This will enable once a user is selected.

  3. Choose the action you wish to implement from the drop-down.

  4. Apply and Send.

4771674.title: Office VPN Mobile Apps (EOL)
4771674.description: Download iOS app or Android .apk for installation
4771674.body:
Office VPN is an access method to the platform relying on VPN clients/apps.

Important: Office VPN for iOS and Android are being retired by end of February 2022.

The Office VPN for iOS is available from the Apple App Store.

Download iOS App

The Office VPN for Android is not available on the Google Playstore anymore, but you can still download it from here and install it on your Android device.

Download Android App (.apk)

How to install an APK on your device:

  1. Download the Android APK.

  2. You can either navigate to your Download folder using a file browser app or begin the install by clicking on the completed download in your mobile browser.

  3. Android will ask you to grant permission to either the file browser or your web browser to install the app. Grant the permission, and it should bounce you back to the installation screen. If not, navigate back to your Download folder after granting permission to try again.

  4. The app will safely install.

Note: You will need to trust installation from unknown sources.

4811060.title: ActiveSync (Reverse Proxy)
4811060.body:
What is ActiveSync

The Exchange ActiveSync (EAS) protocol was developed by Microsoft in the 1990’s to allow mobile devices to synchronize Mail, Calendar, Contacts with either a desktop PC or a server running a compatible software product.

Our ActiveSync service (sometimes referred to as the ActiveSync reverse proxy) access method is an optional feature provided by the platform to allow secure connectivity from mobile devices to an on-premise Exchange Serve for the purposes of Email, Calendar, Contacts sync, using the EAS protocol.

Microsoft Office support for ActiveSync

Microsoft have been in the process of deprecating (removing) support for the authentication protocol that EAS (and hence the ActiveSync (Reverse Proxy)) relies on for several years, as more secure authentication protocols are now widely available. Also, more and more enterprises are migrating away from on premise Exchange servers to cloud-based options such as Office365.

Support for this variant of the ActiveSync protocol is now being removed from the latest email clients (e.g. Windows 10 Mail). Unfortunately, it is not possible for our ActiveSync service to be updated to support newer versions of the protocol. Some customers using some newer email client versions may start to see issues connecting to their on-premise Exchange Server using our ActiveSync service.

What workarounds are possible

If users encounter issues with the ActiveSync (Reverse Proxy) service on particular email clients, we recommend trying an alternative client if possible. We cannot give any more specific advice than this due to the huge variety of potential email clients on the market.

Recommendation

We recommend that customers immediately plan to migrate away from reliance on the ActiveSync (Reverse Proxy) secure connectivity solution.

ActiveSync (Reverse Proxy) is being retired by end of February 2022.

4835768.title: How to reset 2FA phone number
4835768.description: Change the phone number used for 2FA authentication
4835768.body:
As part of the registration process a phone number is required, and is utilized for 2FA for any latter login. This phone number is provided by the user as part of the registration flow in the portal.

If the phone number of a user needs to be changed there are 2 options:

  • Another admin user with enough permissions can delete the user from all accounts it is assigned to and create it again. As part of the new registration the phone number is requested again.

  • If deleting the user is not feasible, then you can contact support and request the change. You will find the support contact details in the top right menu of the portal.

4860488.title: Can I change the IP range of a Network or a Device?
4860488.description: How to expand, reduce or change the custom IP range of a Network
4860488.body:
When creating a Network a custom IP range is specified. Each Device Interface that belongs to the Network will get a private static IP that will be reachable from the private corporate network where the Network Connector is installed. The IP addresses for each device interface is assigned on a first-come, first-served basis from the IPs available in the custom range.

From time to time, there might be a need to use a different IP range for the devices. It could be because:

  • You have exhausted the IP addresses available in the current IP range and you need to expand it

  • You want to adjust the size of the range to your use case and you want to reduce it

  • You want to avoid overlap with some other subnet and you want to modify it

  • You want to change the IP address of a specific device interface

For any of these situations you can always create a new Network via the self-serve portal or API, specifying the new IP range that you need and move your devices to the new Network.

If you want to modify the IP range for an existing Network and don't want to create a new one:

  • Expand: you can expand the size of a Network self-serve via the portal or the API. There is a maximum size of /18 for a Network (16,384 IPs). If you need to go above this you can contact support

  • Reduce or modify: these actions can create a number of issues on connected devices so they are not available via self-serve portal or API. If you want to reduce or modify the IP range you can contact support

It is currently not possible to change the IP address assigned to a specific device. However, you can add and enable your devices in the order you want the IP addresses to be assigned.

NOTE: It might take several hours for a device interface to be assigned the new IP address. We recommend that you disconnect and reconnect your device from the cellular network (e.g. by going to flight mode if available) or reboot your device to force the new IP to be assigned.

More information about IP address management

4900479.title: Using a speed test
4900479.description: Test and assess the speed of the internet according to these recommendations
4900479.body:
It is a familiar refrain and even more common in these remote working and remote learning days that everyone is experiencing a slower Internet connection. This article will explain Internet speed, how to test your speed, common issues, and who to contact in case you experience poor internet speeds.

When accessing the internet from your Wi-Fi access point in your home on your laptop or a mobile device (such as a smartphone), the path taken by your device to the internet is different and can experience other congestion points that can slow the experience. The fixed internet connection typically takes a much shorter path as compared to your mobile service provider. Also, the mobile service is a shared access service, so when there are many users on the same mobile cell site, all users will experience congestion, as you will experience at a stadium or a concert.

For these reasons, it is normal for a mobile 4G service to be slower than a fixed/Wi-Fi connection. The most common issues affecting a mobile service connection speed include:

  • Radio congestion has too many users accessing the same mobile cell tower.

  • Poor cellular coverage in the location of accessing the mobile service.

  • Background applications running on the mobile service are consuming the internet bandwidth. For example, you may have malware on your device.

  • Your provider may be throttling (slowing down) your connection due to a fair use policy to protect the service for all users in case of congestion or overuse.

How does this service work on my mobile connection, and does it slow down the connection?

This service works on a standard mobile internet connection. It is powered using advanced networking technologies to offer a secure slice of the mobile internet to businesses or organizations like schools, health services, etc.

The service's primary access method is via Private APN (Access Point Name), acting as a gateway between your mobile device and the internet. A mobile device requires an APN to access the Internet. Your device comes pre-configured with a general internet APN overridden at setup by our service Private APN. Private APNs, although carefully optimized, will always have a small impact on the internet connection speed.

We are continuously monitoring our service and optimizing the experience to ensure any congestion is minimized. However, connection speeds can arise from time to time as providing a secure internet connection means all traffic origins and destinations must be analyzed and secure policies applied.

How to check your mobile data speed?

There are many tools on the internet to check the internet speed of your internet connection.

Here are some of the best internet speed tools available:

https://www.speedtest.net/

https://speedof.me/

https://testmy.net/

http://internethealthtest.org/

https://speedsmart.net/

Make sure you are on mobile data enabled, the private APN is on, and your Wi-Fi is disabled first before doing the test.

If the speed results seem inaccurate, we recommend you verify various factors:

  • Every speed test application will give you different results as their servers are not located at the same place. Therefore, it would help if you tried out various applications for comparison.

  • The application test servers might not perform equally well, and servers closer to your location will provide better results. Therefore, make sure you try several servers of the same application for comparison.

  • Browsers are not recommended for running speed tests. Recommend dedicated speed test applications are preferred.

Who to contact if you are still concerned about internet speed?

Suppose you are still unhappy with your speed. In that case, we recommend that you contact your mobile service provider, and they will tell you whether there is good coverage at your location or another issue that may be impacting your internet speed.

4942769.title: What is IoTFlex
4942769.body:
Optus IoTFlex is a self-service secure private network and policy platform for IoT solutions. The private network protects cellular IoT gateways and devices by eliminating exposure to cyberattacks on the public internet, keeping IoT deployments safe from day one.

IoTFlex service allows IoT endpoints on a cellular network to be part of a private network and get controlled and secured access to the internet. It will keep your devices secured and enforce compliance, give visibility and control with a scalable solution and minimal effort, with no software installation required on the device.

The main capabilities are managed in the different sections of the portal:

  • Networks: assign a static IP to your devices, attach a private Network Connector if you need private connectivity, and even customize your routing for private and internet access.

  • Policies, Groups and Zones: define specific Access Policy rules such as IP ACLs, DNS whitelists, and even cap max Data Speed. You can have different Policies assigned per Group of devices within the same Network. You can use Zones to define different rules depending on where they are roaming from.

  • Devices: manage the list of all your devices with real-time status, static IP and metadata associated. Your devices are automatically synced every few minutes from your Cisco IoT Center account.

  • Reports: get visibility of your historical usage, per network, per group or per device and get insights to customize your policies or optimize your configuration.

  • Account and users: add other portal or API Users with different system or custom roles, generate API Credentials or configure your Account and Profile Settings.

4946117.title: Account creation and sync with Cisco IoT (Jasper) Control Center integration
4946117.description: Create an account that automatically synchronizes devices with Cisco IoT Control Center
4946117.body:
Accounts are created from the channel management by a tenant admin. If Cisco IoT Control Center integration is configured, the account will retrieve and provision devices via API. This requires Cisco IoT CC API details to be provided when the account is created.

Pre-requisites

  • A working Cisco IoT CC account

  • A user linked to the Cisco IoT CC account with the following requirements:
    - API access
    - API credentials generated (username and API key)
    - Role assigned with permission to "Search Devices" and "Get Device Details"

Account creation

When a new account is created, the following information is required:

  • Organization Name: Name of the customer's organization. It can be modified at any time.

  • Admin email: email of the first account admin user. A welcome registration email will be sent to the user. Users can be created, updated, and deleted after the account is created.

  • Region: the region where data is kept at rest

  • Bundle: 4 options for the four license tiers. The feature set is the same for the four license tiers, but a different data allowance is included in each tier. Data allowance is not enforced by the service policies but utilized for billing purposes

  • Cisco IoT Control Center details
    - URL: URL for Cisco IoT Control Center API. It will be pre populated, and usually, there will be no need to modify it
    - Username: username from the Cisco IoT user created for device provisioning
    - API key: API key from the Cisco IoT CC user created for device provisioning
    - Condition: optional field. The condition will filter which devices from Cisco IoT CC will be retrieved and provisioned into this specific account. E.g. if "customer:acme" is provided then only devices that belong to that specific customer will be provisioned and the rest will be ignored. This would allow for one Cisco IoT CC to be mapped to multiple service accounts.

On account creation, the service tries to make the first connection to Cisco IoT Control Center. If the API credentials are incorrect or there is some other problem (e.g. insufficient permissions), it will raise an error, and the account creation will fail.

After the creation, the service will synchronize every few minutes with Cisco IoT CC to retrieve changed or added devices and will update or provision them accordingly. The synchronization mechanism will also retrieve the Cisco IoT CC account id and will store it as the "External Id" of the newly created account.

Tenant admin responsibilities

It is the responsibility of the tenant admin creating the account to guarantee that:

  • Each device is provisioned only to one account. I.e. the same Cisco IoT Control Center account cannot be mapped to more than one service account unless the "condition" parameter identifies which devices are provisioned in each one.

  • The account is created under the right tenant in the hierarchy.

  • The appropriate bundle is selected for billing purposes.

  • Cisco IoT Control Center details are correct, and API Key is not compromised.

Synchronization errors

If at some point there is an error in the sync process the account will be flagged as "Error". This error can be due for example to:

  • Invalid API credentials (e.g. they have been removed or reset on Cisco IoT CC)

  • The process is trying to provision devices that already exist in another account. This can happen if the same Cisco IoT CC API credentials are utilized for 2 different accounts. The duplicate devices will not be provisioned.

In any of these scenarios the admin will have to provide new API credentials to enable the synchronization again.

4949092.title: API security
4949092.description: Security in API authentication and usage
4949092.body:
Users can generate API credentials to access the service and utilize platform capabilities. API access allows:

  • Integration with 3rd party systems

  • Create automation of specific tasks like retrieving reports, status, creation or updates on resources, etc.

API credentials

API credentials consist of a client id and client secret and are generated and retrieved from the portal. API credentials always belong to a user. Therefore for API credentials:

  • The role assigned to the user is applicable, and the same role-based access controls and related permissions are enforced. Therefore, admin users have granular controls for the level of access granted via API.

  • The client id is a UUID, and the client secret is a 32 bytes random key Base64 encoded.

  • API client secret can be retrieved only when it is first created. After that, it is stored encrypted with a one-way encryption, which cannot be recovered again in clear again.

  • More than one set of API credentials can be created for any user, allowing easy rotation of credentials as needed.

  • It is the user's responsibility to retrieve and securely store the API credentials.

Authentication

To use the API, authentication is required, using OAuth2 protocol. A successful authentication returns an access token that grants access to the API.

  • Authentication is always over HTTP, using state-of-the-art TLS encryption, ensuring the confidentiality of the API credentials and the token.

  • The access token is valid for one h. After this period, a new token must be retrieved

API usage

Once authentication is completed, API endpoints are accessible using the access token. This access is restricted by the role and permissions of the corresponding user.

  • API endpoints are available only over HTTP, using state-of-the-art TLS encryption.

  • Throttling controls are applied to all API endpoints.

Auditing and access logs

All API usage is logged. This includes authentication and usage of any other API endpoint. API activity logs allow tracking when a user has used any resource of the platform and the action that has been executed.

4985349.title: Getting Started
4985349.description: New to the platform? This guide will help you get going and answer some frequently asked questions
4985349.body:
So you've just signed up for the service, but what next? This guide is designed to help you get the most out of our platform, and avoid any unexpected issues. So let's get going.

In case you're not familiar with what we do already, our service revolves around three key principles:

  • Simple: We want everything to be as simple as possible, we reduce noise and provide explanations of why the problems we identify could lead to a breach.

  • Proactive: When new vulnerabilities are discovered we will run proactive emerging threat scans on your systems, looking for those new weaknesses. Where you are affected we will notify you by email and Slack (Pro/Verified plans only).

  • Perimeter-specific: We've designed our platform to highlight issues that are only important in the context of being exposed to the internet. This includes things like databases, but there are also hundreds of other examples.

Add your targets 🎯

Manually add targets

Assuming you've already managed to log in, the first thing you'll want to do is tell us where your systems are. You can do that on the targets page. You can also keep your targets organized by adding tags.

Add from cloud providers

Connect your AWS, Google Cloud or Microsoft Azure accounts to synchronize your external IP addresses and DNS hostnames with the Intruder portal. We monitor your cloud accounts and notify you whenever a cloud IP address or DNS hostname is released, to ensure you never scan any systems that you no longer own. You can also activate CloudBot to automatically add any new external IP addresses or hostnames in your cloud accounts as Intruder targets.

Other FAQs 🧐

Check out our help pages on what our scans test you for, what scanning engine we use, how to add additional users, and how the licensing works.

How to contact us 👋

We're always available in the chat bubble in the corner, and we love feedback, so if you've got any questions, or ideas on how we could improve, please do get in touch!

4999120.title: How can a device be deleted or moved to a different account?
4999120.description: Delete devices or move them between accounts with Cisco IoT CC
4999120.body:
Devices are provisioned via an automated API synchronization process from the linked Cisco IoT Control Center account.

If a device needs to be moved or deleted then you have to delete it or move it from the corresponding Cisco IoT Control Center account following these steps:

  1. Delete the device from the portal. This can be done by:
    - Changing the life cycle status of the device in Cisco IoT CC to "Purged" and waiting a few minutes to trigger the deletion
    - Or by contacting support and asking for the deletion

  2. If you want the device to be in a different account then move the device to the new account on Cisco IoT CC and change the status to the one you need it to be

  3. Wait a few minutes for the device to be provisioned to the new account

If the destination account the device is being moved to doesn’t exist yet it will have to be created first following the standard process.

NOTE: Make sure that each device is provisioned only in one account. Otherwise the process will fail and the device will not be able to connect.

5020455.title: How to block Tethering on iOS/iPadOS devices
5020455.body:
• By applying VOLVOW on a line, you make it work with only Private APNs like ours. The service code VOLVOW must be applied by O2 to the SIM, which can take up to 24 hours, but most likely 1-2 hours as the updates run to a 2 hour schedule Once VOLVOW is applied this SIM cannot use any APN to connect to the internet except a private one, in our case ours, as this is also applied to the SIM.

  • Tethering on iOS will be blocked as soon as this code is applied (as the Personal Hotspot APN is configured to idata.o2.co.uk)

  • To enable Tethering back, you can set our APN manually on the device.

  1. The device must be iOS 13.4 or higher (before the Personal Hotspot APN is not accessible on the device).

  2. The device must have the Personal Hotspot APN changed from idata.o2.co.uk to vpn.amylan.

5023838.title: What browsers are supported
5023838.description: Chrome, Firefox, Safari and Microsoft Edge
5023838.body:
We design our products to support the latest web browsers. We support the current and last major versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Microsoft Edge.

As of June 15th, 2021, we'll no longer support Internet Explorer 11 (or below) and your experience may be suboptimal on this browser. We recommend moving to one of the above browsers as soon as possible for the best experience.

5025043.title: Privacy Policy
5025043.body:
As required by Google policy, we do not share any data collected by our service with any third parties for any reason.

DATA COLLECTION

For devices using the Application we may collect the following Data, which may include personal data:

  • Device number (MSISDN);

  • Time and date of use of device when using mobile data or Wi-Fi;

  • Amount of data sent and received;

  • Location of access and location of device (also collected in the background);

  • Websites accessed and the type of website accessed;

  • Website access attempts blocked, and the type of website access attempt blocked due to policy enforcement actions.

Where the Application is used as the mobile device manager, the following information is collected:

  • General Device information;

  • Information about Device Security settings;

  • List of Installed Applications.

DEVICE MANAGEMENT

For managed devices (i.e., devices where the Application is used as the mobile device manager), then the Application may configure a password policy for the device, lock the device and initiate a remote wipe.

USE OF YOUR DATA

We may use your Data to:

  • ensure that content from the Site and/or the Application is presented in the most effective, responsive and compatible way for you and your computer or device;

  • improve our services and products provided by us to you;

  • carry out our obligations arising from any contracts entered between you and us.

VPN AND MDM USE IN MOBILE APPS

When you use our Mobile Apps, we do not share your Data with third parties except as necessary to comply with applicable law or legal process.

5030432.title: What is an APN?
5030432.body:
An Access Point Name is the name of a gateway between your mobile device and the Internet.

A mobile device requires an APN in order to access the Internet. Your device comes preconfigured with a general internet APN.

Important: You must change the APN for your device in order to use the service and control your mobile data costs.

For more information, see how to configure devices here.

5061151.title: Why data usage report show different totals?
5061151.body:
The Data usage report is combining two data measures.

They do not refresh at the same rate, does not share the same purpose and might often be in contradiction with each other.

  1. The upper part of the report is focused on accurate data usage count, and is meant to line with the policy enforcement rule you have set. It refreshes less often to line up closer with the carrier data usage count. This is the same data count used for Quota policy enforcement such as Cut-Off, and Restricted Policies (Enforcement of quota controls are done in real-time).

  2. The lower part (granular report of categories and websites) are refreshed more often for convenience and helping make sense on how your devices are being used. We have optimized it to refresh every 15 minutes. This is a real added value of the report, providing you with high level of traffic visibility.

See How real-time are the Reports?

5099154.title: Cisco IoT device attributes
5099154.description: Mapping of device attributes with Cisco IoT Control Center (Jasper CC)
5099154.body:
When an account is created and Cisco IoT Control Center API credentials are provided, devices are automatically provisioned to your account from Cisco IoT CC. Besides, when there is any change on Cisco IoT the same automated process will reflect the changes in your account.

Attributes are not the same and not all are required to be synchronized. Therefore, the following table covers the mapping of attributes from Cisco IoT Control Center to your account:

Cisco IoT CC field

Device field

DeviceID

Name

IMSI

Interface Number

ICCID

Interface ICCID

MSISDN

Phone Number

Status

Interface Enabled / Disabled (see below)

On the SIM status value from Cisco IoT CC, the device will not reflect all the possible states. Instead, it is mapped to the enabled/disabled attribute of the device interface reflecting if it can establish a data connection:

Cisco IoT CC life cycle status

Interface

Test Ready

Activation Ready

Activated

Enabled

Inventory

Deactivated

Retired

Replaced

5165530.title: Push the iOS AML mobile app with Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune)
5165530.description: Streamline the deployment of the iOS/iPadOS mobile app for your end users
5165530.body:
The App Push deployment for iOS allows to shorten the user on-boarding process by enabling auto-registration.

Requirements

  • Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune)

  • UEM Connect set up (instructions here)

In your Intune Portal.

Add the iOS store app

Go to Client Apps>Apps

Click on + Add

  1. Under App Type, select iOS store app

  2. Click on Select

Under App information, click on the link Search the App Store

  1. Search for AccessMyLAN, and select the AML app

  2. Click on Select

  3. Adjust the App information and then click on Next

Under Assignments, do not assign this app yet, click on Next

Under Review + create, select Create

Add and Assign the iOS app configuration

Go to Client Apps>App Configuration policies

Click on + Add and Managed devices

Under Basics

  1. Give a Name (e.g. AccessMyLAN iOS)

  2. Select the iOS/iPadOS platform

  3. Click on Select App and add the AccessMyLAN iOS store app, click OK

  4. Click on Next

Under Settings, choose in the drop-down Use Configuration designer

  1. Configuration key is device_id

  2. Value Type is String

  3. Configuration Value is #{{deviceid}}

  4. Click on Next

Under Assignments, define the groups you want to select, then click on Next

Under Review + create, click on Create

Assign the iOS store app

Go back to Client Apps>Apps

Select the AccessMyLAN iOS store app from the list

5187424.title: Do passwords expire?
5187424.description: Do passwords expire after a certain time? Can they be reused?
5187424.body:
The context for password management

Microsoft Azure B2C is being used to manage portal user identities, leveraging authentication capabilities, and providing security and protection.

In relation to password management, the focus is on enforcing strong password policies. The policy is for a minimum of 14 characters with a combination of upper/lower case, numbers, and symbols, with commonly used, weak, or compromised passwords not being allowed.

Protection against brute-force and denial of service attacks is in place by Microsoft, using various signals to intelligently differentiate intended users from hackers and botnets. A smart account lockout capability is enforced based on the IP of the request, the password entered, and other factors, with a duration that depends on the risk identified and the number of attempts. Activity is logged to understand platform activity and diagnose issues.

Password expiration

Password expiration is not enforced and users are not notified. Password expiration is a mechanism for mitigation of the threat of password duplication/theft. The approach from Microsoft Azure B2C authentication to mitigate this risk is to focus on 2FA, enforcement of strong and complex passwords, and detection and banning of common/compromised passwords. This also follows NIST requirements, where password expiration is not considered something that improves security.

Password reuse

Following the same approach, password reuse is allowed. Microsoft Azure AD detects and blocks known weak passwords and their variations. This applies at registration time and also on any subsequent password reset. The list of banned passwords is constantly analyzed by Azure AD Identity Protection team.

References

  • https://docs.google.com/document/d/1vjWSCv2Bt_G_XBvr55zODMvjyb2u1SPoPoH5oaa4rrI/edit?usp=sharing

  • https://help.securemobi.net/en/articles/4604799-web-portal-authentication-security

  • https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory-b2c/technical-overview#protect-customer-identities

  • https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/authentication/concept-password-ban-bad

  • https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory-b2c/threat-management

  • https://pages.nist.gov/800-63-3/sp800-63b.html#sec5

  • https://pages.nist.gov/800-63-FAQ/#q-b05

5207149.title: What is the SIA BizDev channel?
5207149.body:
It is a channel tenant in the SIA Product live environment for Business Development and Sales Enablement activities.

Owned by Asavie Product + Sales Engineering teams, it supports all the latest capabilities and allows demos of the different products with all the available APNs and canned emulated data.

5207154.title: How do I get access to the SPS Secure BizDev channel?
5207154.description: How to get access to the channel?
5207154.body:
If you are reading this you already have an account.

For anyone else that doesn't have an account any tenant manager in the channel can add a new user. He/she will receive a welcome email to access the portal and APIs after registration.

We recommend to go through the product collaterals to get familiar with the product, and check the articles with the Digital Assistant (Mollie)

5207159.title: Can I run a demo with real SIMs?
5207159.body: Yes. In the BizDev channel you can run demos with real SIMs from the set of available APNs. APNs from the same region can also be combined into the same account and network

5207161.title: Can new sub-tenants and accounts be created? And deleted?
5207161.body:
Yes, accounts and tenants can be created from the self-service portal/APIs.

Accounts and tenants can also be deleted. However better be careful and agree on ownership of tenants and accounts and check with the owner before deleting. Also, top level tenants have custom branding configuration that would be lost if they are deleted

A few good practices to follow to avoid conflicts or issues in the channel:

  • You can create your own tenants, accounts and users under the corresponding branch of the tenant hierarchy. You can modify and delete the tenants and accounts you create as needed

  • Don’t delete tenants you haven’t created

  • Minimize the number of accounts and tenants created to keep it manageable

  • When adding new users be aware of the scope, visibility and permissions they would be getting. Whenever possible add them with the lowest privilege possible:

    • At a lowest tenant / account instead of at the top level

    • With a more restricted role

    • To add users to channel tenant or 2 levels below contact product management

  • Accounts, tenants and users will be cleaned as needed

5207524.title: Can I add new admin users?
5207524.body:
Yes, from the self-service portal/APIs, you can add new users and assign them a role.

When adding new users be aware of the scope, visibility and permissions they would be getting. Whenever possible add them with the lowest privilege possible:

  • At a lowest tenant / account instead of at the top level

  • With a more restricted role

  • To add users to channel tenant or 2 levels below contact product management

5207534.title: How can I have an account with emulated data?
5207534.description: Create accounts with pre-canned data to run demos
5207534.body:
In the BizDev channel there is a top level tenant setup for emulated data, with subtenants configured for different brands.

You can create accounts underneath any of them that will be populated with pre-canned data. You can add users to these accounts, and they will be able to login with their own credentials

5207537.title: Can all the APNs be utilized?
5207537.body: There is a list of APNs certified for the service with live SIMs which can be utilized from the different regions. Contact Product Management to get the list of APNs available or to request a new APN to be provisioned.

5207549.title: Can the config of a tenant be changed?
5207549.body:
Yes. Some attributes can be updated via the UI.

Available APNs, bundles, regions and branding are either “managed” (not self-service) or API only and can be updated to target specific demo scenarios.

Check the API documentation or contact product management if you have any request.

5207565.title: Is there billing in the channel?
5207565.body:
No. Channel is designed for business development activities. It can run demos and POCs as long as there is no billing. It is not a channel for direct customer engagements.

An demo / POC account in BizDev channel would have to be recreated in a different channel following the corresponding ordering / provisioning process in order to have billing.

5207570.title: Is there support in the channel?
5207570.body: Support is provided via product management. BizDev channel tenant doesn’t offer any formal SLA but is configured in the production environment expected to stay stable to enable the different BizDev activities.

5207592.title: How can I give feedback about the product?
5207592.body:
Feedback and dogfooding programs are in place.

We are interested in all feedback: features, general usability, configuration and modularity, default initial configuration on accounts, demo-ability (with the emulated data that is shown).

If you want to join please contact product management.

5207602.title: How many accounts or devices can I add?
5207602.description: What is the capacity of the BizDev channel?
5207602.body:
The channel is designed for small size PoCs and a limited number of accounts. Large scale deployments or high usage use cases are not possible in the channel. Anything beyond this scale will impact the performance of the channel.

If you need extra capacity please contact product management to discuss the specific requirements.

5219998.title: My device is in Cisco IoT Control Center but doesn't appear in the portal
5219998.description: Resolve sync issues between Cisco IoT Control Center and the portal
5219998.body:
Devices are automatically synchronized from Cisco IoT Control Center to the portal. If a device is not being provisioned verify the following:

  • The device is in a valid life-cycle state. E.g. a the device in ACTIVATED status will always sync. You can learn more details here

  • After account creation only updated devices are synchronized. If a device is accidentally deleted from the portal you can
    - Make some change to the device in Cisco IoT CC (e.g. modify the deviceID, status or any other attribute). This will trigger the provisioning of the device again
    - Or start a manual sync (Full) to force the re-provisioning of all devices in the account

  • Make sure the device doesn't exist in a different account. If you want to move a device to a different account follow the steps explained here

  • Make sure the default Network has available IP addresses. If need you can expand the IP range of the Network. Learn more here

  • The account is properly syncing. If no devices are synchronized then there might be an account problem. This is usually due to wrong API credentials and can be fixed by providing valid credentials. You can read more about Cisco IoT Control center troubleshooting

5221315.title: Push the Moda APN with Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune)
5221315.description: Streamline the deployment of the iOS/iPadOS APN profile for your end users
5221315.body: Follow our dedicated deployment guide here

5224136.title: Windows 10 Deployment
5224136.description: Get your Asavie Moda devices connected via APN
5224136.body:
In order to configure and deploy APN settings on Windows 10, follow the Microsoft APN provisioning guide.

https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/configuration/provisioning-apn

APN Settings to configure:

  • APN: select the appropriate APN name here

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: PAP

  • MCC: select the appropriate field value here

  • MNC: select the appropriate field value here

  • APN Type: default.

5267817.title: Mobile Apps
5267817.body: Open this link on an iPhone, an iPad or an Android device to install the mobile app, or scan directly the email QR code.

5268351.title: Portal login URLs
5268351.description: Knowledge base articles for logged in users
5268351.body:
In order to access the knowledge base, you need to be logged in as a user of our portal.

  1. Log into your portal account

  2. Click on the Help Center link under the Question mark Icon to authenticate to the knowledge base

  3. Try accessing the article page again

Please select the appropriate portal to log into:

  • SIA Mobile

  • Optus IoTFlex

  • Asavie Moda EU

    • (demo only accounts here)

  • Asavie Moda US

    • (demo only accounts here)

  • AccessMyLAN or AccessMyLAN new UI

    • (demo only accounts here)

  • VDC

    • (demo only accounts here)

Note: if you still cannot access an article after following the above steps, please make sure the article URL is correct. It might also be that this content is not accessible for your portal.

You agree to comply with your obligations and Asavie agrees to comply with its respective obligations under Data Protection Legislation.

You acknowledge and agree that you are the data controller and Asavie is the data processor in respect of all personal data processed by Asavie on your behalf in accordance with this Agreement. You acknowledge that Asavie has no control over or responsibility for how you handle your personal data.

Asavie (and its affiliates) will only act on your instructions regarding the processing of personal data which you have provided to Asavie for the purposes of receiving and operating the Vodafone Data Control service. Asavie will comply with your instructions as to how you want Asavie to use such personal data, subject always to applicable laws.

Asavie will ensure that appropriate technical and organizational measures shall be taken against unauthorised or unlawful processing of the personal data and against accidental loss or destruction of, or damage to, the personal data. Although Asavie has undertaken these measures and met the requirements set out in Data Protection Legislation, Asavie cannot guarantee that your personal data is secure when it is sent or transferred by unsecured means.


Asavie will:

(a) comply with any request from you requiring Asavie to amend, transfer or delete the personal data as soon as possible;

(b) take all reasonable steps to ensure the reliability of any staff who may have access to personal data and ensure such staff are subject to appropriate obligations of confidentiality and at all times act in compliance with Data Protection Legislation and the obligations of this clause 5;

(c) implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to provide you with co-operation and assistance in complying with any data subject rights (including access requests) received by, or on behalf of, you;

(d) not transfer and/or disclose any personal data to any other party (including any other data processor, such as cloud computing service providers, or contractors) unless we have appropriate assurances from that third party that the personal data will only be processed in compliance with the Data Protection Legislation;

(e) not transfer any personal data to any country or territory outside the European Economic Area (EEA) or to any international organizations unless we have appropriate assurances that the personal data will only be processed in compliance with the Data Protection Legislation;

(f) permit you, or a third-party auditor acting under your direction, to conduct, at your cost, data privacy and security audits, assessments and inspections concerning Asavie’s data security and privacy procedures relating to the processing of personal data, its compliance with this clause 5 and Data Protection Legislation. You may, in your sole discretion, require Asavie to provide evidence of Asavie’s compliance with these procedures in lieu of conducting such an audit, assessment or inspection;

(g) notify you immediately in writing by sending an email to our upstream Data Processor or Controller if we become aware of any unauthorized or unlawful processing, disclosure of, or access to, personal data and/or any accidental or unlawful destruction of, loss of, alteration to, or corruption of personal data;

(h) notify you prior to adopting a new type of processing (including, without limitation, the use of new technology to continue current processing) in respect of the personal data, and at your request, Asavie shall participate in a data protection impact assessment in respect of the new type of processing which is being proposed, in accordance with Data Protection Legislation;

(i) on termination or expiry of the Agreement, for whatever reason, cease all use of the personal data and shall, at your election, either destroy all personal data or transfer all personal data to you or a nominated third party (in a mutually agreed format and by a mutually agreed method); and

(j) inform you immediately of any complaint, notice or similar communication we receive from any competent data protection or privacy authority in the United Kingdom and/or in which we process your personal data in connection with our provision to you of the services or your compliance with Data Protection Legislation. We shall provide such assistance as is reasonably required by you to enable you to respond to such complaints, notices or other similar communications and to comply with Data Protection Legislation. For the avoidance of doubt, we shall not respond to any such complaint, notice or other similar communication without your prior written consent.”

You acknowledge and agree that Asavie and its affiliates, (being any related entity, subsidiary or holding company of Asavie) is required to make use of data (which may include personal data) sent from end-users' devices and computers. You further acknowledge and agree that Asavie and its affiliates may also make use of this data to generate anonymous statistical data for product development purposes. Asavie and its affiliates may collect and process the following data in relation to the Vodafone Data Control service for:

For managed devices (i.e., devices where Vodafone Data Control is used as the mobile device manager), then Vodafone Data Control may collect the names of applications installed on the device. The application name data will only be collected if you enable the App Control feature in the Vodafone Data Control management portal. An optional email address and textual description can be configured for your devices. If provided, then Vodafone Data Control will collect and process this information. The email address is an option to invite a device to setup the service while the device description aids understanding of the reporting and alerting provided.

You acknowledge and agree that Asavie may sometimes disclose the above data to agents (being any person or entity authorised by Asavie to act on its behalf), resellers and/or services providers appointed by Asavie who provide services to Asavie in respect of the Vodafone Data Control service.

5291450.title: How do I get started with IoTFlex?
5291450.body:
To get started you need the following:

  • An Optus SIM card that is provisioned in your Cisco IoT Control Center account

  • An IoTFlex account

  • A device that supports a configurable APN (cellular gateway, router, MiFi, etc)

  • If you need private connectivity you need a server to install the Network Connector and admin rights/support to manage it

Once you have your account we recommend the following steps:

  1. Configure your Network

  2. Configure and connect your device

  3. Customize your policies

  4. Check the reports

5291897.title: What are the main IoT Flex service capabilities?
5291897.body:
IoT Flex service allows IoT endpoints on a cellular network to be part of a private network and get controlled and secured access to the internet. It will keep your devices secured and enforce compliance, give visibility and control with a scalable solution and minimal effort, with no software installation required on the device.

The main capabilities are managed in the different sections of the portal:

  • Networks: assign a static IP to your devices, attach a private Network Connector if you need private connectivity, and even customize your routing for private and internet access.

  • Policies, Groups and Zones: define specific Access Policy rules such as IP ACLs, DNS whitelists, and even cap max Data Speed. You can have different Policies assigned per Group of devices within the same Network. You can use Zones to define different rules depending on where they are roaming from.

  • Devices: manage the list of all your devices with real-time status, static IP and metadata associated. Your devices are automatically synced every few minutes from your Cisco IoT Center account.

  • Reports: get visibility of your historical usage, per network, per group or per device and get insights to customize your policies or optimize your configuration.

  • Account and users: add other portal or API Users with different system or custom roles, generate API Credentials or configure your Account and Profile Settings.

5342470.title: Optus IoTFlex channel
5342470.description: Description of the Optus IoT Flex channel configuration
5342470.body:
Optus is set up as a channel tenant configured for the IoT offering (aka Optus IoTFlex) that is deployed in the APAC Akamai managed service hub of the platform.

Optus IoTFlex service is created to be offered to customers in conjunction with Cisco IoT Control Center. I.e. certain Optus customers that are using Cisco IoT service are offered IoTFlex to manage the connectivity of their IoT devices. Accounts are created self-service from the UI/API and from there an integration with Cisco IoT Control Center for provisioning of licenses and devices. Therefore, every IoTFlex account is linked to a Cisco IoT CC account.

Optus IoTFlex is configured with 3 main subtenants:

  • Regular accounts: this tenant is created for all regular enterprise customers.

  • Sensitive accounts: this tenant is created for sensitive enterprise customers that where restricted access is needed

  • Emulated accounts: this tenant is configured for emulated data mode. All accounts created under this tenant will show pre canned emulated data for devices and reports, with the purpose of easy demoing the service.

With this tenant setup, users assigned to the regular accounts tenant will not have access to the sensitive accounts and vice versa, allowing segmentation of access to the channel.

Optus IoTFlex channel is configured to work with a dedicated Optus 3G/4G APN.

5358826.title: Dell Chromebook Deployment
5358826.description: Get your AML or FirstNet device connected via APN
5358826.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect policies created and data allowance set for a group plan. Additionally, you will need to change the Access Point Name (APN) associated with the device.

It is important to note that this is a step-by-step guide to help you set up and configure an APN on a Chromebook.

How to create an APN

Navigate to:

Click the time in the bottom right corner of your screen and select the following;

  • Settings

  • Networks

  • Mobile Network - Select the mobile network AT&T.

This will prompt the end-user to choose the option that corresponds to their cell phone. There is a field for the Access Point, where you will need to enter this information;

  • Access Point Names.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: hotspot.accessmylan.net

Click Save, and you’re ready to go.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

I deleted the Access Point Name (APN). How do I get it back?

First, make sure your Chromebook can connect to mobile networks and that your connection is on.

1. Restart your Chromebook.

2. Select the clock on the bottom right-hand corner

3. Select the Network icon

4. Make sure the AT&T network is turned on.

Next, ensure the AccessMyLAN Access Point Name (APN) is enabled:

1. Click on AT&T

2. If the Access point name box is empty, type: hotspot.accessmylan.net

3. Click Save, and you’re ready to go.

5369747.title: Windows Deployment
5369747.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect policies created and data allowance set for a group plan. In addition, you will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).
We support the following Window Phones:

  • Windows 10

  • Windows 8.1

  • Windows 8

  • Windows 7 & 7.5

How to create a new APN

Select the Network icon on the lower right corner of the taskbar, and then select the cellular network icon that appears in the list. Often, you’ll see the name of your mobile operator next to the cellular network icon.

  • To find this setting, select the Start button.

  • Go to Settings.

  • Tap: Network & Wireless

  • Cellular SIM

  • SIM Settings.

  • Scroll down and tap Add Internet APN.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: vpn.accessmylan.net.

  • If prompted, enter: Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: PAP (select normal if the type is not listed).

Tap the back button to Save

  • Scroll down, tap the Profile name created and Apply.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled.

Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. This option depends on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

Windows 8.1

On Windows Phone 8.1, you need to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • Scroll down and tap: Settings

  • Select Mobile + Sim

  • SIM Settings

  • Manual Internet APN set to on

  • Edit Internet APN Settings.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: vpn.accessmylan.net.

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: PAP (select normal if PAP is not listed).

Press Save.

The device will now appear as Connected. If it does not appear connected, re-enter APN details.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled. Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. This will depend on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

Windows Phone 8

On Windows Phone 8, you need to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • On Start, flick left to App list.

  • Select Settings

  • Add Internet APN

  • APN.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: vpn.accessmylan.net.

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: PAP (select normal if PAP is not listed).

Tap Save.

Note: When you see the word Activated, the APN is enabled. Test the connection on 3G/4G. If you have a dual SIM phone, in Cellular + SIM Settings, tap SIM 1 settings or SIM 2 settings. Depend on which SIM you’re using for your cellular data connection.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

Windows Phone 7 & 7.5

On Windows Phone 7 (and 7.5), you need to enter a new APN in the device Cellular Settings.

Follow these instructions on the Windows Phone (note that Microsoft documentation on adding an APN can be found here):

  • On Start, flick left to App list.

  • Select Settings

  • Add Internet APN

  • APN.

  • In the name Access Point Name (APN) field: vpn.accessmylan.net.

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: PAP (select normal if PAP is not listed).

Tap Done.


The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Device Overview tab in your portal.

5373864.title: How much data are my devices likely to use?
5373864.description: Get a reference of data consumption per device for different use cases
5373864.body:
Different types of devices will have different connectivity usage patterns. For example, mobile phones have other behavior, whether they are corporate devices or for personal use, and machines or IoT devices are entirely different as well, so it is difficult to say how much data your devices are likely to use.


Data usage is counted by the service in bytes and totaled across the entire account at the end of your billing cycle. Similarly, this usage can enforce the restriction or the cutoff limit set for a specific policy group. If you have no limit set, you will still be able to see how much data your devices are using. You can check these levels at any time by logging into your account and viewing the reports.

Please see the following examples to guide you in selecting your data usage limits:

Extremely low usage ( <= 1 MB)

In your project, you might be reading small data packets every few hours from a sensor, like the temperature from a refrigerator.

  • Smart meters

  • Street lighting

  • Smoke detectors

  • Home alarms

Very low usage (<=10MB)

Like the previous one, you need more frequent communication using small data packets every few minutes, e.g., a pump or motor speed.

  • Smart sensors

  • Health monitors & wearables

  • Asset tracking

  • Oil and gas telemetry

Low Usage (<=1GB)

Suppose your project involves sending and/or receiving rich telemetry/transaction data or text files or images. In that case, you will use much more data than the binary data used in the above examples.

  • Car telematics

  • POS devices

  • Energy management

  • Surveillance with still images

High usage (<=10GB)

Video, audio data, or regular browsing will bring your usage into a higher tier.

  • Security Cameras

  • Car infotainment

  • Baby Monitors

  • Digital signage

  • General browsing from corporate devices like smartphones and tablets

Please keep in mind these are reference estimations, and your project might be vastly different from these scenarios.

5388932.title: Nighthawk Mobile Hotspot Deployment
5388932.description: Get your AML device connected via APN
5388932.body:
Make sure you are connected to the Wi-Fi on the modem before proceeding.

  1. ​Launch a web browser and enter 192.168.1.1

  1. Enter the administrator login password. You may need to RESET the modem if you forgot by pressing the RESET button where the battery is located using a paperclip.

You are now on the Dashboard of your device.

  1. Select Settings > Advanced Settings > Cellular.

  1. Click the blue ADD button and complete the fields as follow:

  • APN name: Hotspot AML

  • APN: hotspot.accessmylan.net

  • PDP: IPV4

  • PDP Roaming: None

  • Authentication: None

Leave username and password blank, and click SAVE

  1. Click the round radio button on the left to activate the APN profile under Active. 

This APN is now the active. If you go back to the DASHBOARD, you should now see your device Connected in green.

5511260.title: Count and filter your Devices with Interface Summary 📈
5511260.description: The Device Interface Summary report shows the connection status for your devices
5511260.body:
Introducing our latest UI release, Device Interface Summary. To make it easier for you to identify the connectivity of your devices, we provided you with a new tab called Device Interface Summary.

The new tab within the Device Tab can help you monitor the activity on your configured device, allowing you to streamline the devices connected to the service and focus on those that are not.

🟢 Connected

  • This device is currently connected to this service via an active connection.

⚪ Disconnected

  • Currently, a device is not connected or had joined at one point to the service.

🟠 Never Connected

  • The device indicates that it has never been connected to the service in this state.

⚫ Pending

  • This state indicates that the device has been moved from a Group or a Network. The device will revert to a normal state after making the change.

🔵 Activating

  • In this state, the device is in the provisioning process. Once created, the device moves to one of its custom-defined states.

🔴 Error

  • This state indicates that an error has occurred in processing. Details of the error will be listed. This section will only be visible if an error has been shown.

As of November 5th, 2021, this feature has been released.

5559215.title: Device Interface Summary
5559215.description: The Device Interface Summary report shows the connection status for your devices
5559215.body:
Device Interface Summary makes it easier for you to identify the connectivity of your devices, we provided you with a new tab called Device Interface Summary.

The new tab within the Device Tab can help you monitor the activity on your configured device, allowing you to streamline the devices connected to the service and focus on those that are not.

🟢 Connected

  • This device is currently connected to this service via an active connection.

Disconnected

  • Currently, a device is not connected or had been connected at one point to the service.

🟠 Never Connected

  • In this state, the device indicates that it has never been connected to the service.

Pending

  • In this state, the device is in the provisioning process. Once created, the device moves to one of its custom-defined states.

5567731.title: Apple iCloud Private Relay
5567731.description: How does it work and why we block it
5567731.body:
Privacy is central to Apple’s value proposition, and on June 7th, 2021, they announced iCloud Private Relay.

iCloud Private Relay is a new service that prevents networks & servers from monitoring user activity across the internet. It is part of every iCloud+ subscription, offered as an opt-in beta option, and supported in iOS/iPadOS 15 from September 20th 2021 and macOS 12 later this fall.

How does iCloud Private Relay work?

iCloud Private Relay protects:

  • all web browsing in Safari,

  • all DNS name resolution queries,

  • a small subset of app traffic (e.g., unencrypted DNS queries made by apps)

For completeness, several other categories of traffic are not affected by iCloud Private Relay:

  • Connections apps makeover local networks or to private domain names

  • Apps that provide a network extension to add VPN or app-proxying capabilities and app traffic that uses the extensions

  • Traffic that uses a proxy is also exempt

To protect DNS name resolution, iCloud Private Relay uses Oblivious DNS-over-HTTPS (ODoH)

  • Users of iCloud Private Relay will bypass DNS resolvers assigned by their local network

Why and how do we disable iCloud Private Relay?

For security solutions like ours with valid monitoring use cases, Apple has documented a way to disable the service.

To disable Apple’s Private Relay, two hostnames need to be blocked with NXDOMAIN by the DNS resolver.

  • mask.icloud.com

  • mask-h2.icloud.com

Those blocks are applied to our DNS resolvers from now on, and the iCloud Private Relay cannot operate on our network. There is no opt-out option at the moment.

Source: https://developer.apple.com/support/prepare-your-network-for-icloud-private-relay/ See section “Allow for network traffic audits”

What is the user experience on iOS/iPadOS devices?

From iOS/iPadOS 15, users with iCloud+ paid subscription service have the ability to enable iCloud Private Relay beta.

We recommend that the feature stays disabled to enjoy our service.

If the feature is enabled, once the device has been configured to connect to our service, the user will immediately see a system prompt informing that the mobile data service does not support iCloud Private Relay. The user will then select "Use Without Private Relay" to keep using the mobile data. This removes Apple Private Relay for the internet provider, and our service will now apply normally.

5577646.title: Find any account, device or user under your tenant hierarchy 🔎
5577646.description: Manage your tenants by finding what you are looking for in seconds
5577646.body:
We recently made it easier to search for an account(s), devices, and users associated with your tenant hierarchy. You can search by many attributes such as email address, sim number, account name, IDs, and much more.

Never lose track of a particular device or user, and make the management of your tenant even simpler.

More details here

5584660.title: Secure Wi-Fi for your iOS/iPadOS and Samsung Knox devices 🛡
5584660.description: Wi-Fi Protect enforces Access Policies and secure untrusted networks
5584660.body:
Public Wi-Fi is available just about everywhere, from the local coffee shop to hotels, malls, and airports. Connecting your device on public Wi-Fi often poses a security threat to your device, making them vulnerable to criminal hackers.

The Wi-Fi Protect feature allows you to extend your network security and compliance policies to Wi-Fi, and provide full visibility, by installing a mobile app client on your devices.

You can also define a list of trusted networks where you don't want Wi-Fi Protect to apply, useful if you need to access local resources accessible on the network. (e.g. local email server, local hosted documentation etc.)

Note: Available for iOS 13.2+ and Android Samsung KNOX 2.0+ devices.

5584662.title: Connect users without MDM with simple invitation links sent via SMS or Email 📩
5584662.description: Help your users to set up on any device without an MDM
5584662.body:
We have introduced a friendly invitation feature to help you connect devices to the service without to rely on an MDM.

You can now send an SMS or Email invites with a link to help your users setup the service.

  • For iOS/iPadOS, users will be invited to install an APN profile in a few simple steps.

  • For Android and other device types, users will be guided on how to update their APN settings.

In addition, we are introducing a friendly mobile app that can simplify the onboarding process on iPhones and iPads, as well as Samsung Knox devices.

5584677.title: Track all activities with Audit Logs 📑
5584677.description: Audit logs helps you answer the questions of "who did what, where, and when?" within your tenant
5584677.body:
Audit logs are straightforward to understand. A behavior change usually occurs whenever a change is made to a system. This change would be documented in an audit log.

Audit Logs automatically record any activities or changes made by tenant users with accounts, such as creation, edition, or deletion of entities, user logins, and more.

Audit logs are stored for 90 days and can be browsed and filtered from the UI or fetched via the API.

When an incident occurs, using the audit log, you will gather as much information about it as quickly as possible and get a clear context of what happened.

The Audit Log feature with an account is located under Account Settings.

Click here to read more on Audit logs for Tenants.

As of November 5th, 2021, this feature has been released.

5584699.title: Monitor and configure your Cisco IoT Control Center integration from the portal 🧩
5584699.description: Check status, update configuration and run troubleshooting actions
5584699.body:
Cisco IoT Control Center Integration allows you to connect your account for provisioning. If Cisco IoT Control Center integration is configured, the account will retrieve and provision devices via API.

We are adding now an enhanced user interface to self-manage the integration configuration. This will allow better visibility of status and troubleshooting capabilities.

Besides, the provisioning mapping is allowing more flexibility in how devices are provisioned, e.g. an enterprise with a single Cisco IoT Control Center account can specify which devices are provisioned and map each individual customer to a separate service account.

More details here

5584715.title: Analytics API 📊
5584715.description: This document describes the Analytics API
5584715.body:
The Public Analytics APIs provides programmatic methods to access reporting data in your account. With the Public Analytics API, you can:

  • Build custom dashboards to visualize your account data.

  • Integrate your Secure Mobile account data with your own portal or third party BI tools

With the Public Analytics API you can access the following

  • Device activity summary and time series

  • Account and device-level data usage

  • Account and device-level blocked requests details

  • Top N data usage and blocks metrics

The detailed developer guide is available here

The API reference with its input parameters and response codes are available here

5588237.title: Policy Group
5588237.description: Group devices and assign mobile and access time policies
5588237.body:
To enhance your estate management within a Network, users can group devices to enforce consistent policies. Grouping can be completed per device type, organization departments, locations, solution, importance, carrier plans, and much more.

There are two types of policies:

  • Mobile Policies per Zone

  • Access Time Policy

For each Policy Group, you can define a mobile policy per zone, and even access times.

Mobile Policy

The mobile policy consists of multiple elements:

  • Access Policy (before restriction) is enforced by default. You can attach any access policy you have created, and the associated rules will apply.

  • The device restriction threshold can be defined to allow the rule swap to different access policies in real-time. When a device has consumed the distributed quota, the access policy will change. This is handy if you wish to offer a fair usage policy but protect the rest's device quota.

  • Create a per device cut-off that limits data consumption. This will disable mobile data in real-time when the device has reached its quota.

  • Finally, a restriction threshold per group can be defined. When the entire group reaches its shared quota, the group will see a policy change, resulting in a different access policy in real-time.

More details on Access Policies.

Access Time Policy

In addition, an access time policy can be defined for a user's Mobile Data per group. Decide the hours and days of the week, where mobile data is only allowed. Any other time and days will deny data access completely.

The following information can be used as a guide to aid you when applying an access time policy to a group:

  • You can allow internet connectivity from 8 am to 12 pm Monday to Saturday, then deny 1 pm to 5 pm, and finally, allow access on Sunday from 9 am to 11 am.

Whenever a device tries to connect to the internet outside of these, define hours, and the access time policy would prevent it.

More on how to create an Access Times Policy

5588248.title: Access Policy
5588248.description: Define a set of access rules to customize data traffic
5588248.body:
Access Policy allows you to define rules to restrict, when, and what endpoints devices can access using a different set of granular rules. You can create an access policy for a specific user or group. When a group is created, you can select the default rules created or create a custom rule. Devices are granted access to connect to their endpoint based on policies defined by the administrator.

There are four types of access rules available:

  • Category Rules (not available for IoT seats)

  • Website Rules

  • IP ACLs (not part of all products)

  • Data Speed

Click on each section to find out more.

5591142.title: Asavie APN Shared Credentials
5591142.body:
What is Asavie APN Shared Credentials

For user of the AML portal, APN shared credentials let devices authenticate with specific credentials.

All our more recent products have now moved to a more flexible way to provision APN devices that lets them authenticate using their MSISDNs/IMSIs. Therefore, this way of provisioning devices has now been removed from the UI.

Recommendation

You can still provision new devices and let them authenticate using their MSISDNs/IMSIs. Simply choose "Use mobile numbers only (ignore credentials)".

Between now and January 2022 existing devices provisioned with the option "Use shared credentials" will keep connecting without any issue to the platform.

Asavie APN Shared Credentials is being fully retired by end of January 2022. After that date, existing devices provisioned with shared credentials will keep connecting using their MSISDN/IMSI only.

You don’t have to change anything on your devices as long as the MSISDN / IMSI is unique. The APN username and password configured on the device will just be ignored.

5595245.title: Website Rules
5595245.description: Defining the allow or deny domain list in an Access Policy
5595245.body:
Admins can define rules that can either allowed or denied website domains. Whenever a device tries to access one of those websites, the platform will first match it against the configured controls and rule.

The most common way of setting a website rule is by selecting how the domain ends.

Suffix Rule - "ends with"

Choose the suffix rule, named “Ends with” to allow or deny access to “company.com” and all its subdomains such as www.company.com, “api.company.com,” or even “host1.api.company.com”, all with a straightforward website rule.

Exact Match - "is exactly."

The platform also supports the exact match by selecting “is exactly” in the UI. This is useful if you want to affect a specific, fully qualified domain name specifically, such as “portal.company.com” but not “www.company.com.”

RegEx

Finally, for more advanced use cases, admins can support website rules in the ‘for’ Regular Expressions or RegEx. This is useful to define complex rules affecting parts of a domain.

NOTEs:

  • If you want to deny all domains by default you can create a deny rule with "Regex" and ".*" as hostname

  • By default all domains not explicitly denied are allowed

Therefore, if you want to allow e.g. www.company.com and deny any other domain you can configure these rules:

5601799.title: How to create a Policy Group
5601799.description: Group devices and assign mobile access policies and access times
5601799.body:
The task of creating a Group Policy is relatively simple, as long as you know the device number of the users who the group policy will govern. Admins with permission will have the ability to create, edit, and delete a group for a given user.

More details on Policy Groups.

How to create a Policy Group

Locate the left navigation bar, and select the shield tab 🛡️, and select Policy Group. When on the Policy Group page, locate the (➕) sign to create a new Policy Group. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Group Name - Give the group a name.

  • Network - Select a network from the list of networks created.

Admins can assign Mobile Policy and Access Time Policy to a Policy Group created.

Once created, click on the group policy to make the following edits:

How to assign an Access Policy

Open the Policy Group of your choice, and under the Mobile Policies tab, you can simply add a zone data plan and assign an access policy to any zone.

By default, the default access policy is assigned to assure connectivity is not prevented. You can simply click Edit to modify it.

Add restrictions after a certain data consumption has been reached by either, each individual device, or the entire group of devices (shared data pool). This allows you to choose a different Access Policy once the restriction is reached.

More drastically, you can define data limits and add a device cut-off. Once a device reaches the cut-off, it will lose mobile data connectivity.


You can remove a zone data plan at any time, preventing connections from that zone.

More details on Access Policies.

5601866.title: How to create an Access Policy
5601866.description: An overview of the access policy creation process
5601866.body:
By defining an access policy, you can specify how access is granted to a group according to the rules that you specify.

More on Access Policies here

How to create an Access Policy

Locate the left navigation bar, and select the shield tab 🛡️, and select Access Policy. When on the Access Policy page, locate the (➕) sign to create a new Access Policy. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Access Policy Name - Create a name for this policy.

  • Rules – Select the rules you want to be enabled.

Save to complete.

How to delete an Access Policy

Access policies can be deleted if they are no longer being used or are not needed. When an access policy is deleted from a group, the default policy is applied to the group.

The Access Policy page can be found in the left navigation bar if you select the Access Policy tab.

5601878.title: How to test a Policy
5601878.description: Determine whether an access policy is blocking the DNS of a website
5601878.body:
