This means that a multinational enterprise may decide to organize their structure into a Ireland account, Australia account or USA account, if there is a need to have role-based access associated only with a country for some reason.

5616680.title: Tenant Search
5616680.description: Find tenants, accounts, users and devices across any tenant in the hierarchy
5616680.body:
You can search through the tenant hierarchy for subtenants, accounts, users, and devices using channel search.

  • The channel search will search through the entire hierarchy of subtenants available to the logged-in user. The search will include all child tenancies and sub-tenants, as well as any accounts below that.

  • Channel search will honor role-based access controls (RBAC). For example, if a user does not have permission to access a tenant, they will not search for it.

  • The channel search does not check cases sensitively and allows partial matches. For example, when searching for "me," matching records could include "Medium" or "Acme."

  • Results from channel searches are usually limited to the first 50 results matching the query. Try searching for your result more precisely if you can't find what you're looking for.

  • Combining multiple search criteria is an option. Using Name and Created date together allows you to be more specific, for example.

Tenants

The following fields can be used to search for tenants:

  • Name: the name given to the Tenant by the admin. This is the most common search criteria, however, names are not necessarily unique

  • Id: the globally unique identifier (UUID) of the Tenant

  • External Id: this is an optional field that Tenants can have to link it to some external system. It is not necessarily unique either

  • Created date, where you can specify both before and after dates

Once you search, if you click on a specific result you will go to that specific Tenant within the hierarchy and you will be able to execute the appropriate actions according to your role.

Accounts

Similarly, you can search for Accounts by these fields:

  • Name: the name given to the Account by the admin. This is the most common search criteria, however, names are not necessarily unique

  • Id: the globally unique identifier (UUID) of the Account

  • External Id: this is an optional field that Account can have to link it to some external system. It is not necessarily unique either

  • Created date, where you can specify both before and after dates

Once you search, if you click on a specific result you will go to that specific Account within the hierarchy and you will be able to log in to the account or execute the appropriate actions according to your role.

Users

Users will search for users assigned to any tenant or account. You can search for users by these fields:

  • Username: usually for users with API only access, the username given to the user at creation time

  • Email: for users with web and API access, the users' email (i.e. the one they use to log in to the portal)

  • User Id: the globally unique identifier (UUID) of the User

  • Role: the role of the User, in case you want so search for users with a specific role only

  • Access Type: to allow search for users with access both to Web and API or API access only

  • Status: to find users that are Active or still have an invitation Pending

  • Created date, where you can specify both before and after dates

Once you search, the results include whether the user is associated to an Account or a Tenant and its name. If you click on a specific result you will go to the specific Tenant/Account within the hierarchy and you will be able to execute the appropriate actions according to your role.

Devices

Devices will search for any Device provisioned in any Account underneath in the hierarchy. You can search by these fields:

  • Name: the name given to the Device by the admin. This a common search criteria, however, names are not necessarily unique

  • Number: the unique number of the Device Interface within the platform. It can be either MSISDN or IMSI depending on what has been provisioned.

  • ICCID: the ICCID of the specific Device Interface

  • Status: to find Devices that are Connected, Never connected, Disconnected, Activating, Pending or in Error status

  • Account Id: to search for Devices that belong only to one specific Account

Once you search, if you click on a specific result you will go to the specific Account the belongs to and you will be able to log in to the account or execute the appropriate actions according to your role.

5616684.title: API Access
5616684.description: Generate API credentials for accessing the platform
5616684.body:
For any user, API credentials can be created, which will allow API access to the platform. However, when generating new API credentials, the API secret can be retrieved only at creation time. It is, therefore user's responsibility to retrieve and securely store the API credentials.

API access is constrained by the type of access and role of the specific user. Therefore, the same roles and permissions are applicable and enforced for API users.

Users with portal access can manage their credentials from the profile settings area in the portal.

Note: if a user needs to access only the API and not the portal, then it can be created with web access disabled.

For more information, guides, API references, and sample code, you can check the developer site:

Go to developer site

5616685.title: Tenant Permissions
5616685.description: Permissions provide users with control over what actions can be taken on different resource types
5616685.body:
The permissions on different types of resources determine the type of actions that can be performed.

Permission

Description

iam:read

Read the list of users, names and their assigned roles

iam:write

Create/update/delete users and roles

management:read

Read tenant information and browser subtenants

management:write

Create/update/delete tenants

management-account:read

Read account details from the tenant hierarchy

management-account:write

Create/update/delete accounts from the tenant hierarchy

management-service-limits:read

Read service limits of an account

management-service-limits:write

Create/update/delete service limits of an account (*)

account:read

Login to an account and read the account details

account:write

Update account details

account-access-policies:read

Read access policies of an account

account-access-policies:write

Create/update/delete access policies of an account

account-analytics:read

Read reports of an account

account-analytics:write

Run custom filters on the reports

account-analytics-sensitive:write

Read sensitive reports of an account (including categories and websites)

account-devices:read

Read the list of devices and the device details

account-devices:write

Create/update/delete devices of an account

account-networks:write

Create/update/delete networks of an account

account-policy-groups:read

Read Policy Groups of an account

account-policy-groups:write

Create/update/delete Policy Groups of an account

account-state:write

Update initial account state (EULA acceptance)

account-web-categories:read

Read web categories reports (if available)

account-zones:read

Read the Zones of an account

account-zones:write

Create/update/delete Zones of an account

5616688.title: SSO with external identity providers
5616688.body:
In addition to local identities, the portal supports the use of enterprise or social identities. This allows sign-in with:

  • Azure AD

  • Enterprise or social identities like Google, LinkedIn, Amazon, etc

  • Identity providers that support OpenID Connect protocol

  • Identity providers that support SAML protocol (roadmap)

SSO can be configured:

  • At the channel level. I.e. all users authenticating with partner identity platform

  • For privileged users. I.e. channel admins use SSO and customers use direct authentication with the service (no SSO)

  • For a specific enterprise. I.e. an enterprise uses their own identity provider to access the service

Configuration of SSO is a managed service that will require configuration both in the platform and in the external identity provider. A custom domain is required in order to be able to customize the SSO experience.

If you want to configure Single Sign On please contact support

References (to be removed):

https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory-b2c/identity-provider-generic-openid-connect

5616710.title: Demo mode with emulated data
5616710.description: Discover how to easily see and demo portal capabilities without needing any real devices
5616710.body:
The web portal offers a “demo mode” for service accounts that can demo the portal for testing purposes or for training to familiarize users with all the features. The demo mode will be pre-populated with canned data in the portal's different tabs, similar to what would be presented in live accounts.

A service account in demo mode does not require real devices to connect or real traffic through the Network. A service account in demo mode will show data from the moment Networks are created, device Groups are made, and devices provisioned in various states, Policies being applied, and traffic and usage reports. The canned data varies depending on the product bundle configured for the account, representing different types of use cases.

NOTES:

  • Demo mode applies only to accounts, not to tenants in the hierarchy.

  • Tenant hierarchy can always support demo/test tenants (restricted by user management and role-based access controls). Therefore, there is no need for a demo mode for tenants.

  • If an account is in demo mode, no changes can be made in the portal. Data is read-only, and create, update and delete operations are all disabled.

Accounts in demo mode are created from the tenant hierarchy. A demo tenant needs to be created, and the flag “Emulated Data” needs to be enabled. Once it is allowed, all accounts created under this tenant will be in demo mode. The “Emulated Data” flag cannot be changed after the account is created.

For accounts in demo mode, users are created and assigned in the same way as any other account. Users will follow the standard registration process.

Tenants in demo mode will show an “Emulated Data” flag in the configuration tab. This flag will be hidden for any regular account.

5616730.title: User Login
5616730.description: Securely access the web application portal and change passwords as needed
5616730.body:
Users can log in to the portal from the login page by providing their username. However, for security reasons, the session will expire 24h after the log into the portal or after one hour of inactivity.

Forgot password

If a user has forgotten the password, a new password can be set after further verification of the login email address, following a process similar to the initial registration.

5616789.title: User Management for tenants
5616789.description: Collaborate with others or create API integrations
5616789.body:
User management allows access to each person required to access the platform. A user is permanently assigned to an enterprise account, and roles are also tasked with a set of permissions.

User management serves multiple purposes:

  • It allows you to collaborate with other users. Users can be assigned access, and administrators can be delegated with all access or specific tasks.

  • Access can be controlled by restricting the permission of each user and its actions that can execute.

  • Access can be segmented into different entities and areas of the hierarchy to required users only.

Users

A user represents an entity that interacts with the platform. There are two types of access for users:

  • Web and API access - These users will have access to the UI portal and can create the manage API credentials if needed. They are identified by email.

  • API access only – These users will only have programmatic access via the API with the proper credentials. A username identifies them.

A user always has a role assigned to restrict the actions that can be executed. You can read more about Users in the Hierarchy and Roles and Permission for Tenants.

How to create a tenant user?

Users with multiple accounts

A user can be created against multiple accounts with different roles. When the user logs in to the web application, they would be asked which account they wish to login into. Users can also switch between accounts at any given time.

How to switch from one account to another?

5616790.title: User Management
5616790.description: Collaborate with others or create API integrations
5616790.body:
User management allows access to each person required to access the platform. A user is permanently assigned to an enterprise account, and roles are also tasked with a set of permissions.

User management serves multiple purposes:

  • It allows you to collaborate with other users. For example, users can be assigned access, and administrators can be delegated all access or specific tasks.

  • Access can be controlled by restricting the permission of each user and its actions that can execute.

  • Access can be segmented its different entities and areas of the hierarchy to required users only.

Users

A user represents an entity that interacts with the platform. There are two types of access for users:

  • Web and API access - These users will access the UI portal and create the manage API credentials if needed. They are identified by email.

  • API access only – These users will only have programmatic access via the API with the proper credentials. A username identifies them.

A user has a status that can take two values:

  • Pending - When the user has been added to the service, a welcome invite email is sent. A user stays in pending until the invited user completes the registration process.

  • Active - After registration, a user stays in active status until it is deleted.

A user role is always assigned to restrict the actions that can be executed. You can read more on roles and permissions for users.

NOTE: A user can be created by any other admin user with the required role and permissions. The admin users' responsibility is to create the appropriate users and apply identity and access management and role-based access controls as necessary for their needs, using the platform's self-serve IAM and RBAC services.

How to create a user?

Users with multiple accounts

A user can be created against multiple accounts with different roles. When the user logs in to the web application, they will be asked which account they wish to login into. Users can also switch between accounts at any given time.

How to switch from one account to another?

5616803.title: User in the hierarchy
5616803.description: Maintain and assign users to enterprise and tenant accounts
5616803.body:
A user is assigned either to a tenant or an enterprise account in the tenant hierarchy. A user will be able to manage the tenant it’s assigned to and the child entities underneath. The permissions of the assigned role always restrict this.

For example, in this hierarchy, users can be assigned to different levels within the hierarchy.

Each would have different access roles.

More than one user can be created and assigned to each entity and level in the hierarchy, and the same behavior would apply for each of them.

Users can be managed:

  • For tenants from the user tab in the tenant hierarchy

  • For enterprise account from the user management menu in the top right corner

5617869.title: Account Settings
5617869.description: Change your account information by visiting your account page
5617869.body:
Each Account exposes its settings where the following details are visible:

  • Name of the account (used in notifications)

Furthermore, you can see important account information set at account creation time and cannot be altered, such as:

  • The unique Account ID - read-only

  • The unique External ID - read-only

  • The account time zone - read-only

  • The account Region - read-only

  • The account quota cycle day - read-only

  • The license counter - read-only

5617870.title: Account Switcher
5617870.description: Switch between accounts without re-logging in
5617870.body:
A user can be assigned to more than one tenant or enterprise account. When this happens, the user will be verified to the portal and choose which account to log into.

A user can also switch to a different tenant or enterprise account without signing out and back in again.

5617881.title: Profile Management
5617881.description: Take care of more than just your mobile devices.
5617881.body:
Account management allows you to do more than manage your mobile device. You can edit your username and password, view your Account Number, Billing Date, Time zone, and much more.

So, let's make some edits.
Navigate to the account icon located at the top far right of the page, click on Account. On the Account page, make edits if required.

My Account

  • Username - This is your portal name

  • Password - We advise password should be above eight characters, containing upper & lowercase letters, numbers, and a symbol

  • Billing date - Your monthly data allowance is reset based on your billing date.

  • Licenses - Total number of devices licensed to run the service

  • Time zone - Set the correct timezone if incorrect

  • Daylight saving time - Set if appropriate for your geographic location

  • Company name - The company name helps end-users identify that the message is legitimate.

Note:

The company name will appear on all text correspondence to mobile devices, such as when a mobile has almost reached its usage threshold.

5617885.title: How to troubleshoot a problem with the Network Connector on Windows
5617885.description: Verify and solve common configuration problems of the Network Connector on Windows servers
5617885.body:
If your Network Connector has a problem (e.g., it is offline or it cannot be started), you can follow these steps to fix it:

Network Connector installation

  • Verify that you have Administration rights on the host server to install

  • Verify the server or computer must be able to reach the internet (outbound connections to both port 80 and 443). See Network Connector Installation requirements for more details.

  • Open Services on the host server and make sure “AccessMyLan Routing Service” is set to Automatic and running and same for “IPSec Policy Agent”

  • If you have a firewall check the blocking logs

Network Connector in-life

  • If you don't need this Network Connector, disable it on the portal to remove the status error message

  • Check if the Network Connector is offline and try to "Refresh Status."

  • If it is enabled and still down, follow these steps. Once any of these is completed, restart the Network Connector Agent Manager from the Network Connector app panel in your server.
    - Ensure that the server where it is installed is turned on
    - Check that the host server has Internet access
    - Is the tunnel up? You can check that in the Network Connector status indicator and opening the Network Connector app panel in your server. If it is down, follow the tunnel troubleshooting steps explained below
    - Check the version of the server OS (Windows 2012 R2 server and newer only supported)
    - Is the host using TLS 1.0? If so, disable it (on Windows, you can follow some guide like this )
    - Check that no firewall have been added inadvertently that could be blocking outbound traffic towards TCP & UDP port 443 and 53 to any of the required domains (*.accessmylan.com)
    - On Windows, check that you have all these services running and set to start automatically
    AccessMyLan Routing Services
    IKE and AuthIP IPsec Keying Modules
    IPsec Policy Agent

  • If none of the above solves the issue retrieve the diagnostics report and submit it to us so we can help you (on Windows, you can do this from the Network Connector app panel in the "About" tab)

NOTE: You can restart the Network Connector Agent Manager

Open the Agent Manager from the Windows app panel

On the Status tab click on "Restart"

Tunnel

  • Check that the Tunnel Adapter is installed on your server (on Windows, go to Control Panel > Network & Internet > Network and Sharing Center > Change adapter settings). The Tunnel Adapter will say "Asavie Virtual Network Adapter" beneath the name.

  • Check that the Tunnel Adapter IP address also matches the Network connector's IP address in the portal. Note that the mask should always be 255.255.255.252, and the gateway should remain empty (on Windows, from the Tunnel Adapter settings right click on the Asavie adapter and go to "Properties" --> Double click on "IPv4").

Customer Network

For traffic to be able to reach the devices there should be a back route added to any gateway device on the network which traffic will traverse. The back route needs to be a persistent route to tell the gateway to the device IP range

Network Connector Diagnostics

If the above steps did not resolve the problem you can submit diagnostics information. You can do this with the following steps:

  • Open the Network Connector Manager on the agent host from the apps panel

  • Click on the About tab

  • Click “Submit Diagnostics”. This creates a password protected compressed file.

  • Contact support and send the file with a detailed description of the problem

5617888.title: How to configure a Network
5617888.description: Create an Internet or private network, as well as assign devices static IP addresses
5617888.body:
A network setup is a process of setting up how your devices will communicate with private corporate networks and/or public internet services. Several steps are involved in starting a network.

  1. Configuration of devices custom IP range and DNS

  2. Attach Gateways (internet and/or private)

  3. Configure your routing

  4. If applicable, install the Network Connector gateway.

This article explains how to complete each stage successfully.

How to create a configure a network

Configuration

Locate the left navigation bar, and select the Network tab. When on the Network page, locate the (+) sign to create a new Network. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Network Name - Give your network name.

  • Devices custom IP range - Specify the range for your devices' static IP addresses (or accept the default suggested value).
    NOTE: once the Network is created, the IP range cannot be changed or reduced. However, you can continually expand the size of the range or create a new Network. For more info, read about the IP address allocation and the rules and default values.

  • DNS server - Choose Internet resolver if you only need to resolve public internet domain names or Network Connector if you need to resolve DNS inside your private corporate network. This can be modified at any time later. For more information, read about Network DNS.

  • Region - a physical location where the Network will operate. Note: this cannot be modified once the network is created.

Save to complete. Once completed, two other tabs will activate. These tabs are called Routing Table and Gateways. You would need to create a Gateway next.


Gateways

On the Configuration UI page, select the tab Gateway to continue the configuration process. Once enabled:

  • Attach an Internet Gateway if your devices need access to the Internet

  • Create and attach a Network Connector if your devices need to communicate to your corporate network, or you need remote access towards your devices with a private static IP. For more details, you can follow this.

For the 2nd option, click on 'Create Network Connector' and enter the following:

  • Name - Give your network a name.

  • IP Address - Enter an IP Range (or accept the default suggested value)

Save and Attach. Once saved, refresh the page. Click on the three-dot under action to install the Network Connector. Click here for the installation process.

The final step is to create a Routing Table.

Routing Table

In the Configuration UI page, select the tab Routing Table to continue the configuration process. Once enabled, (+) sign to create a new Routing Table and enter the following:

  • Name - A friendly name for the router.

  • Destination - IP Address of the destination network you want to communicate to/from your devices

  • Network Mask - Which defines the range of device sin the destination network

  • Metric - utilized to prioritize routes when there are multiple options (lower value for metric takes precedence)

  • Next Hop - From the list of Gateways attached to the Network, which is the one selected to route the traffic to/from the destination

  • Type - Always static for routes added manually.


Save to complete.

For more information:

  • Learn about Networks

  • Check the installation requirements of the Network Connector.

  • Create and configure a Network Connector

5617889.title: Profile Settings
5617889.description: Manage your profile information by viewing and editing your profile
5617889.body:
Each user has access to its profile where the following areas are managed:

  • First and Last Name of the user

  • The default account if the user has access to multiple

  • The date the user profile was created - read-only

  • The date the user last logged in - ready-only

  • The email address of the user - read-only

  • Role - read-only

Also, from the profile settings, each user can see its API credentials.

The API credentials show how to follow the same principles described in the User Management section, but in the context of the specific user logged in to the portal.

5617890.title: How to troubleshoot a problem with the Network Connector on Linux
5617890.description: Verify and resolve the most common Network Connector configuration issues on Linux servers
5617890.body:
NOTE: Linux Network Connector is in the End of Life process. We recommend you to use the Windows version of the Network Connector. You ran read more here on Linux Network Connector End of Life.

Content below is provided as a reference before the Linux Network Connector is fully retired.

You can fix an offline or unstartable Network Connector by following these steps:

Network Connector service

  • If you don't need this Network Connector, disable it on the portal to remove the status error message.

  • Check in the portal if the Network Connector is offline and try to "Refresh Status."

  • If it is enabled and still down, follow these steps. Once any of these is completed, restart the Network Connector using the command below.
    - Ensure that the server where it is installed is turned on
    - Does your host server have Internet access?
    - Is the tunnel up? You can check that in the Network Connector status indicator and open your server's Network Connector app panel. If it is down, follow the tunnel troubleshooting steps explained below
    - Check the version of the server OS (Windows 2012 R2 server and newer only supported)
    - Is the host using TLS 1.0? If so, disable it (on Windows, you can follow some guide like this )
    - Check that your server’s Firewall isn’t blocking outbound traffic towards TCP & UDP port 443 and 53 to any of the required domains (*.accessmylan.com)
    - On Linux, check that service is running with the status command below

  • If none of the above solves the issue retrieve the diagnostics report and submit it to us so we can help you (on Windows, you can do this from the Network Connector app panel in the "About" tab)

Network Connector - Useful commands

  • Check the status using the command-line status tool.

> sudo asavie-vpn status

  • Restart the Network Connector

> sudo systemctl restart asavie-vpnagent

  • Check logs

> journalctl -u asavie-vpnagent

  • Start the Network Connector

> sudo systemctl start asavie-vpnagent

  • Stop the Network Connector

> sudo systemctl stop asavie-vpnagent

Network Interface

  • Check that the network interface is created and is up on your server with the following command:

> ip addr

Which will output the different network interfaces. Finally, the one created by the Network Connector will be called. tun0

3: tun0: <POINTOPOINT,MULTICAST,NOARP,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1400 qdisc fq_codel state UNKNOWN group default qlen 500
link/none
inet 192.168.255.17/30 scope global tun0
valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever

Check that the network interface is UP and that the IP address matches the Network Connector's IP address in the portal. Note that the mask should always be/30.

If there is an issue, try restarting with the command above.

IP forwarding

If you want your server to be a gateway towards other hosts in the network, you need to enable IP forwarding.

To verify whether IP forwarding is enabled:

> sudo sysctl net.ipv4.ip_forward
net.ipv4.ip_forward = 1

If value is 0 it means that it is disabled. To allow it to insert or edit this line in /etc/sysctl.conf :

net.ipv4.ip_forward = 1

5617894.title: How to create and install a Network Connector on Windows
5617894.description: Set up a Network Connector in a Windows server for E2E private connectivity
5617894.body:
The Network Connector gateway offers an easy way to connect your devices with your corporate network securely. To install a Network Connector, you can click on the Create and Attach Network Connector from the Gateways tab of your Network.

When the Network Connector is created, some information needs to be provided:

  • Friendly name used to refer to the Network Connector

  • Tunnel subnet IP address.
    - A /30 tunnel needs to be specified (different from the Network custom IP range for the devices). By default, the portal suggests a 192.168.255.0/30 range. New Network Connectors in the same Network will present by default the following adjacent /30 IP range
    - The suggested range can be modified to any valid IP range according to defined rules (outside the IP address range assigned for the devices)
    - Once created, the IP range cannot be modified. A Network Connector can always be deleted, and a new Network Connector can be made if needed.

Once created, you will have to wait a few seconds and refresh the Gateways tab until the Network Connector Install action becomes available (you will see it on hover of the three dots). After that, you'll be able to select the Operating System and Version.

The Network Connector has to be downloaded to a corporate network server installed on a server that is “always-on.” This server has access to the internet and the required resources in the customer Corporate Network. (See Network Connector Installation requirements for more details)

For the installation, the enterprise account admin has to follow these simple steps:

1. Install the software package (e.g., run the Windows installer). It is a regular MSI installer that requires Administration rights on the host server

2. Provide the activation code (that are retrieved from the portal)

3. Wait for the Network Connector to complete the enrollment automatically. This is a seamless process that usually takes at most 1-2 minutes.

5617915.title: How to create and install a Network Connector on Linux
5617915.body:
NOTE: Linux Network Connector is in the End of Life process. We recommend you to use the Windows version of the Network Connector. You ran read more here on Linux Network Connector End of Life.

Content below is provided as a reference before the Linux Network Connector is fully retired.

Installation requirements

The Network Connector has to be downloaded to a corporate network server installed on a server that is “always-on.” This server has access to the internet and the required resources in the customer Corporate Network. (See Network Connector Installation requirements for more details)

Note: 64-bit platforms only

  • Enterprise Linux 7 based distros: CentOS 7.0 and newer, Fedora 19+, RedHat Enterprise Linux 7.x and newer

  • Debian based distros: Ubuntu 16.04 and newer, Debian 8.0 (Jessie) and more recent NOTE: Ubuntu 20.04 currently not supported

Note: As a good practice, the latest OS updates should always be installed for security and compatibility reasons.

Installation process

The Network Connector gateway offers an easy way to connect your devices with your corporate network securely. To install a Network Connector, you can click on the Create and Attach Network Connector from the Gateways tab of your Network.

When the Network Connector is created, some information needs to be provided:

  • Friendly name used to refer to the Network Connector

  • Tunnel subnet IP address.
    - A /30 tunnel needs to be specified (different from the Network custom IP range for the devices). By default, the portal suggests a 192.168.255.0/30 range. New Network Connectors in the same Network will present by default the next adjacent /30 IP range
    - The suggested range can be modified to any valid IP range according to defined rules (outside the IP address range assigned for the devices)
    - Once created, the IP range cannot be modified. A Network Connector can always be deleted, and a new Network Connector can be made if needed.

Once created, you'll have to wait a few seconds and refresh the Gateways tab until the Network Connector Install action becomes available (you will see it on hover of the three dots). You'll be able to select the Operating System and Version.

For Linux servers, the Network Connector gets installed as a service (daemon), managed by the system.
Additionally, a command line utility is installed to allow for querying the status.

The enterprise account admin has to follow the following steps which depend on the Linux distribution

RPM package Installation

For CentOS, Fedora, RHEL and similar distros, the Network Connector gets installed as a service (daemon), managed by the system.
Additionally, a command line utility is installed to allow for querying the status.

1. From the command line type the following to install the package:

> sudo yum install -y asavie-vpnagent-0-xxxxxxx.x86_64.rpm

2. Start the service with systemd

> sudo systemctl start asavie-vpnagent

3. Activate the Network Connector using the activation code obtained previously

> sudo asavie-vpn enroll <activation-code>

Once activated, the Network Connector will automatically stay connected.

DEB package Installation


For Ubuntu, Debian, and similar distros

1. From the command line type the following to install the package

> sudo dpkg -i asavie-vpnagent-0-xxxxxxx.amd64.deb

2. Start the service with systemd

> sudo systemctl start asavie-vpnagent

3. Activate the Network Connector using the activation code obtained previously

> sudo asavie-vpn enroll <activation-code>

Once activated, the Network Connector should automatically stay connected.

Useful commands

  • Check the status using the command-line status tool

> sudo asavie-vpn status

  • Check logs

> journalctl -u asavie-vpnagent

  • Stopt and start the Network Connector

> sudo systemctl stop asavie-vpnagent
> sudo systemctl start asavie-vpnagent

5617916.title: Do passwords expire?
5617916.description: Do passwords expire after a certain time? Can they be reused?
5617916.body:
The context for password management

Microsoft Azure B2C is being used to manage portal user identities, leveraging authentication capabilities, and providing security and protection.

In relation to password management, the focus is on enforcing strong password policies. The policy is for a minimum of 14 characters with a combination of upper/lower case, numbers, and symbols, with commonly used, weak, or compromised passwords not being allowed.

Protection against brute-force and denial of service attacks is in place by Microsoft, using various signals to intelligently differentiate intended users from hackers and botnets. A smart account lockout capability is enforced based on the IP of the request, the password entered, and other factors, with a duration that depends on the risk identified and the number of attempts. Activity is logged to understand platform activity and diagnose issues.

Password expiration

Password expiration is not enforced and users are not notified. Password expiration is a mechanism for mitigation of the threat of password duplication/theft. The approach from Microsoft Azure B2C authentication to mitigate this risk is to focus on 2FA, enforcement of strong and complex passwords, and detection and banning of common/compromised passwords. This also follows NIST requirements, where password expiration is not considered something that improves security.

Password reuse

Following the same approach, password reuse is allowed. Microsoft Azure AD detects and blocks known weak passwords and their variations. This applies at registration time and also on any subsequent password reset. The list of banned passwords is constantly analyzed by Azure AD Identity Protection team.

References

  • https://docs.google.com/document/d/1vjWSCv2Bt_G_XBvr55zODMvjyb2u1SPoPoH5oaa4rrI/edit?usp=sharing

  • https://help.securemobi.net/en/articles/4604799-web-portal-authentication-security

  • https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory-b2c/technical-overview#protect-customer-identities

  • https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/authentication/concept-password-ban-bad

  • https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory-b2c/threat-management

  • https://pages.nist.gov/800-63-3/sp800-63b.html#sec5

  • https://pages.nist.gov/800-63-FAQ/#q-b05

5617917.title: Zones
5617917.description: Divide the world into zones to apply roaming policies to mobile devices
5617917.body:
The platform integration with mobile operators allows for granular controls also based on the device location.

Policies set on zones apply instantly upon device network authentication in the portal, providing zero data leakage outside the defined Mobile Policy.

Different roaming zones can be formed based on the Mobile Country Code (MCC) and Mobile Network Code (MNC), e.g., UK (MCC) and AT&T (MNC). In other words, each network can define a set of zones to dynamically apply different mobile policies when a device is traveling or roaming abroad.

A zone always starts a single default World zone. This is the default zone your device will use if any other zone is defined or the device's location is unknown (i.e., the Mobile Network Operator private APN does not provide MCC/MNC information).

The tenant can define a domestic zone to facilitate differentiation between domestic and roaming data (any other zone). The domestic zone can be set to Read-only, and therefore would not be modifiable.

5617930.title: How to create a Zone
5617930.description: Create roaming zones by defining countries and networks
5617930.body:
A zone can be created to apply granular roaming controls and define what country a user can connect from. Based on the Mobile Country Code (MCC) and Mobile Network Code (MNC), Roaming zones can be formed. In other words, each network can define a set of zones to dynamically apply different mobile policies when a device is traveling or roaming abroad. By default, your device will always connect to the default zone unless connecting from another created zone.

How to create a zone

Locate the left navigation bar, and select the shield tab 🛡️, and select Zone. When on the Zone page, locate the (➕) sign to create a new Zone. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Zone Name - A name is required to name the zone.

  • Network - Choose a network created for the zone.

  • Available Countries and Mobile Networks - Use the search filter to look for countries you would like assigned to the Zone created.

  • Selected Countries - Filter the MNC you want the device connected to when in that country.

Press 'Save' to lock actions reacted.

5620848.title: Policy Group
5620848.description: Group devices and assign mobile, wi-fi and access time policies
5620848.body:
To enhance your estate management within a Network, users can group devices to enforce consistent policies. Grouping can be completed per device type, organization departments, locations, solution, importance, carrier plans, and much more.

There are two types of policies:

  • Mobile Policies per Zone

  • Wi-Fi Policy

  • Access Time Policy

For each Policy Group, you can define a mobile policy per zone, and even access times.

Mobile Policy

The mobile policy consists of multiple elements:

  • Access Policy (before restriction) is enforced by default. You can attach any access policy you have created, and the associated rules will apply.

  • The device restriction threshold can be defined to allow the rule swap to different access policies in real-time. When a device has consumed the distributed quota, the access policy will change. This is handy if you wish to offer a fair usage policy but protect the rest's device quota.

  • Create a per device cut-off that limits data consumption. This will disable mobile data in real-time when the device has reached its quota.

  • Finally, a restriction threshold per group can be defined. When the entire group reaches its shared quota, the group will see a policy change, resulting in a different access policy in real-time.

More details on Access Policies.

Wi-Fi Policy

The Wi-Fi Policy consists of a single Access Policy to be applied on Wi-Fi connectivity for iOS/iPadOS and Android devices that has deployed the feature.

You probably want to create a dedicated Access Policy for Wi-Fi.

Access Time Policy

In addition, an access time policy can be defined for a user's Mobile Data per group. Decide the hours and days of the week, where mobile data is only allowed. Any other time and days will deny data access completely.

The following information can be used as a guide to aid you when applying an access time policy to a group:

  • You can allow internet connectivity from 8 am to 12 pm Monday to Saturday, then deny 1 pm to 5 pm, and finally, allow access on Sunday from 9 am to 11 am.

Whenever a device tries to connect to the internet outside of these, define hours, and the access time policy would prevent it.

More on how to create an Access Times Policy

5620870.title: How to create a Policy Group
5620870.description: Group devices and assign mobile and wi-fi access policies and access times
5620870.body:
Creating a Policy Group is relatively simple, as long as you know the device number of the users who the group policy will govern. Admins with permission will have the ability to create, edit, and delete a group for a given user.

More details on Policy Groups.

How to create a Policy Group

Locate the left navigation bar, and select the shield tab 🛡️, and select Policy Group. On the Policy Group page, locate the (➕) sign to create a new Policy Group. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Group Name - Give the group a name.

Admins can assign Mobile and Access Time Policy to a Policy Group created.

Once created, click on the group policy to make the following edits:

How to assign an Access Policy

Open the desired Policy Group and assign an access policy created to this Policy Group under the Mobile Policies tab.

By default, the default access policy is assigned to assure connectivity is not prevented.

Add restrictions after a specific data consumption has been reached by each device or the entire group of devices (shared data pool). This allows you to choose a different Access Policy once the restriction is reached.

More drastically, you can define data limits and add a device cut-off. Once a device reaches the cut-off, it will lose mobile data connectivity.


You can remove a zone data plan at any time, preventing connections from that zone.

Under the Wi-Fi Policy, you can choose a specific Access Policy to be applied on Wi-Fi for iOS/iPadOS and Android devices.

More details on Access Policies.

5620923.title: Audit logs for Accounts
5620923.description: Chronological record of admin user activity on portal and API
5620923.body:
Audit logs record the activity of admin users on each Account across the service, which can be retrieved as a chronological list of events. These records can be utilized to audit activity, identify security incidents, troubleshoot operational issues or get other insights. Audit logs, therefore, serve to increase security and accountability, prove compliance and optimize the behavior and management of the service.

Different types of actions are recorded (see below full list). Audit logs are available via portal and API and are stored for 90 days.

Each audit log record includes the date & time of the event, the action that was executed, the user, and some information about the context. So, the full list of fields of each record is:

  • Id: a globally unique id for this record (UUID format)

  • Date and time of the action (in UTC format). E.g. 2021-09-21T01:41:19.444Z

  • The action that was executed (see below for full list)

  • The User that executed the action. The user is identified by:
    - Unique user id (UUID format)
    - E-mail (web portal user) or username (API only user)
    - Role assigned to the User and the permissions associated with that Role)

  • Source IP of the user that executed the action

  • Full body of the request and response

Audit logs can be filtered by any of the fields to quickly find some specific record.

Actions

Actions

Description

access-policy.create

access-policy.delete

access-policy.update

An Access Policy has been created, deleted, or updated.

account.create

account.delete

account.update

An account has been created, deleted, or updated

auth-token.create

An API authentication token has been created. This means a user has authenticated to use the API

device.create

device.delete

device.update

A Device has been created, deleted, or updated. This includes when a Device is moved to a different Policy Group.

NOTE: There is a separate action for an Interface

device-invite.create

A Device Invite has been created. I.e. an end-user Device invite has been sent for device onboarding

NOTE: This action is not applicable for all products

gateway.create

gateway.delete

gateway.update

A Gateway has been created, deleted, or updated.

NOTE: This action is not applicable for all products

integration.create

integration.delete

integration.update

An Integration with a 3rd party service has been created, deleted, or updated.

interface.create

interface.delete

interface.update

A Device Interface has been created, deleted, or updated.

network.create

network.delete

network.update

A Network has been created, deleted, or updated.

NOTE: This action is not applicable for all products

policy-group.create

policy-group.delete

policy-group.update

A Policy Group has been created, deleted, or updated.

If you need audit logs for more than 90 days we recommend that you periodically fetch and store the logs via the public API.

5620935.title: How to invite a device via SMS or Email
5620935.description: Deployment without an MDM/UEM. Required for Wi-Fi Protect
5620935.body:
In order for your devices to start enjoying the service, they need to get configured first. Please have a look at our deployment guide to explore all the options at your disposal.

In case you cannot deploy via a third-party solution, we have created a friendly way to message your devices via SMS or Email, and help them get configured. This is also the preferred way to deploy Wi-Fi Protect.

You can invite multiple devices at once by selecting them on the devices list and applying the action "invite". Or you can invite them one by one, simply select the target devices.

If device Never Connected to the service, you will see an orange banner at the top of the device details.

Click on "Invite Device Now".

In the newly opened modal, you can choose between two deployment types.

  1. Send mobile link - for iOS/iPadOS and Samsung Knox only

    This is required for Wi-Fi Protect. This will invite the device user(s) to download the mobile app and will guide the user in setting up the service.

    Click here to see the iOS/iPadOS and Knox flows.

  2. Send setup instruction link

    This will send a link to the device user(s). If the link is opened on an iPhone or iPad, it will offer an APN profile to be installed. If this is opened on any other device type, it will provide generic setup instructions.

You can also select the invitation type between:

  • SMS - It will use the Device Phone number(s) from the device(s) CONTACT DETAILS section - This is not suitable for non SMS enabled devices such as iPads or Mifis.

  • Email - It will use the Device Email address(es) from the device(s) CONTACT DETAILS section. The email will contain a link and a QR Code that can be scanned directly by a phone or tablet.

Once you are ready, click on Confirm to send the invite(s).

If device already connected at least once to the service but has not installed the Mobile App yet, you can still invite them to the mobile app for Wi-Fi Protect. You would see a blue banner at the top of the device details.

Tips: the QR code for registration can be found on the portal under each invited device Mobile App tab. Useful if you do not wish, or cannot receive an SMS or Email. You could add a coworker as a Device Manager user that would be responsible for deploying the service. The QR codes can be used then to setup a fleet of iPads for example.

Under each device, under the Mobile App tab, you can find information regarding the setup of each device, and valuable app details.

Mobile app statuses:

  • Unregistered - the device user has never been invited to install the mobile app

  • Invited - the device user has been invited to install the app.

  • Registering - the device user has started but not completed the registration process

  • Registered - the device user has successfully completed the installation and registration of the mobile app

You can click Deregister App to reset the mobile app to Unregistered. That would remove the Wi-Fi Protect VPN if already installed. The APN configuration will remain on the device.

Wi-Fi Protect statuses:

  • Not invited - the device user will not be offered to configure the Wi-Fi Protect VPN

  • Pending - the device user has not yet configured and connected with the Wi-Fi Protect VPN

  • Complete - the device user has successfully configured and connected with the Wi-Fi Protect VPN

You can click Remove Wi-Fi Protect to automatically remove the VPN from that device. The mobile app will remain registered, so device users can still view their reports and policies.

App Details:

  • Device OS - such as Android, iOS or iPadOS

  • OS version - last known version of the Operating System of the device

  • Push Notification - enabled if the device user granted the mobile app the permission

  • Last app launch - Last time the device user has launched the mobile app

  • Last update - Last time the device user has updated its mobile app

  • App version - Latest mobile app version installed

5620961.title: Mobile Apps
5620961.description: A friendly companion with Wi-Fi Protect on iOS/iPadOS and Samsung Knox
5620961.body:
The mobile apps are companion to the service.

They are not mandatory for the service to work, but can be convenient to facilitate deployment on iOS/iPadOS and Samsung Knox in case you don't have a UEM/MDM solution in place that supports APN deployment.

See our friendly deployment guide for more information

The mobile apps are however required for Wi-Fi Protect.

The mobile apps also provide your device users with visibility into their current policies, as well as a 30-day friendly usage report.

See below the device user installation flows:

  • How to install the iOS/iPadOS Mobile App

  • How to install the Android Mobile App

5621462.title: How to setup the iOS/iPadOS Mobile App
5621462.description: See how the service can be set up by a device user with our mobile app
5621462.body:
iOS Mobile App APN Setup flow

  1. From the launch screen, press Begin Registration

  2. Under the Registration code screen

    1. Device will register automatically if you have use a link received via SMS or Email

      or

    2. you need to scan the QR code in the received email.

  3. Under the Secure your mobile internet step

    1. Press Download Profile

    2. Tap Allow when asked about permission to download a configuration profile

    3. Choose iPhone as a Device (only if an Apple Watch is paired)

    4. Tap Close once the profile is downloaded

    5. Press on Go to Settings Application

  4. Once on the device Settings App

    1. Tap on Profile Downloaded shortcut

    2. Under Install Profile, press Install and follow the steps.
      If you are asked for a passcode, enter the device screen unlock code.

  5. Go back to the Mobile App, and if asked about Wi-Fi Protect, press Install Wi-Fi Protect and select Allow. (this step will only appear if this feature was activated by your organization)

5621465.title: Audit logs for Tenants
5621465.description: Chronological record of admin user activity on portal and API
5621465.body:
Audit logs record activity, admin users on each Tenant across the service, which can be retrieved as a chronological list of events. These records can be utilized to audit activity, identify security incidents or troubleshoot operational issues.

Audit logs for Tenants work in the same way as the Audit logs for Accounts, with the main difference of the list of actions specific to what is available in the Tenant hierarchy.

You can learn more about Audit logs for Accounts here.

Actions

5621508.title: Wi-Fi Protect
5621508.description: Apply Access Policies on Wi-Fi for iOS/iPadOS and Samsung Knox device users
5621508.body:
With 4G/LTE and now 5G, your mobile devices are connecting the internet at incredible speeds. But coverage is not perfect, and they will end up having to connect via Wi-Fi hotspots on the go. Those hotspots are usually lacking the necessary level of security you would want for your employee.


Enters Wi-Fi Protect for iOS/iPadOS 13.2+ and Samsung Knox 2.0+ devices.

Click here to discover the iOS/iPadOS mobile app guide and Android Samsung Knox mobile app guide.

Wi-Fi Protect encrypts all the traffic each time a device connects to an untrusted SSID. In addition to that Man-in-the-Middle protection, your access policies are also enforced providing DNS level security and even acceptable use policies.

Click here to discover Policy Groups

You want to deactivate the VPN on your corporate networks, or on any trusted SSID? You can easily create an exception list.

Click here to discover how to create Wi-Fi Trusted Networks list

5623411.title: Wi-Fi Trusted Networks
5623411.description: SSID exception list for your corporate Wi-Fi network
5623411.body:
Wi-Fi Protect is powered by our iOS/iPadOS and Samsung Knox Mobile Apps. It creates a VPN to encrypt and secure your Wi-Fi traffic on unsecure hotspots, but for your corporate wi-fi networks this can get in the way of accessing local resources (e.g. local email server, local hosted documentation etc.), and being redundant with your company security perimeters.

You can easily declare an exception list for your Wi-Fi network SSID that will deactivate Wi-Fi Protect when employees are connecting to those trusted networks. Basically, the VPN tunnel will not be established.

SSID is short for Service Set Identifier, in other words it is the name for a Wi-Fi network.

Go to Accounts Settings, and select Mobile Apps.

Under Wi-Fi PROTECT, click on the option icon, and select Update trusted networks.

From, here simply list your Wi-Fi network SSIDs. An SSID is case sensitive, so make sure to type it exactly as it is, then select Add to add it to your list.

Once the list is complete, select Confirm.

The list is then dynamically pushed down to the iOS/iPadOS and Android apps that have Completed with Wi-Fi Protect setup.

5626903.title: Configure your Android device
5626903.description: Follow those simple steps to set up the service on Verizon 4
5626903.body:
These instructions are a guide to setting up the Verizon 4 APN on an Android device. There might be slight variations depending on the version of the Android operating system your phone has installed.

From the home screen, swipe up to access the Apps tray, then scroll to and select the Settings:

Select Networks & internet, then Mobile Data:

Finally Access Point Names, then select the + Add Icon on the top right end corner:

Now you are in Edit access point, so simply enter the following values:

  • APN: eaaa.aone.vzwentp

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: CHAP

  • APN Type: default

Finally, select the Menu icon the top right end corner, then select Save.

5645263.title: What is the Public gateway address? DRAFT
5645263.description: How to whitelist the service on the IT network
5645263.body:
IT often needs to add our service public IP ranges to an allow list within the customer network.

Traffic passing via our internet breakouts is coming from one of the ranges below:

8.30.159.0/24

207.155.193.0/24

216.50.216.0/24

173.222.0.0/21

173.222.237.0/24

173.222.239.0/24

However, the private IP address that will show assigned as the actual IP of the device itself when it connects to our service will always be within the range 10.128.0.0/9

5653317.title: How to create a Policy Group
5653317.description: Group devices and assign mobile and wi-fi access policies and access times
5653317.body:
The task of creating a Group Policy is relatively simple, as long as you know the device number of the users who the group policy will govern. Admins with permission will have the ability to create, edit, and delete a group for a given user.

More details on Policy Groups.

How to create a Policy Group

Locate the left navigation bar, and select the shield tab 🛡️, and select Policy Group. When on the Policy Group page, locate the (➕) sign to create a new Policy Group. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Group Name - Give the group a name.

  • Network - Select a network from the list of networks created.

Admins can assign Mobile Policy and Access Time Policy to a Policy Group created.

Once created, click on the group policy to make the following edits:

How to assign an Access Policy

Open the Policy Group of your choice, and under the Mobile Policies tab, you can simply add a zone data plan and assign an access policy to any zone.

By default, the default access policy is assigned to assure connectivity is not prevented. You can simply click Edit to modify it.

Add restrictions after a certain data consumption has been reached by either, each individual device, or the entire group of devices (shared data pool). This allows you to choose a different Access Policy once the restriction is reached.

More drastically, you can define data limits and add a device cut-off. Once a device reaches the cut-off, it will lose mobile data connectivity.


You can remove a zone data plan at any time, preventing connections from that zone.

Under the Wi-Fi Policy, you can choose a specific Access Policy to be applied on Wi-Fi for iOS/iPadOS and Android devices.

More details on Access Policies.

5671165.title: How to create an Access Policy
5671165.description: An overview of the access policy creation process
5671165.body:
By defining an access policy, you can specify how access is allowed to a group according to the rules that you specify.

More on Access Policies here

How to create an Access Policy

GIF TBC with IP ACLs but not Categorization

Locate the left navigation bar, and select the shield tab 🛡️, and select Access Policy. On the Access Policy page, locate the (➕) sign to create a new Access Policy. Doing so will enable you to enter the following:

  • Access Policy Name - Create a name for this policy.

  • Rules – Select the rules you want to be allowed.

Save to complete.

5708922.title: Cisco IoT Control Center Integration
5708922.body:
Cisco IoT Control Center Integration allows you to connect your account for provisioning. If Cisco IoT Control Center integration is configured, the account will retrieve and provision devices via API.

The configuration allows you to be more specific and filter which devices are provisioned and on which Network and Policy Group.

  1. Pre-requisites

  2. Server configuration

  3. Provisioning mapping

  4. Admin responsibilities

  5. Status and troubleshooting

Pre-requisites

For the Cisco IoT CC integration you need:

  • A working Cisco IoT CC account with the devices you want to provision

  • A user linked to the Cisco IoT CC account with the following requirements:
    - API access
    - API credentials generated (username and API key)
    - Role assigned with permission to "Search Devices" and "Get Device Details" (e.g. ACCOUNTADMIN role is enough. For more details check Cisco IoT Control Center documentation)

Server configuration

The server configuration is required for the integration to work:

  • Name: you can give a friendly name to the integration e.g. if you want to identify your Cisco IoT CC account

  • URL: URL for Cisco IoT Control Center API. It will be pre populated, and usually, there will be no need to modify it

  • Username: username from the Cisco IoT user created for device provisioning

  • API key: API key from the Cisco IoT CC user created for device provisioning

As part of the server configuration you can enable or disable the integration. An integration needs to be enabled to be executed. However, you might want to disable it if you want to pause it, if there is a problem or for some troubleshooting actions.

NOTE: If an integration is disabled and you enable it, the execution will not trigger immediately but after 5 minutes. This will give you time to start a manual sync if needed.

Provisioning mapping

Provisioning mapping allows you to customize the way the provisioning is run.

  • Condition: optional field. The condition will filter which devices from Cisco IoT CC will be retrieved and provisioned into this specific account. You can select from a number of predefined conditions that map to the different device attributes on Cisco IoT CC. If "All devices (no condition)" is selected then all devices will be provisioned E.g. Condition is "Customer" with value "acme" then only devices that belong to that specific customer will be provisioned and the rest will be ignored. This would allow for one Cisco IoT CC to be mapped to multiple service accounts.

  • Target Network: You can select the specific Network you want your devices to be provisioned to.
    NOTE: Make sure your Network has enough IP addresses available for the number of devices you will provision. If you need a bigger Network you can increase the IP range of the Network from the Network configuration or you can select a different Network with a larger IP range.

  • Target Policy Group: you can select the specific Policy Group you want your devices to be provisioned to.

  • Target APN: in case there are multiple APNs, you can specify the APN your devices will be using.

Admin responsibilities

It is the responsibility of the admin user creating or updating the integration to guarantee that:

  • Cisco IoT Control Center details are correct, and API Key is not compromised.

  • Provisioning mapping is configured so that each device is provisioned only to one account. I.e. the same Cisco IoT Control Center account cannot be mapped to more than one service account unless the "condition" parameter identifies which devices are provisioned in each one.

  • The target Network has enough available IPs for the devices that need to be provisioned.

Status and troubleshooting

When an integration is enabled the service will synchronize every 5 minutes with Cisco IoT CC to retrieve changed or added devices and will update or provision them accordingly. The synchronization mechanism will also retrieve the Cisco IoT CC account id and will store it as the "External Id" of the newly created account.

If the API credentials are incorrect or there is some other problem (e.g. insufficient permissions), it will raise an error.

The status panel of an integration will reflect the outcome of the last execution of the integration (success or error) and the date and time it was run.

From here you can also start a manual sync to force the provisioning of your devices.

To learn more on how to start manual sync and solve common issues read the troubleshooting article.

Finally Access Point Names, then select the + Add Icon on the top right end corner:

Now you are in Edit access point, so simply enter the following values:

  • APN: asavie.net

  • Username: data

  • Password: data

  • Authentication Type: PAP

  • APN Type: default

Finally, select the Menu icon the top right end corner, then select Save.

5717872.title: Configure your device
5717872.description: Follow those simple steps to set up the service on O2 UK
5717872.body:
You can configure any device to connect to the service. All you need to do is change the device configured Access Point Name (APN).

Mifis, USB Modems or Dongles come with Connection Manager Software that enables you to configure the Settings of the device. Other cellular gateways and IoT devices may need manual configuration via terminal. Please refer to the device's user guide for more information on how to configure the APN.

Using the Connection Manager Software, create a new APN profile and

  1. Enter APN : asavie.net

  2. If the software requires credentials, set both the username and password to data.

  3. If there is an option to apply the Authentication Type, select PAP.
    Otherwise, leave all other fields empty.

We have dedicated guides for iOS/iPadOS here and Android here.

5763413.title: Account Permissions
5763413.description: Permissions provide users with control over what actions can be taken on different resource types
5763413.body:
The permissions on different types of resources determine the type of actions that can be performed.

Permission

Description

iam:read

Read the list of users, names and their assigned roles

iam:write

Create/update/delete users and roles

account:read

Login to an account and read the account details

account:write

Update account details

account-access-policies:read

Read access policies of an account

account-access-policies:write

Create/update/delete access policies of an account

account-analytics:read

Read reports of an account

account-analytics:write

Run custom filters on the reports

account-analytics-sensitive:write

Read sensitive reports of an account (including categories and websites)

account-devices:read

Read the list of devices and the device details

account-devices:write

Create/update/delete devices of an account

account-networks:write

Create/update/delete networks of an account

account-policy-groups:read

Read Policy Groups of an account

account-policy-groups:write

Create/update/delete Policy Groups of an account

account-state:write

Update initial account state (EULA acceptance)

account-web-categories:read

Read web categories reports (if available)

account-zones:read

Read the Zones of an account

account-zones:write

Create/update/delete Zones of an account
