5781892.title: Troubleshooting problems with Cisco IoT Control Center integration
5781892.description: Check status and resolve common provisioning or synchronization issues
5781892.body:
Status

When an integration is enabled the service will synchronize every few minutes with Cisco IoT CC to retrieve changed or added devices and will update or provision them accordingly. The synchronization mechanism will also retrieve the Cisco IoT CC account id and will store it as the "External Id" of the newly created account.

If the API credentials are incorrect or there is some other problem (e.g. insufficient permissions), it will raise an error. You can learn more on the Cisco IoT Control Center integration here.

The status panel of an integration will reflect the outcome of the last execution of the integration (success or error) and the date and time it was run.

From the status panel you can also start a manual sync, with 2 options:

  • Full: this will re-provision all the devices from the Cisco IoT CC account. This is useful for example if some device has been deleted or wasn't provisioned in a previous execution due to some other problem. However, this option can take longer for large accounts.

  • Changes only: this option will retrieve an provision only the devices that have been added or updated on Cisco IoT CC from the last successful sync. This option is usually much faster and is the one that is automatically run every few minutes.

NOTE: For performance reasons the synchronization process can be triggered at most once per minute. If you have an error stating a sync wait a few moments and try again.

Troubleshooting synchronization errors

If at some point there is an error in the sync process the integration status will change to error and on every login to the account you will see an error toast highlighting that there is a problem.

When you click on review you will be sent to the Cisco IoT Control Center configuration page where you will be able to see the error status and your configuration.

To resolve any synchronization errors review the following:

  • Invalid API credentials
    Your API credentials might have been modified. To resolve this make sure the API credentials (username and API Key) in your Cisco IoT Control Center account are still valid and are the ones configured in the portal.
    Also make sure that the API credentials have the required permissions (See pre-requisites).

  • Missing devices (duplicate from a different account)
    If your devices are not being provisioned it could be because they are already in some other account.
    This can happen if the same Cisco IoT CC API credentials are utilized for two different accounts. The duplicate devices will not be provisioned. To resolve this you have to follow the steps to Move the devices to a different account.
    Once the device has been deleted we recommend to start a manual sync (Full) to make sure any missing devices are provisioned.

  • Missing devices (not enough IP addresses)
    It could be that your devices are not being provisioned because the target Network doesn't have enough IP addresses available. Check the number of IP addresses available in the Network. To fix this you can:

    1. Increase the size of the IP range or modify the provisioning mapping in the Cisco IoT CC integration to select a different target Network with enough IP addresses available (Read how to Change the IP range of a Network)

    2. Trigger a new sync or wait a few minutes for the automatic sync to be triggered.
      In this scenario it might be the case that some devices are provisioned but shown with error status in the devices list. In this case we recommend you Delete the devices before synching again.

  • Missing devices (other reasons)
    It could be that some devices were not provisioned in previous runs of the synchronization. In this case, you can start a manual sync (Full) to force provisioning of any device that could not be provisioned in the past.

5785598.title: How to setup the Android Mobile App (Knox)
5785598.description: See how the service can be set up by a device user with our mobile app
5785598.body:
Android Mobile App APN + Wi-Fi Setup flow

  1. From the launch screen, press Begin Registration and Accept & Continue the Privacy Policy

  2. Under the Registration code screen

    1. Device will register automatically if you have use a link received via SMS or Email

      or

    2. you need to scan the QR code in the received email.

  3. You are almost there! Tap Next

    1. Activate device admin app to allow the app to set you up

    2. Under KLMS Agent, accept the terms and conditions and press CONFIRM.

      This will automatically configure the APN settings on your device (Knox only).

    From here it only applies if Wi-Fi Protect feature was activated by your organization.

  4. Locations services is needed for Wi-Fi Protect trusted networks. Tap Next

    1. Tap Let's Continue

    2. Choose "Allow while using the app"

  5. Tap Next. Under Wi-Fi Protect

    1. Tap "Grant VPN Access"

    2. Under Connection request tap OK to grant the app VPN Access.

5813155.title: Vulnerability Assessment  - Log4j
5813155.body:
Since December 10, 2021 - Akamai has been aware of the recently reported Apache Log4j RCE vulnerability (CVE-2021-44228). We have been actively evaluating these vulnerabilities and assessing any impacts on our products & networks.

This statement covers Secure Internet Access Products, AccessMyLAN, Asavie Moda, IoT Connect Products and Channels:

  • No specific customer action is required in relation customer use of these services

  • The platform backend has limited use of Java as a dependency. Where it is in use, we do not directly depend on log4j.

  • We have investigated those components which indirectly are affected through use of log4j. We have assessed and are confident that no customer facing services are vulnerable.

  • Where we have identified potential vulnerabilities in our indirect vendor dependencies we have completed all priority recommended mitigations. We will continue to implement vendor recommendations for risk mitigation as they become available.

  • In addition we are performing log and tracing analysis to verify that our product services and our dependent services have not been exploited. To date no exploit signatures have been detected.

UPDATE: April 29th, 2022 - Akamai have also investigated any potential impact of the related Log4j patch vulnerabilities (CVE-2021-3100, CVE-2022-0070). We can confirm that the actions referenced above also address any risks associated with these CVEs.

UPDATE: May 5th, 2022 - Since the disclosure of the original Log4j vulnerability (CVE-2021-44228), we have been explicitly removing this component from our platform (even though it was never in direct use). In addition, Akamai have also been working with our vendors to ensure that we have mitigations and/or updates in place that will minimize the risk of any “log4shell” JNDI-type RCE exploits in future. These actions together mean we are confident we are not exposed to this class of vulnerability. This includes (but may not be limited to) CVE-2021-44228, CVE-2021-44832, CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2022-23302, CVE-2022-23307.

The explicit removal of log4j from the platform means we are not directly vulnerable to any Log4j-specific CVE’s. And of course when it comes to potential indirect impact of such vulnerabilities via 3rd-party systems we depend on, we continue to work with our vendors & partners to ensure the appropriate patches/mitigations are applied as part of our normal Vulnerability Management program at Akamai.

As ever, we continue to be diligent in the face of continuing emerging threats.

Related Akamai blog links:

https://www.akamai.com/blog/news/CVE-2021-44228-Zero-Day-Vulnerability

https://www.akamai.com/blog/news/CVE-2021-44228-Patching-is-Recommended

5827059.title: iPhone/iPad Microsoft Intune Deployment
5827059.description: Get your iOS/iPadOS devices connected via Microsoft Endpoint Manager Intune
5827059.body:
Implement the right APN setup applied to your chosen iOS/iPadOS devices by configuring a Microsoft Intune Device Profile.

Note:

Microsoft Intune does not support the configuration of the APN for Samsung Android devices. Please refer to your Microsoft Intune support contact for validation here.

How to create a new APN profile

1. First, download the appropriate iOS Configuration Profile:

  • Select a device.

  • Under interface details, select Download iOS APN Profile

  • Download it on your computer (.mobileconfig).

2. Under the Microsoft Endpoint Manager admin center, create a Microsoft Intune iOS/iPadOS devices "Configuration profile". See How to create device profile in Microsoft Intune

  • Platform: iOS/iPadOS

  • Profile type: Custom

  • Name: descriptive name for the profile such as APN Profile

3. Under the Configuration settings:

  • Custom configuration profile name: set as required

  • Configuration profile file: Import the iOS Configuration Profile downloaded in the first step. You will see the contents of the configuration profile displayed in File Contents.

4. When complete, return to the Create profile blade and select Create.

5. To deploy the configuration profile, you must assign them to the group(s) of devices that you want to apply this setting. See How to assign user and device profiles in Microsoft Intune.

  • Choose the Microsoft Intune iOS Device Profile you created in the second step and on the APN Profile Reports blade, select:

  • MANAGE

  • Assignments.

  • Choose: Include

  • Assign to: Groups you want to apply the profile.

Save.

6. Microsoft Intune will now instruct the affected devices to check in with the Intune service. When the device checks in next, the device profile will be applied, and the device should automatically be configured to use the APN. The process typically takes less than five minutes.

See Troubleshooting device profiles in Microsoft Intune for additional information.

5827093.title: iPhone/iPad VMware Workspace ONE UEM Deployment
5827093.description: Get your iOS/iPadOS devices connected via VMware WSP1 UEM (AirWatch)
5827093.body:
Implement the right APN setup to your chosen iOS/iPadOS devices by defining the appropriate Device Profile within your VMware Workspace ONE UEM portal.

A separate Device Profile is used for iOS/iPadOS devices. You can choose to modify existing Device Profiles or add new ones. Once you have your profile defined, you can apply it to individuals or groups of devices.

How to create a Profile

1. Navigate to:

  • Devices

  • Profiles & Resources

  • Profiles.

2. Select:

  • ADD

  • Add Profile.

3. Select Apple iOS and Device Profile

4. Configure the profile's General settings. These settings determine how the profile is deployed and who receives it and should be set according to your organization's needs. Some recommendations include:

  • Name: descriptive name for the policy such as APN Profile

    • Description: optional description

  • Deployment: either Managed or Manual according to your general management approach Profile

  • Scope: Production or Both if you wish to test the profile in staging

  • Assignment Type: Auto

  • Allow Removal: as per management approach. Suggest With Authorization Managed By: as per administrative approach

  • Assigned Groups: Set to the groups you wish to receive this profile. In this case, we assume that the devices that should receive the APN Profile setting are grouped via a standard or smart group

  • Exclusions: as per preferred deployment approach

  • Install only on devices inside selected areas: unchecked

  • Enable Scheduling and install only during selected periods: unchecked Removal Date: unset.

5. Choose a Cellular Payload and select Configure.

6. Under the APN list (leaving the Attach APN fields blank) configure the following APN fields:

  • APN

  • User Name

  • Password

  • Authentication Type

Leave all other areas empty

You can find the appropriate values on the portal under INTERFACE DETAILS panel of one of your device.

Select Save & Publish.

VMware Workspace ONE UEM will now apply this profile to the identified devices at the next available opportunity.

Source: https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-Workspace-ONE-UEM/2011/iOS_Platform/GUID-AWT-PROFILECELLULAR.html

5827149.title: iPhone/iPad IBM MaaS360 Deployment
5827149.description: Get your iOS/iPadOS devices connected via IBM MaaS360
5827149.body:
Implement the right APN setup to your chosen iOS/iPadOS devices by defining the appropriate MDM Policy within your IBM MaaS360 portal. A separate policy is used for iOS devices. You can choose to modify existing Policies or add new ones.

Once you have your policy defined, you can apply it to individuals or groups of devices.

How to create a Policy

To add a new Policy from within your IBM MaaS360 Portal:

  • Select Security

  • Policies

  • Select Add Policy.

Enter the following settings:

  • Name: Descriptive name for the policy. This can be APN Policy.

  • Description: Optional description

  • Type: iOS MDM

  • Start From: An existing iOS MDM policy as a template for your new policy.

Select Continue

Expand Advanced Settings and select Cellular

Select Edit

Select: Configure Advanced Settings

Configure the following APN fields:

  • APN

  • User Name

  • Password

  • Authentication Type

Leave all other areas empty

You can find the appropriate values on the portal under INTERFACE DETAILS panel of one of your device.

Save And Publish.

To Publish the policy enter:

  • Optional description to help with any future audits

  • password.

Select Continue.

Your new iOS MDM Policy can now be applied to individual or groups of devices.

5827287.title: iPhone/iPad MobileIron Cloud Deployment
5827287.description: Get your iOS/iPadOS devices connected via MobileIron Cloud Deployment
5827287.body:
Implement the right APN setup applied to your chosen iOS devices by configuring a MobileIron Cloud profile.

From within your MobileIron Cloud portal:

1. Select:

  • Configurations

  • + Add.

2. Select Mobile.

3. Enter:

  • Name: descriptive name for the profile such as APN Profile

  • Description: optional description

  • Configured APN Types : Data APNs

Configure the following APN fields:

  • APN

  • User Name

  • Password

  • Authentication Type

Leave all other areas empty

You can find the appropriate values on the portal under INTERFACE DETAILS panel of one of your device.

4. Select Next →.

5. Check Enable this configuration.

6. Choose your preferred option:

  • All Devices to deploy the configuration to all devices

  • No Devices if the configuration is to be deployed later

  • Custom to select specific device groups to which this configuration should be applied

and select the groups as required.

7. Select Done.

MobileIron Cloud will now distribute the configuration to the selected devices at the next available opportunity.

5827467.title: iPhone/iPad APN Profile Deployment
5827467.description: Get your iOS/iPadOS devices connected via APN profile installation
5827467.body:
The APN Profile deployment is a straightforward process to connect your mobile devices to the service.

Bare in mind that Wi-Fi Protect requires the installation of the mobile app client.

How to get the APN Profile installed

  • Send the profile via email or SMS to the device from your portal

    • See detail here here.

  • Get the profile from your portal account to your computer.

    • Select a device.

    • Under interface details, select Download iOS APN Profile

    • Download it, copy the URL and send it to the devices to be deployed. Or simply scan the QR code.

Once the configuration file has downloaded on the iOS/iPadOS device, select from your device:

  • Settings

  • General

  • Profiles

  • APN under DOWNLOADED PROFILE

  • Install and follow the steps.
    If a passcode is required, this is the device unlock screen code.

Once complete, your mobile device will get connected to the portal.
The CONNECTION STATUS field should now show Connected in Devices page.

Tip: If the device does not show a status of Connected:

  • Make sure the device is not connected to Wi-Fi.

  • Select the refresh icon in the top right-hand corner

5828278.title: iPhone/iPad Mobile App Deployment
5828278.description: Get your iOS/iPad devices connected on cellular and Wi-Fi
5828278.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, the Access Policies created or the configured Policy Group Zone Data Plan will not take effect.

The Mobile App is a friendly guided deployment method. It is recommended to customers not having the capacity to push APN settings via an MDM/UEM solution, or to deploy the Wi-Fi Protect solution.

The Mobile App supports all operating systems from iOS 13.2 and above.

There are multiple ways to get the Mobile App installed and registered (see here).

  • Invite device from the portal via SMS or Email

  • Invite device from the portal and scan the QR code from the UI

The Mobile App can be pushed via any UEM/MDM solution, but the device end user will need to use a Registration token received via SMS or Email to be allowed to setup the service.

Once installed, open the App and follow the simple setup steps. This article covers them in detail via Video.

Once complete, your mobile device will have connected to the service.

The device should now show Connected in Devices page.

Tip: If you want to push the app via MDM/UEM, and need to confirm the correct URL, you can find the Apple App Store URL of the mobile app on your portal, under Accounts Settings, Mobile App, in the MOBILE APP card.

5833689.title: Android Samsung Knox Mobile App Installation
5833689.description: Get your Android devices connected on cellular and Wi-Fi
5833689.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, the Access Policies created or the configured Policy Group Zone Data Plan will not take effect.

The Mobile App is a friendly guided deployment method. It is recommended to customers not having the capacity to push APN settings OTA with their carrier, via a compatible MDM/UEM solution, or to deploy the Wi-Fi Protect solution.

The Mobile App supports Android 7.1 and above and Knox v2.0 and above.

There are multiple ways to get the Mobile App installed and registered (see here).

  • Invite device from the portal via SMS or Email

  • Invite device from the portal and scan the QR code from the UI

The Mobile App can be pushed via any UEM/MDM solution, but the device end user will need to use a Registration token received via SMS or Email to be allowed to setup the service.

Once installed, open the App and follow the simple setup steps. This article covers them in detail via Video.

Once complete, your mobile device will have connected to the service.

The device should now show Connected in Devices page.

Tip: If you want to push the app via MDM/UEM, and need to confirm the correct URL, you can find the Google Play Store URL of the mobile app on your portal, under Accounts Settings, Mobile App, in the MOBILE APP card.

5836130.title: Samsung Knox VMware Workspace ONE UEM Deployment
5836130.description: Get your Android devices connected via VMware WSP1 UEM (AirWatch)
5836130.body:
Implement the right APN setup to your chosen Samsung Knox devices by defining the appropriate device profile within your VMware Workspace ONE UEM portal.

A separate profile is used for Samsung Knox devices. You can choose to modify existing device profiles or add new ones. Once you have your profile defined, you can apply it to individuals or groups of devices.

How to create a Profile

  1. Navigate to:

    1. Devices

    2. Profiles & Resources

    3. Profiles

      1. Click ADD

      2. Add Profile.

  2. Select Android.

Configure the profile’s General settings. The APN profile only displays when the OEM Settings field is toggled to Enabled and Samsung is selected from the Select OEM field. These settings determine how the profile is deployed and who receives it. They should be set according to your organizations needs. Some recommendations include:

  • Name : descriptive name for the policy such as APN Profile

  • Description : optional description

  • Deployment : either Managed or Manual according to your general management approach

  • Profile Scope : Production or Both if you wish to test the profile in staging

  • Assignment Type : Auto

  • Allow Removal : unset

  • Managed By : as per administrative approach

  • Assigned Groups : set to the groups you wish to receive this profile. In this case we assume that the devices that should receive the APN Profile setting are grouped together via a standard or smart group

  • Exclusions : as per preferred deployment approach

  • Install only on devices inside selected areas : unchecked

  • Enable Scheduling and install only during selected time periods : unchecked

  • Removal Date : unset.

3. Choose the APN payload and configure the following APN fields:

  • APN

  • Mobile Country Code (MCC)

  • Mobile Network Code (MNC)

  • User Name

  • Password

  • Authentication Type

Leave all other areas empty

You can find the appropriate values on the portal under INTERFACE DETAILS panel of one of your device.

4. Select Save & Publish.

VMware Workspace ONE UEM will now apply this profile to the identified devices at the next available opportunity.

Source: https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-Workspace-ONE-UEM/2011/Android_Platform/GUID-AWT-PROFILEAPN.html

5836176.title: Android APN Deployment
5836176.description: Get your Android devices connected via APN (non-Samsung)
5836176.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect the policies created, data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

How to enroll your device

From the home screen, tap the Menu button.

1. Navigate to:

  • Applications

  • Settings

  • Mobile Networks (tap More if necessary)

  • Access Point Names.

2. Tap the ADD button normally located at the top right of the screen.

3. Locate the following fields and configure the following APN fields:

  • APN

  • Mobile Country Code (MCC)

  • Mobile Network Code (MNC)

  • User Name

  • Password

  • Authentication Type

Leave all other areas empty

You can find the appropriate values on the portal under INTERFACE DETAILS panel of one of your device.

4. Save the new APN configuration.

Once complete, your mobile device should now show Connected when viewing the device in the Devices tab.

Tip: If the device does not show a status of Connected:

  • Make sure the device is not connected to Wi-Fi and Mobile Data is turned on.

  • Select the refresh icon in the top right-hand

5862768.title: MiFi, USB Modem or Dongle Deployment
5862768.description: Get your device connected via APN
5862768.body:
Before utilizing any of the various features available on the service, each device must first connect to the service. If the device does not connect to the platform, we cannot control or affect the policies created, data allowance set for a group plan. You will be required to change the device Access Point Name (APN).

MiFi, USB Modems or Dongles come with Connection Manager Software that enables you to configure the device's settings.

How to create a new APN

Using the Connection Manager Software, create a new APN profile for the device and configure the following APN fields:

  • APN

  • Mobile Country Code (MCC)

  • Mobile Network Code (MNC)

  • User Name

  • Password

  • Authentication Type

Leave all other areas empty

You can find the appropriate values on the portal under INTERFACE DETAILS panel of one of your device.

The device should now show as Connected when viewing the device in the Devices tab of your account.

5899000.title: Linux Network Connector End of Life
5899000.body:
What is the Linux Network Connector

The Network Connector is a software component that you can install inside your network and gives an easy way to connect your devices with your corporate network securely.

So far there were 2 major versions of the Network Connector: for Windows servers and for Linux servers. The Linux version was in Beta and is now being removed from the service.

Recommendation

Between now and October 2022 you can keep using any Linux Network Connector that is already installed and connected to your Network. For any new one you will only have the Windows version available.

Support for Linux Network Connector will be retired by end of October 2022. You will have to replace it before that date.

The recommended replacement is to use the Windows version of the Network Connector. For this you can follow these steps:

  1. Login you your portal account

  2. Create a new Network Connector (Windows) following the regular procedure

  3. Install the Network Connector in a Windows server inside your corporate Network

  4. Update the routing table to use the newly created Network Connector

  5. Once the new Network Connector is online you can uninstall your existing Linux Network Connector and delete it from the portal

NOTE: You can read here a more detailed article on how to install a Windows Network Connector

If you don’t have any Linux Network Connector installed you don’t have to do anything.

Please contact us for support or if you have any question.

6007411.title: Tenant configuration
6007411.body:
Each tenant allows a separate configuration to control how the tenant and the direct accounts underneath will behave.

The tenant configuration comprises the following properties:

  • Default time zone
    The time zone to use by default for child accounts

  • Default quota cycle
    The day and time quota controls and monthly reports are reset for child accounts

  • Available regions
    The available regions specifies in which regions child accounts can be created.

  • Available subscription / product bundle SKUs
    It specifies the products that are available for child accounts

  • Available APNs
    It specifies the list of APNs that are available for child accounts

  • Default APNs
    The default APNs of the account. A default APN must be provided for each of the regions available in the account

  • APN settings
    A set of APN configurations. Each APN available for this tenant must have APN settings specified

  • Support details (email, phone, URL, support hours)
    The support contact details that will be shown to account admin users

  • Required integrations
    A list of the 3rd party integration details that need to be provided when creating an account

  • Emulated data
    A flag indicating if child accounts use demo emulated data

When an account is created under a tenant only the available regions and product bundles will be available for selection. Besides, by default it will use the time zone and quota cycle day specified on the tenant configuration unless something different is provided at account creation time.

When a tenant is created it inherits the configuration from the parent tenant. The configuration of a tenant can be updated at any time after creation.

However, keep in mind that if the configuration of a tenant is updated it will not affect to the existing child tenants and accounts.

6040151.title: Failed connections
6040151.description: Device failed connection events due to policies being enforced, configuration issues or other problems.
6040151.body:
When a device connects it is authorized and authenticated by the service enforcing the specific enterprise Access Policies specified in the Policy Group the device belongs to. In this context, a device might fail to connect due to some Access Policy or other type of control being enforced. Beside, a device might also fail to connect due to some misconfiguration or some other problem.

This page lists the list of reasons why a device could fail to connect and what to do in each case:

List of causes:

  • Access Times

  • Already authenticated

  • Cert PIN missing

  • Cert PIN mismatch

  • Cut-off reached

  • Interface disabled

  • Duplicate session

  • Invalid IMEI

  • Mobile network blocked

  • No IMEI

  • Roaming blocked

NOTE: For any other cause not in this list please contact support

Access times

The device is trying to connect out of the access times configured in the Access Times Policy

Event Id: OutsideLoginHours

Action

On the portal, under your device Policy Group, verity the access times rules configured under the Access Times Policy. You can either modify the access times or wait and connect the device during some permitted period.

Already authenticated

The device is trying to establish two simultaneous cellular connections.

When a device connects to the cellular network it has to complete two phases:

  1. Authorize

  2. Accounting Start.

This specific error occurs if there is already a successful Authorize for a device, and if before the service gets an Accounting Start, another Authorize request is received for the same device.

Event id: AlreadyLoggedIn

Action

Wait ~30 seconds and try to connect again. After this period the first Authorize expires so any new connection will be accepted.

If the error affects the device connectivity and keeps happening please contact support.

Cert PIN missing

The device is trying to connect over VPN (e.g. over Wi-Fi) without a client certificate id. This could be a security issue, but it is often due to some misconfiguration.

Event id: PinsMissing

Action

De-register and re-invite the device to the service mobile app, in order to repeat the device onboarding procedure.

Cert PIN mismatch

The device is trying to connect over VPN (e.g. over Wi-Fi) with an invalid client certificate id. This could be a security issue, but it is often due to some misconfiguration.

Event id: PinMismatch

Action

De-register and re-invite the device to the service mobile app, in order to repeat the device onboarding procedure.

Cut-off reached

The device has reached configured cut-off data limit.

Event id: DataQuotaExceeded

Action

Go to the Policy Group the device belongs to, and under Mobile Policies increase the zone device cut-off limit. Then try to connect the device again. This will affect all device in the Policy Group.

Besides, data usage is re-set every quota cycle so the device will be able to connect in the following one. The quota cycle can be configured to reset monthly or daily. You can verify this in the Policy Group configuration in the portal.

Interface disabled

The device is trying to connect while disabled in the portal.

Event id: UserNotEnabled

Action

Enabled the Device Interface from the Device details page in the portal and try to connect again.

Duplicate session

A duplicate accounting session id received when the device is trying to connect.

Event id: DupAcctSessionId

Action

Wait a few seconds and try again. If the device still cannot connect contact support.

Invalid IMEI

The SIM card has been moved to a different device while the IMEI locking capability is enabled. Note that this could also happen if the SIM card has been moved to a different SIM slot in the same device.

Event id: InvalidIMEI

Action

This could mean that your device has been stolen. Verify that the SIM is in the appropriate. Once confirmed you can disable and enable again IMEI lock for the specific device interface and try to connect again.

Mobile network blocked

The device is trying to connect from the mobile network that is not allowed.

Event id: MobileNetworkBlocked

Action

Verify the configuration of Zones for the Policy Group the device belongs to and that each Zone has assigned a valid Access Policy. If the device still cannot connect contact support.

No IMEI

The device is trying to connect with IMEI lock enabled and the mobile network is not sending the device's IMEI

Event id: NoIMEI

Action

Try again. Sometimes the mobile network will not send the IMEI due to some other limitation, specially in roaming scenarios. In this case if you need to connect disable IMEI lock capability for the specific device interface.

Roaming blocked

The device is trying to connect from a Zone that is not allowed.

Event id: RoamingNotAllowed

Action

Verify that the device is connecting from the right location.

Once confirmed verify the configuration of Zones for the Policy Group the device belongs to and that each Zone has assigned a valid Access Policy.

6093885.title: Failed connections - Platform configuration
6093885.description: Device failed connection events due to platform configuration issues
6093885.body:
When a device connects it is authorized and authenticated by the service enforcing the specific enterprise Access Policies specified in the Policy Group the device belongs to. In this context, a device might fail to connect due to some Access Policy or other type of control being enforced. Beside, a device might also fail to connect due to some misconfiguration or some other problem.

This page lists the causes due to platform configuration issues.

List of causes:

  • Access network

  • Access service

  • No APN access

  • No group

  • No quota

  • No restricted policy

  • No VPN NAS

  • No VPN policy

  • No VPN zone

  • No zone

  • Maintenance mode

  • Network disabled

  • SIM number not present

For a list of causes related to policies, customer configurations or other issues visit this article : Failed Connections

Config - Access Network

Platform configuration problem. Invalid Access Network.

Event id: InvalidCAN

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

Config - Access Service

Platform configuration problem. Invalid Access Service.

Event id: InvalidCAS

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

Config - No APN access

Platform configuration problem. Access over private APN not enabled for device.

Event id: NotEnabledOnCAS

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

Config - No group

Platform configuration problem. Access group needs to be provisioned / configured for cellular connection.

Event id: NoAccessGroupFound

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

Config - No Quota

Platform configuration problem. Access group doesn't have quota defined or quota is invalid.

Event id: NoQuotaDefined

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

Config - No Restricted Policy

Platform configuration problem. Restriction level is defined but there is no restricted policy for the access group zone.

Event id: NoRestrictionDefined

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

Config - No VPN NAS

Platform configuration problem. Device is not allowed to connect to the specified NAS.

Event id: IOTDeviceNotAllowedOnNAS

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

Config - No VPN policy

Platform configuration problem. Policy not found for access group VPN zone.

Event id: NoAccessGroupOTIZonePolicyFound

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

Config - No VPN zone

Platform configuration problem. No zone provisioned for country code on VPN connection and no default defined.

Event id: NoOTIZoneFound

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

Config - No Zone

Platform configuration problem. No zone provisioned for MCC/MNC on cellular connection and no default defined.

Event id: NoAPNZoneFound

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

Maintenance mode

Configuration problem. The specific Network in the account is in maintenance mode.

Event id: MaintModeActive

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

Network disabled

Configuration problem. The specific Network in the account is not enabled.

Event id: AccNotEnabled

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

SIM number not present

Authentication problem. SIM number not present.

Event id: CliNotAvailable

Action

Contact support with reference to event id.

6093905.title: Events
6093905.description: Retrieve the activity of the devices and other entities in your account.
6093905.body:
Events show the activity of the devices in your account. You can use it to monitor status, verify policies are enforced, detect security issues, anomalies or configuration issues.

All events share 3 attributes:

  • Date: the date and time the event was generated

  • Type: the type of event. Initially device failed connection events are shown. Other types of events will be added in the future

  • Description: a human readable description of the event that shows the most relevant information

Besides, events can have other attributes, that will depend on the specific event. From the portal you can see use the Customize columns dropdown above the table to show them.

Each event has a number of actions, that will point you to the relevant related entities that you will need to learn more about the event, investigate any underlying potential issue or update the configuration to resolve it.

Events are shown in chronological order and retained for 48h. Dates shown in the portal are adjusted to your browser time zone.

6099490.title: Apple iCloud Private Relay
6099490.description: How does it work and why we block it - (Public link)
6099490.body:
Privacy is central to Apple’s value proposition, and on June 7th, 2021, they announced iCloud Private Relay.

iCloud Private Relay is a new service that prevents networks & servers from monitoring user activity across the internet. It is part of every iCloud+ subscription, offered as an opt-in beta option, and supported in iOS/iPadOS 15 from September 20th 2021 and macOS 12 later this fall.

How does iCloud Private Relay work?

iCloud Private Relay protects:

  • all web browsing in Safari,

  • all DNS name resolution queries,

  • a small subset of app traffic (e.g., unencrypted DNS queries made by apps)

For completeness, several other categories of traffic are not affected by iCloud Private Relay:

  • Connections apps makeover local networks or to private domain names

  • Apps that provide a network extension to add VPN or app-proxying capabilities and app traffic that uses the extensions

  • Traffic that uses a proxy is also exempt

To protect DNS name resolution, iCloud Private Relay uses Oblivious DNS-over-HTTPS (ODoH)

  • Users of iCloud Private Relay will bypass DNS resolvers assigned by their local network

Why and how do we disable iCloud Private Relay?

For security solutions like ours with valid monitoring use cases, Apple has documented a way to disable the service.

To disable Apple’s Private Relay, two hostnames need to be blocked with NXDOMAIN by the DNS resolver.

  • mask.icloud.com

  • mask-h2.icloud.com

Those blocks are applied to our DNS resolvers from now on, and the iCloud Private Relay cannot operate on our network. There is no opt-out option at the moment.

Source: https://developer.apple.com/support/prepare-your-network-for-icloud-private-relay/ See section “Allow for network traffic audits”

What is the user experience on iOS/iPadOS devices?

From iOS/iPadOS 15, users with iCloud+ paid subscription service have the ability to enable iCloud Private Relay beta.

We recommend that the feature stays disabled to enjoy our service.

If the feature is enabled, once the device has been configured to connect to our service, the user will immediately see a system prompt informing that the mobile data service does not support iCloud Private Relay. The user will then select "Use Without Private Relay" to keep using the mobile data. This removes Apple Private Relay for the internet provider, and our service will now apply normally.

6099599.title: Start a tour on demand  🎉
6099599.description: Learn how to better self serve with tours, making it much easier to understand the service.
6099599.body:
Get acquainted with our latest feature release called "Start Tour." This feature allows you to start a tour on demand.

We have taken self-serve to the next level to help you navigate around the portal easier.

You can snooze a tour and take it at a time convenient to you.

Each section has a "Start Tour" icon on the top right, allowing you to start a tour whenever you feel like it.

As of March 30th, 2022, this feature has been released.

6104031.title: Searching for articles couldn't be easier  🔎

6104031.body:
We have revamped the Article Search and improved our knowledge base.

With our recent enhancements to Articles, you can now view all articles associated with a given tab in a collection.

This allows an easy reading experience and enables you to scan through articles regarding each portal section you are viewing.

Each section has a "Read Article" icon on the top right, allowing you to view articles whenever you feel like it quickly.

As of December 8th, 2021, this feature has been released.

6211295.title: Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune) Integration
6211295.description: Prerequisite, setup and features of the integration
6211295.body:
Table of content

  1. Introduction

  2. Prerequisite

  3. Integration Setup

    1. Create Intune Integration

    2. Device Mappings

    3. Group Mapping

    4. iOS/iPadOS App distribution with auto-registration

Introduction

This integration allows you to connect your account to an Intune account and sync Intune device attributes with your device platform attributes. It also allows you to define groups mapping to automatically move devices between policy groups based on their device mapping.

Prerequisite

Here is a summary of steps on how to prepare the client credentials. Please review Microsoft official guides if you come across any difficulty from below steps.

  1. Sign in to the Microsoft Endpoint Manager admin center using administrative credentials.

  2. Select All services > M365 Azure Active Directory > Azure Active Directory > App registrations.

  3. Choose New registration to create a new application.

  4. In the Register an application pane, specify the following:

    • A Name for the application.

    • The Supported account types. This value can be default.

    • A Redirect URI value. This value is an option.

  5. After registered, from the application pane:

    • Note the Application (client) ID value.

    • Note the Directory (tenant) ID value.

  6. From the API permissions pane, choose Add a permission > Microsoft APIs > Microsoft Graph. Then, select the type of Application permissions we require:

    • DeviceManagementManagedDevices.Read.All

    • DeviceManagementConfiguration.Read.All

    • DeviceManagementApps.Read.All

    • Group.Read.All

    • Directory.Read.All

    • User.Read.All

  7. From the same panel, select Grant admin consent for your organization to apply the permissions

  8. From the Certificates & secrets pane, choose Client secrets > New client secret.

    • Note the Secret value.

Integration setup

Create Intune Integration

To create an Intune integration, simply navigate to Integrations, then click on (+) Create Integration.

Choose Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune) under UEM integrations. Then provide the values gathered in the Prerequisite step:

  • Application ID

  • Directory ID

  • Client Secret

SCREENSHOT TBA

After this is created, you have connected your account to Intune, your devices will start syncing from Intune and their details will start getting updated, to see what details are being pulled from Intune, you can check the default device mappings of this integration.

Device Mappings

Device mappings define what fields from an intune device you want to persist your platform device. Here are the default device mappings for this integration:

External Device Attribute

Platform Device Attribute

deviceName

name

managedDeviceName

name

operatingSystem

os

managedDeviceOwnerType

metadata

serialNumber

metadata

osVersion

metadata

model

metadata

id

metadata

azureADDeviceId

metadata

Here we have defined 2 mappings with the same `platform_device_attribute`, this means that the system will get the `deviceName`, if it is not available it will use the `managedDeviceName` field and set the `name` of the Platform device to its value.

Metadata attributes are additional information enriching the device platform information.

Group Mappings

Group mappings will update the Policy Group of your Devices based on their Intune groups.

Create a Group mapping list

To create group mappings, we need to know what Intune Groups you are going to map to your Policy Groups.

Under GROUP MAPPING, simply select the (+) icon and search for an Intune group using its name or ID. Select the Intune group you want to map and select Add.

INSERT SCREENSHOT

A new entry will appear under the GROUP MAPPING section. Under TARGET POLICY GROUP, select the desired existing Policy Group you want to map to this Intune group.

INSERT SCREENSHOT

You can add multiple entries and sort them by priority (top to bottom), or delete any entry you do not need anymore. When you are done, do not forget to save your group mapping list.

How does Group mapping works

Once you have defined a group mapping list, if you have a device in "Intune group 1" and the device exits in our platform, it will be assigned to the Default Group, if you move that device in Intune to "Intune group 2", that device will be moved to "My other policy group".

If the device is in both groups in Intune, then the mapping higher in the list will determine where the device will go, in this case it will go to "Default Group"

INSERT SCREENSHOT

iOS/iPadOS App distribution with auto-registration

This deployment option for iOS/iPadOS allows to shorten the user on-boarding process by enabling auto-registration.

Note: For the deployment of the APN to iOS/iPadOS devices, see this guide here.

Add the iOS/iPadOS store app

In your Intune Portal.

Go to Client Apps>Apps

  1. Click on + Add

    1. Under App Type, select iOS/iPadOS store app

    2. Click on Select

  2. Under App information, click on the link Search the App Store

    1. Search for the service mobile app (tip: you can find in your account under Account Settings>Mobile Apps), and select it

    2. Click on Select

    3. Adjust the App information and then click on Next

Under Assignments, do not assign this app yet, click on Next

Under Review + create, select Create

Add and Assign the iOS/iPadOS app configuration

Go to Client Apps>App Configuration policies

Click on + Add and Managed devices

Under Basics

  1. Give a Name

  2. Select the iOS/iPadOS platform

  3. Click on Select App and add the service iOS/iPadOS store app, click OK

  4. Click on Next

Under Settings, choose in the drop-down Use Configuration designer

  1. Configuration key is device_id

  2. Value Type is String

  3. Configuration Value is #{{deviceid}}

  4. Click on Next

Under Assignments, define the groups you want to select, then click on Next

Under Review + create, click on Create

Assign the iOS/iPadOS store app

Go back to Client Apps>Apps

Select the service iOS store app from the list

Under Properties, select Edit next to Assignments

You can now assign the app to the groups you want the app distributed to.

Under Review + Save, then Save

6252351.title: Deployment Overview
6252351.description: Summary of the various deployment options
6252351.body:
To utilize the service, you need to connect your devices. This can be achieved by changing the Access Point Name (APN) on the device.

There are different Deployment options available depending on the device type you wish to connect.

Deployment Guides

There are various configuration guides available ranging from for iOS/iPadOS, Samsung Knox, Android, USB Modem or Dongle and Windows laptops. Devices can be deployed for:

  • iOS/iPadOS using our user-friendly iOS/iPadOS Mobile App , sharing a configuration profile or via UEM deployment for devices under MDM (see below)

  • Samsung Knox using our user-friendly Android Mobile App, or via UEM deployment for device under MDM (see below)

  • USB modems and dongles, stock Android devices and other devices via on-device configuration

  • Windows 10 and 11 laptops using Windows Configuration Designer

UEM Deployment Guides

Deploying through your chosen UEM allows you to apply the correct APN setup without requiring any user interaction, assuming the device in question is already enrolled in your MDM.

This approach will work for iOS/iPadOS and Samsung Knox device types supported by your UEM vendor.

Current known device support is as follows (we recommend you refer to your MDM vendor’s documentation for full accuracy here):

MDM Vendor

iOS/iPadOS

Android (Samsung Knox)

Android (Other)

Microsoft Intune

Yes

Not available

Not available

IBM MaaS360

Yes

Yes

Not available

VMware Workspace ONE UEM (AirWatch)

Yes

Yes

Not available

MobileIron Cloud

Yes

Not available

Not available

6313604.title: How to set daily quota
6313604.body:
To help manage a device's mobile data allowance, set up a daily quota allowance. A device can have a set daily allowance, allowing for better management. Notifications are sent to you by email when a device has reached a limit set. You can decide to keep the current data or increase the data usage until the end of the day.

More details on Policy Groups.

How to set a daily quota

Locate the left navigation bar, and select the shield tab 🛡️, and select Policy Group. On the Policy Group page, tap on the policy group created. Doing so will enable you to make edits. On the home page of the selected Policy Group, tap on Mobile Policy.

Now tap on Deny Mobile Data in This Zone, tap to confirm.

Once deleted, tap on the (➕) Assign an Access Policy for this Zone to set a daily quota. Tap on toggle next to Device cut off to switch from Monthly to Daily. Tap on confirm edits.

6337091.title: Removal notice on Web UIs (WWW, FEPs) support for TLS 1.0 and 1.1
6337091.body:
On June 30th 2022, support for browsers connecting with TLS 1.0 or 1.1 to web endpoints will be deprecated.

What does this EOL mean to you?

Better security. TLS 1.2 is more secure than the previous cryptographic protocols such as TLS 1.0, and TLS 1.1.

Akamai strongly recommend customers to stop supporting the SSL and older TLS protocols for compliance and security reasons. An extended End of Life support has slowly removed TLS 1.0 & 1.1 support on all our services. With practically all customers web browsers now utilizing TLS 1.2 protocols, we are removing the final parts of TLS 1.0 & 1.1 legacy support from our Asavie supported service endpoints.

Once the support for TLS 1.0 and 1.1 is deactivated, the web UIs will only be accessible from browsers supporting at TLS version 1.2 and above.

We do not expect any impact from this change as all browsers accessing these application UIs already support TLS version 1.2 or beyond as this is the recommended standard.

References:

https://community.akamai.com/customers/s/article/Is-Your-Akamaized-Property-Ready-for-the-new-TLS-Security-Requirements-for-PCI-Compliance?language=en_US

https://community.akamai.com/customers/s/article/SSL-TLS-Cipher-Profiles-for-Akamai-Secure-CDNrxdxm?language=en_US

https://community.akamai.com/customers/s/article/SSL-TLS-Protocols-and-Ciphers-for-origin-connections?language=en_US

What alternatives capability can be used?

We recommend, if not already, that you start using a web browser supporting TLS 1.2 and above to keep accessing the application UI. Main web browsers (Chrome, Safari, Edge, Firefox) already support TLS 1.2 in their recent versions.

6337132.title: Removal notice on Windows Agent support for TLS 1.0 and 1.1
6337132.body:
On December 31st 2022, support for Windows Agent (vCPE) connecting with TLS 1.0 or 1.1 to web endpoints will be deprecated.

What does this EOL mean to you?

Better security. TLS 1.2 is more secure than the previous cryptographic protocols such as TLS 1.0, and TLS 1.1.

An extended End of Life support has slowly removed TLS 1.0 & 1.1 support on all our services for compliance and security reasons. With practically all customers Windows Agent now running on machines utilizing TLS 1.2 protocols, we are removing the final parts of TLS 1.0 & 1.1 legacy support from our Asavie supported service endpoints.

Once the support for TLS 1.0 and 1.1 is deactivated, only Windows agents (vCPE) running on Windows OS configured to support TLS 1.2 will keep functioning. We recommend you adjust the Windows config to support TLS 1.2, or that you upgrade the OS to a version that has it by default. Please find relevant Windows documentation here.

We do not expect any impact from this change as long as you are using a Windows OS version configured to use TLS 1.2. All officially supported versions of Windows should already have TLS 1.2 support by default. If that is not the case, we recommend you make the necessary adjustments as soon as possible.

References:

https://community.akamai.com/customers/s/article/Is-Your-Akamaized-Property-Ready-for-the-new-TLS-Security-Requirements-for-PCI-Compliance?language=en_US

https://community.akamai.com/customers/s/article/SSL-TLS-Cipher-Profiles-for-Akamai-Secure-CDNrxdxm?language=en_US

https://community.akamai.com/customers/s/article/SSL-TLS-Protocols-and-Ciphers-for-origin-connections?language=en_US

What alternatives capability can be used?

We recommend, if not already, that you start using a Windows OS configured to use TLS 1.2 by default, to keep your Windows Agent (vCPE) running. All officially supported versions of Windows should already have TLS 1.2 support by default.
